How has your family influenced you?

I’ve got a wry sense of humor, and sometimes apparently inappropriate. Both my parents are hilarious, and you always know what’s in store for you during a meal – my dad’s remarkably funny jokes.

【P1】交通类：船

Do you like boats?

Not really, my negligible experiences of being on a boat could be better. Basically, I suffered from seasickness big time.

Would you like to have your own boat?

Nah, I think I’ll pass, even if I turned a millionaire tomorrow. Being on a boat is likepurgatory for me.

Is boat trip popular in your country?

I don’t think so. Boat trips are extravagant for most people, well, maybe not for the wealthy community. I’d rather rent a boat and have a good time with my friends on one of those artificial lakes in a nearby park.

【P1】基本信息：学习与工作

Do you study or work?

Currently I’m a university student majoring in… and I’m in my junior year now.

I work as an actuary in an insurance company and my job is to tell my company how much they should charge people for insurance based on risks, mortality, accident, sickness, disability, and retirement rates, etc.

Why did you choose to do that type of work?

For your information, I majored in mathematics back in university but my advisors told me that theoretical things are not for me, and that was why I turned to actuarial industry after graduation, thinking that only a certain amount of math skills were needed.

Do you like your job?

Not really. Turned out this job is so much more tedious than I previously thought. I had to deal with tons of paperwork and stuff. I’m considering quitting but not ready for it.

Do you miss being a student?

I miss being a student, like, big time. Do you still remember the good old days in school where you spent carefree days studying and hanging with your mates? How I wish I could be back there.

What’s your major? What subjects are you studying?

I’m a law major and I need to learn how a legislature, an executive and a judiciary work separately and together, along with our constitution, civil law, criminal law and so forth.

Why did you choose to study that subject?

I had been obsessed with Boston Legal, an American legal comedy-drama television series before I entered college. And I really long to be a lawyer for years to come.

Are there many students in China majoring in that?

Let’s be modest here and I would say there are half of a million law majors, give or take. You can basically see a law school in every single university or college.

Are you looking forward to working?

Absolutely. More than anything else. I want to earn some good money to support my family and fulfill my ambition.

【P2】物件类：重要水域

Describe an important river/lake (/area of water) in your country

  • What this river is called
  • Where it is
  • How you knew about it
  • How long/big it is
  • And explain why it is important
  1. 本题的A是a river/lake (water)，描述水域；B是important, in your country，限制属性和地点。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 描述此水域的基本信息
    • 详述此水域重要的原因
    • 对此水域的感受，升华
  3. 时态建议：通篇一般现在时，讲自己第一次见到或知道的经历用过去时
  4. 其他：雅思口语P2关于水域的问题可以说是自成体系了，出题者显然特别喜欢江河湖海，几乎每个题季都有一道以上跟水有关的题目。这是典型说明文，应当把某个水域的特征详细进行介绍，落脚点是“重要”。注意，重要，不是美丽。

This is a no-brainer for me. I’d like to describe one of the most beautiful and fairly important lakes in China, Qinghai Lake, which is the excellent picturesque scene of blue lake, golden rape blossom, and mountains in the distance. It’s also the largest inland saltwater in China, a paradise for avid bird-watchers and of paramount importance in terms of biodiversity.

Qinghai Lake is located in northeast Qinghai Tibet Plateau and northwest Xining City, Qinghai Province, surrounded by four high mountains. The best travel season falls on the autumn months when the vast prairie wear a golden coat of rape flowers, refreshing fragrance drifts with the wind, dotted herdsman tents look like stars in the sky, herds moves as clouds, charming sunrise and sunset consisting of a poetic painting.

I call it important because of one tiny island called Bird Island, a bird refuge which plays a vital part in providing a transitory home to countless numbers of migratory birds which arrive there from Southern China and Southeast Asia. Such birds include the spotted head wild goose, brown head wild goose, fish gull, red sheldrake, cormorants, and black-neck crane. There are more than 100,000 birds that inhabit this small island to nest, lay eggs, hatch, and raise their young. For the avid bird-watcher, Bird Island is certainly a paradise, but even the inexperienced bird watcher cannot help being impressed with thesheer numbers of creatures that gather here. Their constant buzz of activity and theecho of their mating calls, bickering over territory, and shrill cries of their hungry offspring connect the listener with the wonders of the natural world.

This very lake is the perfect epitome of biodiversity, for the local government spares no effort to preserve indigenous and exotic birds, antelopes and fishes. Various campaigns have been conducted to disseminate the idea.

Unfortunately, the Bird Island has been closed indefinitely in order to better protect the fragile ecological system there.

第18天【P2】物件类：城市发展

Describe a recent development (such as new roads, shopping centers) in your city

  • What this development is
  • How it could help people
  • What benefits it could bring
  • Where you saw it
  • And explain whether it changes your life or not
  • and explain how you felt about it)

  1. 本题的A是a development，描述城市一处发展成果；B是recent, in your city, 限制时间地点。

  2. 提纲建议：

    • 描述此发展成果的基本信息（时间地点、功能性等）
    • 详述此发展成果对你及当地人的好处
    • 对此发展成果的评价和感受

  3. 时态建议：介绍这个发展成果用一般现在时，描述建成过程或经历用过去时。

  4. 其他：development有一词多义，就题目括号中给的例子来看，指的是建筑和设施类的具体发展成果，那么在选材时要尽量避免比较虚的发展成果（比如空气变好了、人们生活水平提高了等）。

My city is a rather tiny one, and it surely is a surprise that new development is occurring at a breakneck pace there, and while much of it is happening on the individual level, anot-inconsequential portion of it is also lumped into megaprojects rising all over the 4 districts. Fortunately, one of them has finished recently. The riverside center. Well, part of it.

There are several parts to the West River District megaproject known as Riverside Center, an eight-acre development rising on an old industrial lot and exposed railyard between two main streets on the west part waterfront. The project, conceived by some unknown developers, has been pocked by uncertainty from the get-go, but one of the planned 3 buildings has completed – the biggest shopping mall in town, with the other two under construction which are supposed to be the new administrative center and a skyscraper office building.

This 55-story shopping mall could be the tallest of its kind in the southwest of China. If it wasn’t for the top floor, this mall would be no different than any of its counterpart.

Half area of the top floor was mapped out a municipal museum with the log of city development as well as antiques discovered in the city. Which hasn’t opened yet. And the other half, a perfect observation place where one gets to enjoy a panorama of the whole city.

And yet the most exciting part is that residents can now enjoy strolling in the newly-built park that connects the three buildings. It feels kinda surreal to be in the center with three eerily tall buildings forming a triangle. But it’s also an amazing garden for thousand employees working in the three edifice to wind down.

【P2】物件类：科技产品

Describe a piece of technology you like using except computers

  • What this technology is
  • When it was invented
  • How you learned about it
  • How often you use it
  • And explain what difference it brings to your life
  1. 本题的A是a technology，描述一项科技产品；B是you like using except computers, 限制你对这项产品的情感倾向。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 简述此科技产品的基本信息、发明时间、基本使用方法等
    • 详述你使用此产品的方式、心得或故事
    • 发表评价和感受
  3. 时态建议：介绍这项产品用一般现在时，如果涉及到发明或你与其互动的故事，用过去时。
  4. 其他：注意，这里的a piece of technology指的是一件带高科技的产品，注意，产品，不是某项高科技本身。为了让大家明确这一点，还专门贴心地附上了except computers。为了让串题不明显，尽量也避开手机。这道题看似是说明文，但题目后半句说到自己喜欢用，那么也可以用故事（记叙文）来展开。

I’d like to describe a gizmo that helps me sleep, a sleep aid device thing I’ll call it. I put this little guy on my nightstand the size of a football, beside my bonsai which is also said to be conducive to my sleep. It produces the so-called white noise, a rather constant and steady sound that claims to make one fall asleep faster and their sleep deeper.

Apparently, I’m super sound-sensitive, and even bothered by the slightest sound. The dog next door barking, someone nearby beeping their horn, my partner snoring like a chainsaw, and I’m often staring at the ceiling in frustration rather than sleeping soundly.

As I mentioned, this handy device isn’t intended to play so loudly that they overwhelmoutside disruptions. Instead, it produces white noise and works with my brain’s natural tendency to mostly notice new or variable sounds while tuning out steady, repetitiousnoise. That’s why people who live near freeways or in big cities tend to “get used to” steady traffic sounds, yet awaken when a taxi blares its horn, a helicopter flies overhead, or a dog suddenly barks.

I love this gadget with its soft, steady, repeating loops of sound, helping mask sudden or unusual noise, thus lulling my brain into relaxation, and eventually, sweet sweet sleep. Of course, really loud or irregular noise, such as my baby’s crying, is still going to break through, but in general, I sleep far more soundly with white noise playing in the background than without.

Well I have no idea who the inventor is, but whoever that is is a true hero. Thank god this little guy is right there with me. Sometimes I don’t turn it on, and just the thought that it is there soothes me.

【每日表达】句型、习语、小幽默

on fire

  • on a roll
  • 是不是有时候感觉自己是随性型选手，成绩好坏全看发挥。发挥好的时候所向披靡，发挥不好的时候萎靡不振。这种发挥好的时候的感觉，就可以用on fire或on a roll来形容，指最棒的状态，成功的状态。On a roll还可以形容因为状态好而变得话非常多，在兴头上；on fire还可以形容正处在情绪非常强烈的时刻。
  • With a growing economy and a dropping crime rate, the city has been on a roll.
  • He’s a funny guy, and last night he was really on a roll.
  • The team has been on fire, winning 10 of its last 11 games.
  • He was on fire with enthusiasm when his dad tried to teach him to hunt.

follow/steer/take the middle course/way/path

  • 有些人常常走极端，不左就右。然而很多情况下，极端情形都是比较危险的，会给自己和他人带来伤害。更多的人愿意在做选择时尽量往中间靠，这样最不可能发生危险。这与我们古人中庸的做法有类似。取折中的做法、走中间道路避免危害的行为就叫follow/steer/take the middle course/way/path. 注意哦，排列组合起来可以有9种说法。
  • Most parents steer a middle course between strict discipline and letting their kids run wild.

【P3技巧】比较类与利弊类

比较类 Compare similarities and differences

  • 可细分与不可细分
  • 横向与纵向
  • What are the (similarities and) differences between A and B?
  • Please compare A and B.
  • How has … changed in… years?
  • Do you prefer A or B, why?
  • A and B, which is better?
  • What are some differences between pop stars now and those in the past, like 30 years ago?
  • Which do you think is better? Raising children by parents themselves or by their grandparents?
  • Is there any difference between technology in cities and in small towns?
  • What is the difference between the kind of joy that money can buy and the kind that money can’t?
  • What is the difference between learning history from books and from the Internet?

利弊类 Illustrating advantages and disadvantages

  • 用假想敌帮助思考，注意单复数
  • Can you think of anyadvantages/disadvantages of…?
  • What are the advantages and disadvantagesof…?
  • What are the benefits of…?
  • Are there any disadvantages of…?
  • Would you prefer to …, and why?
  • What are the advantages and disadvantages of the popularity of talent shows on TV?
  • Are there any disadvantages of preserving old buildings in modern cities?
  • What are the benefits of letting students choose the subjects that they learn?
  • Would you prefer to go straight to your destination while traveling? Why?
  • Can you think of any advantages and disadvantages of working in a foreign country?

今日任务：回答下列问题，每题不低于40秒：

  1. What are some differences between pop stars now and those in the past, like 30 years ago?
  2. What is the difference between the kind of joy that money can buy and the kind that money can’t?
  3. What are the advantages and disadvantages of the popularity of talent shows on TV?
  4. What are the benefits of letting students choose the subjects that they learn?

【纠音跟读】语调综合

One way my personality has changed↑ since I was a child↑ is that I became honest. When I was a little kid↑, I always lied to my parents↑ because I didn’t want them to know that I stole money from them↑, didn’t do homework well↑ and played video games outside. Once I stole five Yuan from my mom↑ and used the money to play video games. Also↑, I was late for school. On that day↑, I lied to my teacher↑ and my parents. Even if I wasn’t caught by anyone↑, I still felt guilty of lying. I found that one lie makes many↑ and that once people realized that I had lied to them↑, they started not to believe me. I felt sad about myself at that time. Gradually↑, I started to hold the belief that honesty↑ is the best policy.

千年

In an age when human beings have learnt the code of human life, and can transmit their knowledge in seconds from one continent to another, no mother in the world can understand why her child should be left to die of malnutrition or preventable disease. No one can understand why they should be driven from their home, or imprisoned or tortured for expressing their beliefs. No one can understand why the soil their parents tilled has turned to desert, or why their skills have become useless and their family is left hungry. People know that these challenges cannot be met by one country alone, or by one government alone. Change cannot be held back by frontiers. Human progress has always come from individual and local initiatives, freely devised and then freely adapted elsewhere.

item.title: Day 17 Speaking
item.description: <h2 id="【P1】爱好类：运动"><a href="#【P1】爱好类：运动" class="headerlink"
item.content:encoded:
【P1】爱好类：运动

运动是这个题季非常钟爱的话题，出现在好几个P1大题中。我将这些题目一并归于这一道中。命题角度从个人喜好到常见运动、受欢迎运动及运动员等，不一而足。

What kinds of exercise do you do?

Sports are my thing, and I do all sorts of them, ranging from individual and fundamental ones like jogging and swimming, to collective and competitive ones like basketball and soccer.

What was your favorite sport when you were young?

I used to be crazy about soccer in my younger days and I was the best striker my elementary school team has to offer.

Do you like extreme sports?

Nah, not really. Do I look like someone who’s into crazy stuff? When it comes to sports, it’s always better safe than sorry, isn’t it?

What kinds of exercise are popular in your country?

Some sports are universally popular, like basketball and soccer, and you do see young fans everywhere. It’s worth mentioning that table tennis, as the so-called national ball, is well-liked among all groups.

Do you think children should play sports regularly?

I surely do since children need to build up their physiques and exercise absolutely helps with that. Apart from that, team spirit and a sense of competition should also be cultivated.

What kinds of sports would you like to try in the future?

I really wanna try diving. It seems thrilling to jump from a 10-meter platform, turn some somersaults and plunge into water. Besides, divers look fit, man, with 6 packs and things. I want that too.

landlubber／sink like a rock

Do you have a favorite sports star?

I like a bunch of athletes like NBA stars Lebron James, Kobe Bryant and rising soccer star Kylian Mbappe. But I can’t say any of them is my favorite.

Do you like to watch sports on TV?

Not really. I don’t even own a tv set in my apartment. But I do watch some reruns of soccer or Olympic events on the Internet.

【P1】天气类：雨天

这次天气类考的是雨天。我给咱们国家下雨规律和趋势做了一个小结，利用季风的原理可以回答绝大部分题目。但各位练习的时候要结合自己家乡的实际情况，选取里面的话来答哦。每道单题都要练习。

Does it rain much in China? (Where and when?)

Is there any part of China where it doesn’t rain much?

When does it rain most in your hometown?

In which season does it rain most in other parts of China?

Where does it rain most in China?

The annual precipitation every year maintains a medium-level. But it rains much more in the South and East than in the North and West. The mainland China is largely affected by monsoons which in summer come from the Pacific and the Indian Ocean carrying water. That’s why the South and East enjoy constant rains in summer. But since monsoons are blocked by plateaus and mountains, it scarcely rains throughout a year in those landlocked provinces in the North and West. Monsoons come in reverse directions in winter, making it rain less across the country then.

Do you remember any time when it rained particularly heavily in your hometown?

It was like 15 years ago when 7 days of torrential rains caused flooding that killed dozens of people in village. Schools were closed and work suspended. Bittersweet.

【P2】物件类：最爱的季节

Describe your favorite season of a year

  • What it is
  • When it is
  • How the weather is like
  • Why you like this season
  • What activities you usually do during it
  • Why you like it
  • How different kinds of weather would affect people
  1. 本题的A是a season，描述一个季节；B是your favorite, 限制这个季节的属性。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 简述该季节的时间和最大的特征
    • 详述该季节吸引人的地方
    • 总结感受，适当升华
  3. 时态建议：介绍季节本身的时间、特征用现在时，如果讲自己在该季节的经历，用过去时
  4. 其他：这是最经典的雅思P2之一，具有早期P2的特征——限制少，自由度高。选取某个季节的旅行计划、景物描述的素材，就可以匹配串题。但别忘了本题题目毕竟要求描述这个季节本身，因此第一段肯定要用一些语言叙述genera的东西。

Fall is my favorite season, by far, for aesthetic and personal reasons. it’s the third season in a year and it usually begins in September and ends in November. Well, at least here in Beijing. All the leaves from the trees and plants change into multi-colored works of art and fall away. It creates bare, and vulnerable branches, revealing the true scenery underneath. It also represents a beautiful cycle of loss, regeneration and regrowth once the spring season comes around. The dead leaves and branches on the ground disintegrate and turn into part of the soil, which are used as seeds and fertilizer later once the cold welcomes the warmer weather.

The fall is always full of magnificent colors. red and gold maples, brown and grey Chinese parasols, let alone colorful fruits and vegies, all interlocking pieces of the puzzle of nature. This season is perfect for taking beautiful scenic photos and spending time outdoors. Nothing can relax and rejuvenate more than enjoying nature and the surrounding scenery.

With cooler temperatures rolling in, saying goodbye to summer and hello to fall is not a hard thing to do. It’s a great time to break out the jackets, coats, boots, scarves and hats in preparation for the change in season. It’s also the perfect time to start making hot cocoa and lighting a fire in the fireplace. This season creates a sense of comfort, warmth and reflection. It’s a great time to go camping, fishing, go on a road trip or anything else where you can spend quality time with family and eat good food.

By the way, I was born in autumn equinox and I’m a Virgo. It’s said that people born around vernal and equinoxes are brighter and more sensible. I think it’s true, isn’t it?

【P2】物件类：童年玩具

Describe a toy you had (that you received) when you were a child.

  • What the toy was
  • When you had it
  • Who gave it to you
  • What it was like
  • How you used this
  • And explain how you felt about it.
  1. 本题的A是a toy，描述一个玩具；B是you had (that you received) when you were a child, 限制这个玩具的来历。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 简述这个玩具的基本信息、特征、来历
    • 详述这个玩具的玩法和特别之处
    • 总结玩具和童年
  3. 时态建议：介绍玩具本身的信息和特征可以用现在时，如果玩具已经不在了也可以用过去时。讲述与玩具的互动，用过去时。述及对现在的影响，可以用现在完成时。
  4. 其他：这也是一个比较老的P2题目了，典型的状物类题目，一定要描述清楚这个玩具的基本信息和特征。另外，可以走温情路线，把童年啊亲人啊放进去。

I’d like to describe my only toy back in childhood. That’s right, the only plaything I’ve ever had. It was a jack in the box. From the outside, it’s one of the typical kinds. It outwardly consists of a box with a crank. When the crank is turned, a music box mechanism in the toy plays a melody. After the crank has been turned at some random times, the lid pops open and a clown pops out of the box. But the clown on the coiled spring is no regular Jack, it’s a clown that doesn’t have white-face make-up or wear pointed hats and ruffled collars, but only has a sorrowful look. To make it even more pathetic, it wasn’t from anyone. I found it at 10 in a clearing within one suburb of my hometown, dilapidated and dirty. Then I picked it up and brought it back home.

Wait, I forgot to tell you that it must have been produced here in China, cause the music it plays when you crank is nothing but the Chinese folk song, Jasmine. As I recall, I used to play with it after school, cranked out my Jack, greeted him with different questions like “How you doing today?” or “Are you feeling better?”, totally random questions. Then I would tell him what happened at school and things. Truth is I was an outsider back then and bullied sometimes, and I really enjoyed talking with the sad clown cause I knew that he wouldn’t judge, and he obviously was a great listener.

Not until my family moved to a new place recently did I realize that I had left him under my bed for so long I couldn’t remember how I had stopped talking to him. And I don’t know whether it’s that I had been a grown-up and viewed things differently or… I was taken aback when I cranked my Jack out after nearly 10 years. It was the creepiest thing I’d ever seen!

【P2】物件类：好照片

Describe a (good) photo you have taken (/of you taken by someone)

  • Where you took it
  • When you took the photo
  • What/who is in the photo
  • And explain how you felt about the photo
  • where it was taken
  • When it was taken
  • Who took it for you
  • And why it was unforgettable for you)
  1. 本题的A是a photo，描述一张照片；B是 (good), you have taken (/of you taken by someone), 限制这张照片的来历（和属性）

  2. 提纲建议：

    • 简述这张照片的来历、基本信息
    • 详述这张照片上的人和物、背后的故事
    • 总结自己的感受，适当升华

  3. 时态建议：描述照片上的画面用一般现在时，讲述照片拍摄时的状况及画面承载的故事用过去时。

  4. 其他：关于照片的P2题目非常多，这次出现了两个版本（括号内为低频版本），差别在于你是拍摄者还是照片中的主人公。为了应对这两个版本，可以更换照片主人公与拍摄者，也可以直接讲成一张自拍，搞定。

A good photo has always been a major throwback. You find yourself reminiscing about what happened back then and having vicarious happiness or sorrows. I’m not a photographer. Actually I even have never owned a camera. But I take some photos every now and then with my phone, some of which I feel proud of.

Like this one I took on an undisturbed narrow lane of a schoolboy in the shaggy blue uniform crouching and touching a puppy. And he is shedding tears. I captured this moment as I passed by, surprised by how agreeable this scene seemed. Then I completely forgot this snapshot until I flickered through my album the other day getting ready to release my storage.

And I bumped into this one once again before I zoomed it in and wondered why the kid was crying. I observed it quite meticulously and didn’t wanna miss anything. Then I thought, um, the puppy was a little weird. Something was missing. Then I zoomed in to its biggest, and I gulped.

The poor little thing has only three legs. Turned out the caring kid wasn’t crying over nothing. For the first time ever, I took it closer and had a thorough look of the picture. The puppy has an innocent face with a playful look, sadness rarely detected. He is staring at our schoolboy with his bulgy jade-green eyes. Standing beside the kid, he seems enjoying the fondling. But the kid’s face crumples, as if he lost his beloved toy.

I have no idea what became of the kid and the puppy, but the photo, I will keep it.

【每日表达】句型、习语、小幽默

a blessing in disguise

  • 我国古代有塞翁失马的故事，焉知非福。祸从天降，不一定就是祸，咬咬牙，坚持坚持，说不定福就在里面藏着呢。福兮祸所依，祸兮福所伏。这个习语讲的就是这件事，字面上看指伪装起来的祝福，指的就是乍看像是坏事或厄运，但实际上是好事和福气。
  • Losing my job was a blessing in disguise — I never would have found this one if it hadn’t happened.

Murphy’s law

  • 墨菲定律想必大家都比较熟悉了，大致指的是自己认为可能会出错的事，最终真的就会出错。如果把它当一个习语用在句子中做一个名词性成分，那么就是一种幽默的表达，很有意思。
  • Sounds like Murphy’s Law is working overtime! （妈的刚还在想会不会加班，你看吧！）

【综合技巧】5“W”1“H”拓展法

Would you like to ride a bike to work in the future?
I would like to ride my bike to work in the near future.
  • (what)
  • Since sharing economy is lit, many commuters turn to those shared bikes, scanning a QR code and here they go. Which I find convenient and eco-friendly. I wanna try that too.
  • (Why)
  • Because cycling for me will not only for transportation, but for fitness too. As so you know, I major in computer science, ant I bet my work after graduation will be so tiring I wouldn’t have time to work out. Besides, it’s eco-friendly, right?
  • (When)
  • …But only when the weather’s good. I mean, here in Hainan, probably means, never.
  • (Where)
  • …But only if my workplace is not far from home, like within 10 kilometers or so. Or otherwise I would be sweaty and stinky all day long.
  • (Who)
  • Since I will be sharing my apartment with another 2 or three roommates and I may not have an office of my own, riding to work can be the only time to be alone.
  • (How)
  • Since I’m terrific at cycling, riding to work will be my preference. I will be able to scuttle through the concrete jungle with ease.
今日任务：开脑洞，回答下列每一道题，分别在5W1H的6个拓展方向中任选3个组合成答案。
  1. Do people still wear watches in your country?
  2. What kinds of foreign food are popular in your country?
  3. When you were young, what would you do if you felt bored?
  4. Do you think parents should keep an eye on the websites their children visit? (P3)
  5. What kinds of people often lose things? (P3)

【纠音跟读】并列与选择，语调扬起来

I’d like a bottle of coke↑, two bottles of apple juice↑, a hamburger↑ and two cake please↓.

I look around me↑ and the room has changed imperceptibly↑ and overtly. There are elephants on thin legs lining the walls↑, the people around me↑ have become giant insects, my watch melts↑ and slowly drips from my wrist. A Dalinian dream?↑ A Kafkaesque nightmare?↑ The breeze of surrealism blows through my hair; an existential whirlwind captures my imagination.

隐私

item.title: Day 16 Speaking
item.description: <h2 id="【P1】资讯类：广告"><a href="#【P1】资讯类：广告" class="headerlink"
item.content:encoded:
【P1】资讯类：广告

广告的类型、出现的地点、你对广告的态度，这是最常见的三个角度。

Are there many advertisements in your country?

There are tons of advertisements in my country. In a single day, one may be bombarded**with dozens of different commercials everywhere, if not** hundreds.

What are the various places when we see advertisements?

We see advertisements literally everywhere. Commercials on the Internet and TV, light**boxes in bus stops and subway stations, billboards on both sides of a highway, let alone posters and flyers. We’re suffocated**.

How do you feel about advertisements?

Most advertisements are annoying except for a few of fantastic aesthetic appeal and with a smart plot. But in general, I try to avoid them, but often in vain.

Why do you think there are so many advertisements now?

Well, producers and sellers try to tout their products and advertising campaigns are often rolled out long before production. And apparently, they work.

【P1】科技类：手机应用

这是比较时兴的科技类问题，是每个题季的重点类型。自己对常见的应用类型要有所了解，储备流行的、好用的、喜欢的应用。

What was the first app you used?

I had long been fantasizing about online shopping even before smartphone was invented. So yeah, I installed Taobao, an e-commerce app and registered the minute I got my first Samsung.

What kinds of apps would you like to have in the future?

I would say I’m quite satisfied with the apps I currently use. If anything, I would like to see more superapps with which many different things could be done.

What kinds of apps do you often use?

I’ve been using all sorts of apps, ranging from fundamental ones for calling, texting, and well, mapping apps, since I have a terrible sense of direction, to more specific ones like online dictionaries, shopping apps and most of all, games.

Do old people in your country like to use apps?

I doubt that. Most old people are not able to use a smartphone, not to mention apps. But my granny is a surprisingly proficient mobile expert.

What’s the most popular app in your country?

It’s gotta be WeChat, which I’m sure you have heard of or used already. It’s more than your typical instant messaging app. More functions like online payment, newsfeed and even games are integrated.

【P2】物件类：想换掉的东西

Describe something you own that you want to replace
  • What it is
  • Where it is
  • How you got it
  • And explain why you want to replace it
  1. 本题的A是something，描述某个东西；B是you own that you want to replace,限制这个东西的属性。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 简述这个东西的来源，状况，引出想换
    • 详述想换掉这个东西的原因
    • 总结感受，适当升华
  3. 时态建议：介绍这个东西本身可以用现在时，介绍来历用过去时。讲述想换的原因可用回现在时。
  4. 其他：想换的东西不一定是旧的破的，也可以是新的但是坏了、无法发挥其功能了。所以想材料和串题的时候不要被束缚住了。另外，从题目本身的表述来看，东西本身、想换的原因都是重点，不要偏废。

Let’s get it straight, I wanna replace my iPhone. But not because it doesn’t work properly anymore, but because the infamous “planned obsolescence” has beenunraveled which makes me uncomfortable. And yes, my current 6s is a victim, too.

First it was the touchscreen which has been incrementally slow since half a year ago. It seems that every phone misses or misinterprets a tap or a swipe occasionally. But my phone has been ignoring taps and swipes or extremely slow in registering and responding to them. Then it was the operation system. I made a rash decision to have updated it 3 months ago which turned out to be so wrong. I have to reset and restore my phone couple of times a day, or otherwise the apps would keep crashing and OS unstable.There’s more to it. The camera takes way too long to capture a photo, the battery life isn’t long enough to get through half of my day, and the phone overheats even when I’m not using it.

Come on, Apple. I only got it one and a half years ago. I have never missed my old Nokia so much. And then, I knew about the so-called planned obsolescence, a strategy that companies intentionally design products to die before their natural time, in order to force you to keep upgrading. Which Apple confirmed later this year. Shame on you, Apple! Shame on you! Even when the popular electronics company began pledging to slash prices for battery replacements. Unacceptable! Period.

【P2】物件类：有趣工作

Describe an interesting job that you want to have in the future
  • What it is
  • How you can find this job
  • What qualities it requires
  • And explain why it is an interesting job
  1. 本题的A是a job，描述一项工作；B是interesting, that you want to have in the future, 限制工作的属性和渊源。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 简述这项工作的名称、内容
    • 详述这项工作有趣之处，表达想尝试的意愿和如何找到、如何努力达到要求
    • 总结这项工作的特点，适当展望未来
  3. 时态建议：描述这项工作基本情况用现在时，描述自己与这项工作的渊源可以用过去时。展望未来用将来时或虚拟语气。
  4. 其他：注意本题的job不见得指的是自己以后要从事的职业，只是一项工作罢了，可能是临时的、可能是不挣钱的、可能只是为了尝鲜、可能非常新奇。所以，开脑洞吧！

Some jobs sound perfectly normal, like a product manager, but others too good to be true, like a bed tester. But I read an article about a job that combines these two the other day in Financial Times. The moment I shut that magazine, I was like, how amazing life would be if I got to spend my workdays testing beds and pillows, and the overall experiences of staying in a hotel.

Basically, the bed tester is a product tester job you can literally sleep through. But there’s more to it. A bed tester needs to start with the bed and the linen, bouncing up and down and learn how to lie down to make sure the mattress has no dips, check that the edge of the bed is strong enough to sit on and that the support is evenly distributed so that couples don’t roll into each other. Then, the overall experiences should also betaken into account. From check-in service, to the color scheme of the room, sanitary**conditions of bathrooms to whether the time they spend tweaking the design of the blackout curtains is worth it. Not to mention the room service of some extravagant**hotels.

I can’t help noticing that some review sites have been recruiting bed testers. Yelp, the Internet has become a highly valuable resource for consumers looking to get a sense of a restaurant, hotel, or product they might be interested in. I myself have landed superior experiences based on those reviews and recommendations several times. Being a bed tester, I might not only get paid to sleep, but help people. How cool it would be!

【P2】物件类：数学课技能

Describe a useful skill you learned in a math class in your primary/high school.
  • What the skill was
  • How you learned it
  • Who taught you
  • And why it was useful to you
  1. 本题的A是a skill，描述一项技能；B是useful, you learned in a math class in your primary/high school, 限制这项技能的属性和来源。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 简述这项技能的名称和来源，引出useful
    • 详述这项技能的内容和用处
    • 总结感受，适当升华
  3. 时态建议：介绍这项技能用现在时，讲述自己学习该技能的经历用过去时，讲述它的用处可以用现在时，也可以用过去时（讲具体经历）
  4. 其他：这是本题季的新题，体现了雅思口语P2新题角度新、偏的特点。注意，题目中给出“math class”不是白给的，一定要描述一个只能在数学课上学到的技能，什么合作精神、沟通技巧、人际交往、逻辑思维，通通不要来。否则，太像串题了。

I’m at times amazed by the astonishing simplicity and the impressive might of math, usually at the same time. Bernard Russel meant it when he wrote he tried to comprehendthe Pythagorean power by which number holds away above the flux. Here’s a perfect example of it, something eerily simple and powerful I learned in high school math class – normal distribution.

My math teacher Mr. Jiang was buzzed-up in that class saying that what we were gonna learn that day is the most prevalently used model of all time. So basically, a normal distribution is an arrangement of a data set in which most values cluster in the middle of the range and the rest taper off symmetrically toward either extreme.

Intelligence is one simple example of something that follows a normal distribution pattern: in a class, most students are of average intelligence, say iq 80, the numbers of people that are smarter or less smart than average are fairly equal, and a very small number of students are either extremely smart or extremely dumb.

Come to think of it, many things in life suddenly seem to have happened for a legitreason. Although we only acquired a drop in the bucket, I finally got it why most people are of average height, intelligence, capabilities and stuff. They are all stuck in the top of that “bell”. It suddenly struck me that in order to be something, I needed not only toemulate my peers, but go one better.

I know it sounds cliched, but the haunting bell-shaped curve just hovers above, reminding me that I need to fight, for life is tough and every second counts.

【每日表达】句型、习语、小幽默

It takes one to know one

  • 这是个很传神的习语，讲的是只有你自己在某方面做的不好，才能察觉出别人在这方面做的也不好。俩人吵架的时候最好用了：
  • Zack: You are a two-faced bitch! 你个两面人！
  • Clay: It takes one to know one. 呵呵，彼此彼此！
  • 然后，也可以替换中间的某些词，用我们说的“偷换习语”的方式，达到幽默的效果。这个习语最重要的词是takes，比如Damien Rice的歌 It takes a lot to know a woman, 指的就是说想了解一个女人，真的需要花非常多心力（no offence!）。
  • When my sister hammered about how bad a cook I was, I was like, well, it takes one to know one.

Live and learn (you live and you learn)

  • 这个习语常被翻译为“活到老学到老”，其实不太准确。它更像“人非圣贤孰能无过”+“吸取教训”这个意思，指人犯了错或者见到了新鲜事物，感叹自己“嗯，学习了”。
  • Today I learned that Japanese dip ingredients in raw eggs. Well, you live and you learn.

【综合技巧】过去时用不好，used to来讨巧

  • I used to dream of being a pilot.
  • I used to turn to my mom, tell her about my worries and ask her for advice.
  • Traffic used to be a pain in the neck. Cars used to be stuck, roads congested, pedestrians complaining about car horns. Such a mess!
  • I work in a hospital, so I’m used to long hours.
  • They’ve already been used to doing sports regularly.
今日任务：回答下列问题，注意运用used to：
  1. Did you ever give money to other kids when you were young?
  2. What did you do when you felt bored when you were young?
  3. What was your first watch like?
  4. What are the differences between technologies people use in big cities and the ones in small towns? (P3)

【纠音跟读】小词弱读与综合练习

at

  • we’ll have dinner at my apartment
  • i’ll be at school all day long
  • i’ll be at her house
  • i’m gonna be at Anna’s until 6
  • i’m busy at the moment

that

  • the movie that i saw was boring

  • wh thought that we could be there

  • i read that her team won

  • it wasn’t THAT easy

  • A I need to go to Walmart today. Do you want to come?

  • B No, I don’t think so. I’ve got a lot of work to do.

  • A Do you want me to get you anything there?

  • B Well, if you don’t mind, I could use some socks.

秋天

There is no month in the whole year, in which nature wears a more beautiful appearance than in the month of August. Spring has many beauties, and May is a fresh and blooming month, but the charms of this time of year are enhanced by their contrast with the winter season. August has no such advantage. It comes when we remember nothing but clear skies, green fields, and sweet-smelling flowers—when the recollection of snow, and ice, and bleak winds, has faded from our minds as completely as they have disappeared from the earth – and yet what a pleasant time it is! Orchards and cornfields ring with the hum of labours; trees bend beneath the thick clusters of rich fruit which bow their branches to the ground; and the corn, piled in graceful sheaves, or waving in every light breath that sweeps above it, as if it wooed the sickle, tinges the landscape with a golden hue. A mellow softness appears to hang over the whole earth; the influence of the season seems to extend itself to the very wagon, whose slow motion across the well-reaped field, is perceptible only to the eye, but strikes with no harsh sound upon the ear.

item.title: Day 15 Speaking
item.description: <h2 id="【P1】日常类：家庭时光"><a href="#【P1】日常类：家庭时光" class="headerlink"
item.content:encoded:
【P1】日常类：家庭时光

与家人的互动，是否已经成为了奢侈？本题很简单，借此多想想是不是陪家人的时间太少了吧。

What do you usually do when you are free?

I’m a homebody and I hate socializing. Being alone with myself, reading a fiction and binge watching my favorite shows seem so much better than going around consuming my life energy.

Do you like spending time with your family?

I absolutely do, but truth is, I only get to meet with them once a year in Spring Festival when all Chinese people are supposed to go back home and reunite with their families.

What do you and your family like to do in spare time?

If I had the chance, I would hang out with them, having meals and chatting about our life. some family activities like a picnic or an outing seem nice too.

Do you think you will spend more time with your family?

Don’t do this man. How I wish I could be with them more often. Family is my everything, and I work so hard in a faraway city for some reason.

【P1】情绪类：无聊

情绪类题目一定要拓展到具体的动作和实例，不要抽象描述情绪。另外，注意同意转换哦。

What do you do when you feel bored?

For the record, I seldom feel bored, cause this is such a wonderful world with myriads of things to explore. But when I do feel tedious, I meditate on my life.

What kinds of things are boring to you?

A lengthy meeting is the most tedious thing in the world. You don’t wanna listen to your boss droning on and on about trivia and you don’t wanna be seen tinkering with your phone by the HR. plus, I surely don’t enjoy being dragged to go window shopping by my girlfriend.

When you were young, what would you do if you felt bored?

I had a colorful childhood. I used to hang out with my mate playing video games whenever we were free. And since I have a superior IQ, study didn’t seem monotonous for me.

Do you think young people are more likely to get bored?

Not really. Young people are still exploring this wonderful world, trying new things all the time, while old people may be more likely to get bored with their life for they’ve already tried everything. But this is more of an individual matter and I’ve seen bored teenagers and engaged centenarians as well.

【P2】物件类：丢失要物

Describe something important that you lost in the past

  • When this happened
  • Where you lost it
  • What it was
  • What you did after it was lost
  • And explain why it was important
  1. 本题的A是something，描述一个东西；B是important, that you lost in the past, 限制东西的属性和故事性。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 简述这个东西的基本信息和重要性，讲故事的开头
    • 详述丢失的经过及后续
    • 总结经验教训，适当升华
  3. 时态建议：描述东西本身可以用现在时，讲述丢失和后续的经历用过去时。
  4. 其他：本题是物件+事件类的题目，东西本身和丢失及后续的经过缺一不可。

So I lost the diamond from my engagement ring. Compared to all we lose in an ordinary lifetime, losing a stone was not to be considered a tragedy. So, why, then, did I feel such loss?

I sulked around for days after I tried to backtrack and find it, when it didn’t appear. In retracing my steps, I recalled that I was on an unfamiliar treadmill and smacked my hand on the night that I lost it. I didn’t realize it was gone until the next morning. Then I searched under every piece of equipment at that gym I was visiting; I put up reward signs, I searched the pavement of the parking lot. This was months ago, and finally I knew it was time to replace it, and start to build new memories.

Despite my fears, my marriage seemed rock steady. I thought about not replacing it, not spending the money on something so frivolous. But I really missed my ring, missed the little stone that was in his pocket when my spouse proposed in Mauritius a lifetime ago, in another century, another millennium. I felt lucky though that I turned to him who then told me something and the lightbulb went on: The diamond is not our marriage. It has nothing to do with our marriage.

I didn’t even need to be reassured that we had this enduring bond. What I realize is that I may have lost something valuable, I did not lose something precious.

【P2】物件类：有趣学科

Describe an interesting subject (your favorite subject) that you learned at school
  • What the subject was
  • What your teacher was like
  • When and where you study(ed) it
  • How long you learned this subject
  • And explain why it was interesting to you (why it is/was your favorite)
  1. 本题的A是subject，描述一个科目；B是interesting, (your favorite), that you learned at school, 限制该学科的范围和属性。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 简述这个科目的基本信息，何时何地所学，内容、学了多久、老师的基本情况等
    • 详述这个科目有趣的地方
    • 总结这个科目给自己人生带来的变化
  3. 时态建议：描述学科本身以现在时为主，讲自己学习的经历用过去时。
  4. 其他：这是一篇说明文，讲一个在校学习的科目，没有限制是哪个阶段，因此小学到大学都可以，主流非主流科目也都可以，因此范围相当广。但要注意不要盲目串题，不是简简单单把某个故事或人物说进来就可以了的，必须以科目本身开头，过度到具体内容。也可以说得比较个人化一些。

I’m not gonna mouth platitude about how a good teacher changes one’s life, but my high school English teacher, Ms. Lin, really made me love the subject. As so you know, ordinary Chinese youngsters learn English for all but 12 years, from grade one in elementary school to senior 3 in high school. Well, I hated it for the first 9 years, but everything changed in the tenth year. I would say, Ms. Lin and her English classes changed my life.

Ms. Lin always seems to be in a jolly mood, which is quite contagious and made me bubbly in every single English class. Unlike typical English teachers who merely instructed students to do endless exercises, assigned them with endless homework and ordered them to recite endless passages, Ms. Lin, a breath of fresh air, held various gaming activities like debates, charades and a very different version of Family Feud.

Among which a very speech contest is a turning point of my life. She first gave us a topic, a feminist one I couldn’t remember clearly, then left us 15 minutes to get ready. Then we were requested to go on the dais and address our classmates after which they should answer the tough questions raised by Ms. Lin, who was playing devil’s advocate.

The first half of my speech was a fiasco. I couldn’t figure out where to look at and I was fidgeting with my draft. But Ms. Lin kept smiling at me. I gritted my teeth and unbelievably did a much better job in the second half.

I couldn’t believe it, cause as so you know, I had been this intimidated guy who wouldn’t be able to speak in public. And ever since then, I magically have become a lot more confident man and definitely a better version of myself.

【P2】物件类：想尝试的运动

describe a sport that you have watched (on TV) before and you want to try

  • what it is
  • when you first watched the sport
  • what you like about the sport
  • and explain why you would like to try this sport
  1. 本题的A是a sport，描述一项运动；B是that you have watched (on TV) before and you want to try, 限制这项运动的渊源。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 简述这项运动的基本信息，在哪里看到，引出想尝试
    • 详述这项运动吸引人的地方，以及自己想尝试的打算
    • 总结感受，表达希望尝试的意愿
  3. 时态建议：描述这项运动本身用一般现在时，讲述自己看电视的经历用过去时。展望未来想尝试可用将来时或虚拟语气。
  4. 其他：本题可以理解为纯介绍运动的说明类话题，从电视看到只是一个切入点；也可以理解为说明+经历类题目，把这项运动本身的介绍和看电视的经历结合起来。

The first time I watched an Olympic curling match on television, I entertained a thought that is surely shared by everyone who sees the sport for the first time: What the hell am I looking at? It was during the 2014 Games in Sochi, Russia, and I tuned in to the live feed at the very beginning of a women’s medal match. I was intrigued by the grace of the players and how they could effortlessly slide those huge, bulb-like stones down the ice. But everything else about it was confusing. It looked sort of like shuffleboard, but with more yelling. And lots of weird stuff. The rules, the lingo, the way they used brooms—brooms! —to make the stones slide around. And it just seemed so boring. How could anyone endure watching a sport with such a lack of obvious athleticism, such inscrutable gameplay, and such a lethargic pace?

By the two-hour mark, I was riveted. I still didn’t understand what the brooms were for, but I was beginning to figure out the rules. The lingo was beginning to make sense. And I was absolutely consumed by the drama. When the match ended, I quickly turned on my computer and scoured the Internet for every curling broadcast for the rest of the Olympics, including reruns. I was hooked. What had started as a chance encounter with an esoteric sport had ended in an insatiable thirst for more, immediately.

Well, I’ve always wanted to try some winter sports which are not common in my province. We are in the South. But if I ever look for an ice-sport that doesn’t require strenuosity but intelligence and strategies, curling seems nice!

It was a high voltage match／However, it was difficult for me to enjoy the entire match as I could not see the replays and the slow-motion ball deliveries or the LBW replays on the screen as I am habituated to watch them on the television screen.／Overall, it was a nice experience for me. Visiting the stadium, the crowd of people, shouts from the supporters and the other matters were attractive. I was too happy to see some of the international players from a close distance.

The topmost reason is that it was my very first experience of watching a cricket match inside a stadium

【每日技巧】句型、习语、小幽默

time flies when you’re having fun
  • 快乐的时光总是过得很快，痛苦的时光确是煎熬。好了，我们就学个前半句吧。Time flies when you’re having fun，完美表达了这个意思。当你在玩乐的时候，时间转瞬即逝。当然，这个说法也不见得非得要用于快乐的时光。如果用于痛苦的时光，那就是一种幽默啦。
  • -I can’t believe we’ve spent four hours cleaning this carpet.
  • -Well, time flies when you’re having fun.
the whole nine yards
lock, stock barrel
  • 两个人离婚分居，一方把家里的一切都带走了，除了那一亩三分地。家里的一切陈设、锁具，连那个大水桶也不放过。这个“一切”，就可以用这两个习语来表达。
  • I served a huge Thanksgiving dinner: turkey, mashed potatoes, pumpkin pie—the whole nine yards.
  • She gave her friend everything she owned, lock, stock, and barrel, and moved away.

【P3技巧】原因类与立场类

原因类 Listing reasons

  • 按照逻辑顺序排布原因，若临场无法想出第二个原因，则继续多样拓展第一个原因。
  • Why do you think…?
  • Can you give me some reasons/causes for…?
  • What are the factors of…?
  • What is it that…?
  • Why do Chinese people like to go to celebrations like wedding ceremonies?
  • Why do some people like to share things with strangers rather than with family members?
  • There are more and more people freelancing. Why?
  • Why do some people hate sports?
  • Why do you think some people choose to be vegetarians?
立场类 Defending your stance
  • 坚定立场不动摇，先画靶子后射箭
  • Do you think…?
  • Is it a good idea to…?
  • Should we/people/citizens/the government…?
  • Do you agree that…?
  • Do you think weather has an influence on people’s mood?
  • Do you think technology always has positive effects?
  • Do you think parents should control what websites their children visit?
  • Do you think competition is important at work places?

今日任务：回答下列问题，每道题不低于40秒：

  1. Why do some people like to share things with strangers rather than with family members?
  2. There are more and more people freelancing. Why?
  3. Do you think technology always has positive effects?
  4. Do you think parents should control what websites their children visit?

【纠音跟读】小词弱读1

  • to
  • for
  • can
  • Let’s go to the store!
  • I thought to myself
  • how to do it
  • I bike for transportation/transport, not for fitness.
  • This is for my parents’ anniversary.
  • They’re for the idea, not against it.
  • I can do that.
  • you can have my ticket.
  • I can.
思维

But growth requires a seed, and the heart of the creative process lies in the production of theoriginal fertile nucleus from which growth can proceed.This initial step in all creation consists in the establishment of a new unity from disparate elements, oforder out of disorder, of shape from what was formless. The mind achieves this by the plastic reshaping, so as to form a new unit, of a selection of the separate elements derived from experience and stored in memory. Intuitions arise from richly unified experience.

item.title: Day 14 Speaking
item.description: 【P1】基本信息：名字
item.content:encoded:
【P1】基本信息：名字

Do you like your name?

I can’t say I like it, since the two character are both mediocre, one of them being among top 10 most common Chinese characters.

Does your name have any special meaning?

It does. The first character is my family name, obviously, and the second is my mom’s family name. so basically my dad gave me this name to show his love to, well, my mom.

What kinds of names are popular in China?

Certain characters are common choices for new parents in here, like Gang and Qiang for boys which mean steel and strength, Li and Ting for girls which means beauty and elegance. They are just like Jason Tom Mary in English world.

Are there many Chinese people who have the same name as you?

There surely are. I once googled my name, only to find that there are thousands of peoplesharing this name, some of whom are criminals.

Is there any tradition about naming babies?

Come to think of it, many Chinese parents resort to fortune-tellers who suggest several characters that are supposed to make up for what the babies are missing in their future life, based on our philosophical five-element system.

Is it hard to change one’s name in China?

I think it is. I mean, you need to go through complicated formalities. You go to the police station, prove who you are by providing certificates, fill in endless forms, wait for several months, and here you go.

【P1】爱好类：音乐

乐类型、听音乐的时间、音乐会演唱会，这些是这个题季音乐话题的重点。

When do you listen to music?

I can’t live without music, which is why I listen to it literally all the time, esp. when I’m working on something else. I believe it boosts my productivity.

How much time do you spend listening to music every day?

Let me do the math here. I spend 2 hours commuting every day, 30 minutes in all on my meals and 10 minutes taking a shower. I have to treat my ears when I’m doing these. So almost 3 hours every day.

What kinds of music do you like to listen to?

I’m a classical music buff. I enjoy symphonies, sonatas and piano concertos from Tchaikovsky and Beethoven. And jazz music too, when I really need to wind down.

Have you ever been to a concert before?

Duh. I’ve been to several concerts, both classical ones and pop ones. I recently went to Lang Lang’s piano recital in Beijing Music Hall. It was awesome.

Do you prefer to go to a live concert or watch one on TV?

A live concert, always a live concert. If you don’t wanna feel the vibe of live music, then why bother tuning in to a concert anyway?

【P2】事件类：好决定

Describe a (good) decision someone made.

  • Who made it
  • What was the decision
  • When it was made
  • And explain why it was a good decision.
  1. 本题的A是a decision，描述一个决定；B是 (good), someone made, 限制做决定的主体（以及决定的属性）。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 简述决定做出的情形、时间地点、主人公
    • 详述做出该决定的原因、经过、结果
    • 总结和感受，讲出为对这个决定的评价
  3. 时态建议：过去时，没得商量。
  4. 其他：注意，本题做决定的主体不见得是自己，可以是任何一个人，而纯粹的过去时要求决定故事可以发生在任何时候。而故事人物所做的任何一个小的决定，都符合题目要求。因此，这个题应该和happy experience一道列为本题季最简单的两道题之一。

I’d like to tell you a story about a momentous decision which turned out a matter between life and death. It was made by the fabled Antoinede Saint-Exupery, the author of The Little Prince. He used to be a senior pilot and combated in Spanish civil war. Unfortunately, he was taken captive. A simple decision to smile to his enemy, a jailer saved his life.

He was sure that he was to be killed and became terribly nervous. He fumbled in his pockets for cigarettes, but no matches were found before he looked through the bars at the jailer and asked if he got a light. The jailer looked at him, shrugged and came over to light the cigarette with his eyes inadvertently locked with our pilot. At that moment, our pilot decided to smile, out of nowhere, nervously. To his surprise, the jailer smiled too, and soon asked about his family and kids. They exchanged photos and our pilot was shedding tears worrying about whether he would see his kids again.

Suddenly, without another word, the jailor unlocked his cell and silently led him out. Out of the jail, quietly and by back routes, out of the town. There, at the edge of town, he released our pilot and turned back toward the town.

Antoinede proudly wrote about how this smile saved his life in his memoir. If he hadn’t made this decision to let loose a smile to his enemy which could have been an irritant, he wouldn’t have been able to live and left this heartwarming tale of the little prince.

【P2】事件类：离家旅行

Describe a holiday/vacation (away from home) you would like to take in the future

  • When and where you would like to go
  • Who you would like to go with
  • What you would like to do on this holiday
  • And explain why you would like to take the holiday
  1. 本题的A是a holiday/vacation，描述一次外出经历；B是 (away from home), you would like to take in the future, 限制时间（和地点）。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 简述这次假期的时间、地点、任务
    • 详述假期的相关信息，如计划的过程
    • 总结和感受，讲出为何要度假的原因
  3. 时态建议：将来时或虚拟语气。如果用plan的框架，可以用现在时。讲解地方风土人情也用现在时。
  4. 其他：本题是对自己某次假期的畅想，除了描述自己即将度假的点点滴滴之外，不要忘了解释自己为什么要度这个假，比如学习工作压力太大，假期拿来好好放松就是个很好的理由。

I’ve been telling myself for the last couple of weeks that I really deserve a break. I mean, who could live with being on overtime for consecutive 3 months to get one single job done. I work for 12 hours every day lately, dealing with bags of paperwork and concalls. But whenever I get to breathe, I take a look at my calendar with the red circle on the whole week next month – my annual leave. I conjured up this plan to travel to Antarctica the day I had a sudden heat stroke on my way to work last month. I mean,parenthetically, I was aided by people passing by to hobble to a store and enjoyed a few minutes of air-con. I’ve had enough!

I happened to know a travel maniac, a backpacker who’s been to the frozen continent 3 times before. I couldn’t have elaborated my itinerary without his help. For my ten-day journey, he suggested I take the classic trip which would take me on an awe-inspiring journey down to the Antarctic Peninsula giving me around 6 days exploring the peninsula and surrounding Islands. I can’t wait to enjoy dramatic ice scenery. Towering icebergs, glaciers and huge fields of ice set the backdrop for a host of outstanding wildlife. He also told me not to miss the South Georgia for even more wildlife viewing opportunities. This is where I may find nesting albatrosses and beaches filled with king penguins and their chicks, plus beached elephant and fur seals.

Unfortunately, my friend has already booked the flight to South Africa or otherwise we could go to the icy paradise together. I’m not afraid of being alone or anything. It’s just, who will take a picture of me and my waddly friend?

【P2】物品类：有趣动物

Describe an interesting animal

  • What it looks like
  • When you saw/knew it for the first time
  • Where it lives
  • And explain why it is interesting
  1. 本题的A是an animal，描述一个动物；B是interesting, 限制该动物的属性。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 简述这个动物的基本信息，引出有趣
    • 详述这个动物有趣的地方
    • 表达对这个动物的情感倾向
  3. 时态建议：描述动物以现在时为主，讲述自己看见或认识这种动物的经历时用过去时。
  4. 其他：观察题目不难发现，这是一篇说明文，因此一定要以对动物的基本信息描述开始。但这并不意味着不能讲故事，tip中有要求讲明第一次知道或看见该动物的情形，因此也可以选择以初见经历串起整题。

I’d like to describe my favorite animal by far, the owls. These mysteriously nocturnal raptors never fail to fascinate me. As a bird of prey, they can be extremely adorable too, which can be evidently spotted in a gif that went viral in which a baby owl is tilting his head as if he’s curiously watching you.

By the way, did you know the fact that owls can actually rotate their heads more than 120 degrees in either direction? I found this eerily funny the first time I saw one snowy owl doing it in the zoo when I was like 10, which is also the first time I saw an owl ever. I was so scared of the hissing sound it made and the ridiculously terrifying head turning. But that image haunted me for several years until I saw that the second time when I found that I had finally come to terms of it.

They are mythologically mysterious too. I mean, they symbolize contradictory things in different cultures. In ancient Greece, the Little Owl was the companion of Athena, the Greek goddess of wisdom, which is one reason why owls symbolize learning and knowledge. But Athena was also a warrior goddess and the owl was considered the protector of armies going into war. If Greek soldiers saw an owl fly by during battle, they took it as a sign of coming victory. Interestingly, from ancient times on, owls have been linked with death, evil, and other superstitions. Many cultures saw owls as a sign of impending death. For example, an owl was said to have predicted the death of Julius Caesar. They’ve also been associated with witches and other so-called evil beings. While this may sound like Halloween fun, many cultures still have superstitions about owls and in some places, and owls are killed based on these beliefs.

Well, don’t do that. Please.

【每日表达】句型、习语、小幽默

By fair means or foul

By hook or by crook

  • 为了上位、为了成功、为了财富名誉、为了爱情，有很多人会不择手段，直到达到目的。手段正不正义对他们来说不重要，结果比较重要。不择手段，就可以用这两个习语来表示。从构成上看，这两个习语都包含了正义、不正义的两种手段，正好可以说明这种两面性。
  • The young Jack was so determined to start his own business, by hook or by crook.
  • We need to get that damn thing on TV, by fair means of foul!

Like taking candy from a baby

It’s not rocket science (it doesn’t take a rocket scientist)

  • 形容一件事易如反掌，英文里有许多有趣的习语，这就是其中的两个。一个是说，这件事容易得像是从婴儿手里抢糖果（婴儿招谁惹谁了），另一个是说这件事并不难，它又不是火箭科学（火箭科学：mmp）。这两种说法同时也有幽默的效果哦。
  • We’re talking basic common sense here - it isn’t rocket science.
  • Asking her to marry me is supposed to be like taking candy from a baby. We’ve been together for 10 years.

【综合技巧】列举变长句，语调加上去

  • And of course, there was the idea that our life there would be one long afternoon curled up by a blazing fire eating freshly baked cake, having been on a bracing walk on which our apple-cheeked children would have gathered bugs, birds’ nests and wild flowers.
  • I used to save money to buy whatever I wanted, like wonderful novels, exquisite stationery, scrumptious snacks, and so forth.
  • We hang out all the time, cooking dinners at home, watching a blockbuster film in the theater, shopping in a local mall, and so on.
  • I enjoy driving to school back then, and to my office now.
  • I enjoy driving, no matter a long-haul journey or a short-haul one.

今日任务：回答以下问题，每题至少加上一处列举变长句，记住变语调哦。

  • What do you usually do with your family?
  • Do you think the weather influences human activities or not?
  • What is the difference of living in the city and in the countryside? (P3)
  • Do old people in China like to hoard things? (P3)

【纠音跟读】帮单词找C位

  • picture doctor dictate punctuate
  • adventure advice advertisement abnormal acclamie acknowledge
  • in this picture, there’s a doctor and adictator learning punctuation.
  • it’s adivsed in the advertisement that the abnormal adventure doesn’t deserve acclamation.
完整

Once a circle missed a wedge. The circle wanted to be whole, so it went around looking for its missing piece. But because it was incomplete and therefore could roll only very slowly, it admired the flowers along the way. It chatted with worms. It enjoyed the sunshine. It found lots of different pieces, but none of them fit. So it left them all by the side of the road and kept on searching. Then one day the circle found a piece that fit perfectly. It was so happy. Now it could be whole, with nothing missing. It incorporated the missing piece into itself and began to roll. Now that it was a perfect circle, it could roll very fast, too fast to notice flowers or talk to the worms. When it realized how different the world seemed when it rolled so quickly, it stopped, left its found piece by the side of the road and rolled slowly away.

The lesson of the story, I suggested, was that in some strange sense we are more whole when we are missing something. The man who has everything is in some ways a poor man. He will never know what it feels like to yearn, to hope, to nourish his soul with the dream of something better. He will never know the experience of having someone who loves him give him something he has always wanted or never had.

item.title: Day 13 Speaking
item.description:
【P1】日常类：绿植

强调绿植，而不是植物呢？因为在很多外国人眼里，flowers are
item.content:encoded:
【P1】日常类：绿植

强调绿植，而不是植物呢？因为在很多外国人眼里，flowers are not plants，plants特指绿色植物。所以答题的时候尽量避免提到花。

Do you keep plants at home?

Well, I used to. I bought 3 bonsais and several lovely succulents on a whim last year. But they all died as a result of excessive watering.

What plant did you grow when you were young?

This may surprise you, but every Chinese citizen is obliged to plant at least 1 tree per year, that’s why we used to go on a tree-planting trip organized by school every year. I planted at least 10 saplings. But I have no idea what became of them.

Do you know anything about growing a plant?

I know nothing about it. Come to think of it, all the potted plants constantly died last year due to my negligence. Don’t worry, I’m not planning to grow anything.

Do Chinese people send plants as gifts?

We sometimes do, yeah. Potted plants are well-liked housewarming gifts. They are practical too, considering they absorb carbon-dioxide and formaldehyde, right?

【P1】日常类：新食物

foodie们的专属题。你尝遍全球了吗？要积累一些外国食物的名称和特征哦。当然还有形容东西好吃和难吃的形容词。

Have you ever tried foreign food?

I just did the other day when a backpacker friend came back from France with canned snails. Well, let’s just say, I threw it away the second I opened it.

Do you like to try new food?

I’m always ready to embrace new things in life and trying new food is one of my things, esp. when I travel to new places. Exotic food never fails to fascinate me.

What kinds of new food have you tried recently?

Well, other than the snails, I tried Tom Yum Goong, a hot and sour Thai soup, pretty much a mishmash of sea food and spices like lemongrass and chili peppers. Sounds appetizing right?

What kinds of foreign food are popular in your country?

American fast food restaurants are a dime a dozen in any Chinese city where fried chicken, French fries and hamburgers are served. Japanese delicacies like sushi and sashimi have been new favorite too.

Do you like any food from the countries near China?

I tried sukiyaki, the Japanese hotpot recently and I can’t get enough of it. It’s basically a dish consisting of thin slices of meat, tofu, and vegetables cooked in soy sauce and sugar. What’s most interesting about it is that dips are raw eggs. Yum!

【P2】事件类：帮助他人

Describe an occasion when you helped a person

  • What the situation was
  • Who you helped
  • How you helped him/her
  • And explain how you felt after helping them.
  1. 本题的A是an occasion，描述一个场合；B是 when you helped a person, 限制内容。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 简述事件发生的时间、地点、参与人物，点出需要帮助的方面
    • 详述帮忙的经过
    • 总结和感受，适当升华
  3. 时态建议：过去时，没得跑。
  4. 其他：本题是最标准的事件类话题，只需要按照现行后续描述事件即可。注意叙事要突出细节。

So I’m a backpacker and I’ve been so many places in my life and time, but I can never forget last summer when I finally comprehended how surreal it feels to save lives. I rented a Harley and drove on the Delhi-Gurgoan Highway like a streak of lightening with the beat of Eminem. Cool, right?

Then a white car loomed on the edge of the road which seemed not ready to pull off. I just had a hunch that there was something wrong cause I saw its blinkers were on before I parked behind it and drank some water from my water bladder. Then I went closer to the car, waiting to check on the driver.

I had never seen someone suffer like that in my entire life. there he was, a twenty-something in a white jacket, coughing, wheezing and shuffling for something. Apparently, he was heavily out of breath and desperately in need of help. I soon recalled that an asthma can be alleviated with water. I saw it on tv and I couldn’t imagine how it was like to have the muscles around the airways contracting. Then I took my extra bottle of water from my backpack and offered it to him right away. He struggled to open the door, staggered out, grabbed the bottle and gulped it down. Then he hobbled further and threw up. He was feeling better, I could sense that.

I helped him back to his car and asked him if he needed to go to the hospital. He replied with a voice flat and tired. Then I stayed with him for another 15 minutes until he said he was a lot better and could drive again. I took out of my water bladder, handed it to him and said, “In case you need it later” before I walked to my Harley. I noticed when I drove past him that he wanted to say something but couldn’t utter it because he was too feeble. I got it and I didn’t need to hear it.

【P2】事件类：感兴趣的历史

Describe a historical era(/period) you are interested in

  • When this era was
  • How you learned about it
  • What it is famous for
  • What happened during that period
  • And explain why you are interested in this era
  1. 本题的A是a historical era/period，描述一个历史时期；B是you are interested in, 限制特征。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 简述这一历史时期的基本信息，大致起讫时间等
    • 选择这一历史时期有趣的一两个地方详讲
    • 总结和感受，适当升华
  3. 时态建议：讲历史时期的基本情况和事件用过去时，述及后人的评价可用完成时和现在时。
  4. 其他：本题需要注意留余地，因为感兴趣的历史之所以还能继续让你感兴趣，大致上就是因为还有你不了解的地方。另外本题在准备的时候，资料比重是比较大的，一定要做好充分的准备。

Just the other day, I bumped into a picture when I was browsing news sites which showed some early morning bathers at a hotel pool in Las Vegas stop to watch the mushroom cloud of an atomic detonation at a test site about dozens of miles from the city. It was taken in the 1970. There’s nothing more appalling than nuclear bombs. But I’m so curious about how people dealt with that primal fear on a daily basis back then.

It followed by the report that throughout the 1950s, atomic detonations brought countless tourists to Sin City — and helped make it what it is today. The threat of nuclear annihilation throughout the Cold War, its early years especially, might conjure up images of schoolchildren being told to “duck and cover” under their desks in the event of an attack, which one can get hint of easily by watching movies about that period. However, it soon struck me that in classic American fashion, fear wasn’t the only reaction. In addition to the terrified people who built bomb shelters in their backyards, there were also plenty of enterprising individuals who saw the silver lining, or should I say green lining, of the Atomic Age.

In the 1940s, the United States government began its first batch of nuclear testing in the desert areas near to Las Vegas. And soon the Soviet Union followed suit. Escalations were rampant and people in an unprecedented fear. But the pictures and report I read about showed the other side of the story. To take the edge off the tremendous horror, people tried to have some fun of theirs.

I was literally shocked by how resilient people sought happiness through embracing tough times. Compared to that interesting period, the adversities my generation encounter are all but nothing.

【P2】事件类：花钱少的外出

Describe a special day out（a day out/a special day which did not cost too much）

  • What day it was
  • Where you went on that day
  • What you did
  • Who you went out with
  • And explain why it was special to you
  1. 本题的A是a day out，描述一次外出经历；B是special, which did not cost too much, 限制特征。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 简述这一外出的时间、地点、人物，引出省钱多
    • 详述外出的过程、省钱的经过和结果
    • 总结和感受，点出special，适当升华
  3. 时态建议：没商量，过去时。将special如果上升到对人生的影响，可以是完成时或现在时。
  4. 其他：本题是典型的记叙文，讲故事，注意故事的细节和生动性。另外，没花多少钱也是一个重点，需要单独提出，最好独立成段。Special也需要体现在外出的经过中。

So last Sunday, I was still in my sweet dreams when my cousin Jerry bombarded me with voice messages on WeChat, urging me to get dressed and go on a picnic with him and my 70-year-old grandma. Wait, did he just mention something about taking our grandma, the lovely old lady out, first time this year? How could I miss it, right? I soon got up and went straight to the nearby store and purchased the lamb kabab, marshmallows and beverages as Jerry suggested, which cost me only 50 rmb.

It was a typical picnic. Jerry drove us to a riverside park in a suburb. We unfolded the cloth, unloaded our snacks and got ready to be kissed by the brisk breeze of morning wind. I hadn’t had that guilty pleasure of binge eating cheap junk food for too long that I devoured 10 kebabs, 2 bags of marshmallows and 2 cans of soda. It’s good to see my granny taken aback by my sound belch.

Plus, it was nice talking with my grandma and my cousin. We chatted about a lot of stuff, from my stressful study to my cousin’s new job, to my grandma’s recent medical treatment. We had a great talk. My grandma is a loving and caring old lady Who loves spinning yarns and talking about how frugal their life in the past was, how we should save as much as can. And of course, the rehashed version of the old story of how she was adopted and how her biological brother would wade miles and miles to get her back home to stay for a day and send her back. We were blessed to be able to hear that story time and time again.

【每日表达】句型、习语、小幽默

be all eyes

be all ears

  • 这两个习语很生动地表达出了全神贯注地看或听的场面、动作。当需要全神贯注看一个精彩绝伦的表演或惊心动魄的电影时，是不是希望自己全身上下长满了眼睛，不错过任何一个细节？用all eyes就可以表达出这种感受。相类似的，听一段美妙的音乐或富有感染力的演讲时，也希望自己全身上下长满了耳朵，听到每个乐器的呼吸，听到演讲者的抑扬顿挫。用all ears就能表达。记住，这两个习语用作表语是最常见的，因此记得在前面加be动词。
  • Children were all ears when Granny spun yarns.
  • Once Michael appeared on the stage, the audience was all eyes.

play fast and loose (with)

  • 有些电影电视剧小说等文艺作品在处理历史类题材时，往往会做大量的改编，与史实有重大出入。这当然有好有坏。对于历史或任何事实的“戏说”、改编、不认真看待，就叫play fast and loose with something. 当然，这个习语不光只能用在文艺作品中，对任何东西不认真看待都可以用。情感倾向上，它用作中性偏贬义居多。
  • The film is very entertaining even though it plays fast and loose with the historical facts.

【P2技巧】什么是好故事？

People keep talking about how shallow puppy love is, but I’m pretty sure that I was in love at first sight. So it was the orientation day of high school and a scorching summer day. I was fondling with the piano right in the corner of the teaching building lobby. I was a mediocre player back then and played a little Chopin, clumsily. But no one was listening, so I didn’t care. Then I saw a reflection on the upright lid looming. It was a girl with her black hair flowing over her shoulders. I was getting a little intense and electricity which got the best of my fingers. I botched the Nocturne. Sorry Chopin.

Turned out she would be my classmate for the next three years and coincidentally a better piano player. She sat on the stool with her back straightened up and began the miraculous Fantasie Impromptu. Beautiful Andante was flowing in the air, like her hair, and like my heart.

…

Then a white car loomed on the edge of the road which seemed not ready to pull off. I just had a hunch that there was something wrong cause I saw its blinkers were on before I parked behind it and drank some water from my water bladder. Then I went closer to the car, waiting to check on the driver.

I had never seen someone suffer like that in my entire life. there he was, a twenty-something in a white jacket, coughing, wheezing and shuffling for something. Apparently, he was heavily out of breath and desperately in need of help. I soon recalled that an asthma can be alleviated with water. I saw it on tv and I couldn’t imagine how it was like to have the muscles around the airways contracting. Then I took my extra bottle of water from my backpack and offered it to him right away. He struggled to open the door, staggered out, grabbed the bottle and gulped it down. Then he hobbled further and threw up. He was feeling better, I could sense that.

I helped him back tohis car and asked him if he needed to go to the hospital. He replied with avoice flat and tired. Then I stayed with him for another 15 minutes until he said he was a lot better and could drive again. I took out of my water bladder, handed it to himand said, “In case you need it later” before I walked to my Harley. I noticed when I drove past him that he wanted to say something but couldn’t utter it because he was too feeble. I got it and I didn’t need to hear it.

今日任务

  • 重新练习以下P2题目，注意调整结构，加上环境、人物动作、神态、心理活动等具体描述。
  • Describe a happy experience.
  • Describe a time when you had to change your plan (or you changed your plan).

【纠音跟读】失去爆破2

  • I have 5 books to read.
  • This yard is about 100 square meters, give or take.
  • Just do it
  • I’m loving it
  • I’ve finished my first book in age 24. How about that?
  • I’m not fed up with writing yet.
  • How often do you use your electronic map?
  • Please tidy your desk.
  • Love me, love my dog.

  • comb, climb, thumb

In reality, love probably goes on with breaks and interruptions. First, it’s difficult to suppose that one can experience anything continuously. Sleep interrupts wakefulness and sleep itself is interrupted by dreams and nightmares. The feeling one has for his lover during wakefulness may be blotted out or intensified by sleep. In either case, the feeling changes.

When one is awake, he cannot fix his eyes for his attention constantly on a single object. He must blink, if nothing else. More likely he will look to something else for variety or from necessity. His mind may turn to the stock market or he may become fascinated by the operation of a pile driver on his way to work. His focus for such of his day is on work. As he closes the door to his office, his thoughts may turn to his love, but sitting at his desk, his eyed fix on the print and figures there.

item.title: Day 12 Speaking
item.description: <h2 id="【P1】梦想职业"><a href="#【P1】梦想职业" class="headerlink"
item.content:encoded:
【P1】梦想职业

中在儿时与现在两个阶段的梦想职业及其变化的过程和原因。选取自己两个阶段的想法，并有一定拓展即可。

What was your dream job when you were a child?

I used to dream of being an astronaut when I was in elementary school. The mysteriously boundless universe never failed to fascinate me I once asked my parents to buy this delicate diorama of the solar system and plenty of sci-fi books.

Have you changed your mind on your dream job?

I have, pathetically. Not until I entered college did I realize that it’s not realistic to strive for the stars. Instead, I chose to study aviation as my major and wanna devote to the aviation industry when I graduate.

What kinds of jobs are popular in China?

Well-paid jobs are popular, obviously. The lawyer and the physician rank the most popular two. Some graduates long to become a civil servant or a teacher too. Stability seems the second crucial factor here.

Have you had a full-time or a part-time job before?

I once was a part-time waiter in a local Chinese restaurant. Not until I got the job did I realize how hard it is to make a good money as a result of the latest recession.

【P1】事件类：省钱

开源节流，省钱是很重要的理财手段。注意总结自己现在和儿时的省钱经历、方法等。

Did you save money when you were young?

I didn’t, even if I wanted to. I mean, I got a weekly allowance as scanty as 2 rmb for my stationery, books and the like. I had to bum some snacks off my classmates back then.

Did you ever give money to other children?

I didn’t give any money to any kids until I was 18 and supposed to send red packets to younger generation. I myself couldn’t save any, what was the point of giving it out?

Do you think parents should teach children to save money?

They surely should. I don’t think any parent would want a prodigal son, cause they never return. Plus, personal financing is such a critical skill for anyone living in this society with high inflation.

Do parents give children pocket money in China?

They do. Many parents give children weekly or monthly allowances in order to guide their kids to save money and spend money in a sensible way. Although it’s wishful thinking sometimes.

【P2】事件类：庆祝重要事件

Describe an important event that you celebrated

  • What it was
  • Where it happened
  • Why it was important
  • How people felt about it
  1. 本题的A是event，描述事件；B是important, that you celebrated, 限制内容。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 介绍这次庆祝的源起、时间地点、准备工作等
    • 详述庆祝的经过
    • 叙述自己和其他参与者的感受，总结
  3. 时态建议：本题应主要使用过去时，讲到该庆祝的规律性事项时使用一般现在时。述及对现在的影响用现在完成时。
  4. 其他：一次庆祝事件用标准的记叙手法展开即可，但要尽量穿插一些细节和人物心理活动描写，避免流水账。

Last month, my brother and I threw our parents a party, a grand function, I would call it,to celebrate their silver jubilee. They actually had turned down the idea when I came it up to them 2 months ago, saying that it was not worth all the hassle. Then yeah, it would be a surprise.

My brother proposed decorating our living room with a retro 90s twist. We affixed cheesy posters of Andy Lau – my mom’s idol in her younger years – to the wall, placed bouquets of lilies – the flowers my dad used to send Mom when they were in college when everything was still up in the air, and so on. I called and invited everyone on their phone book as well.

When they came back home from the date in a fancy restaurant that we urged, everything was in order. I can never forget their faces when they stepped in, holding hands, mouth agape, before my mom complained about her make-up. The best part of that party is when Dad renewed his very wedding vows 25 years before in which he stated how he met Mom in college, how they had their first kiss in front of the class during their graduation trip, how they decided to marry but skip a wedding. I can’t believe it brought tears to my mom’s eyes, once again. Then she begrudged us squandering money on the tedious posters and things, with a broad smile.

【P2】事件类：听说的轶闻

Describe an impressive/memorable story you heard from someone

  • What this story was
  • When you heard about it
  • Who told you this story
  • And explain why you were impressed/memorable by it
  1. 本题的A是story，讲故事；B是impressive/memorable, you heard from someone,限制故事特征和来源。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 做好讲故事的预备，比如从谁听说的，故事起因是什么
    • 详述故事
    • 讲述故事的有趣或令人印象深刻的点
  3. 时态建议：从别人那里听说的过程应当是过去时，而讲故事的部分则需根据故事本身的需要调整时态。
  4. 其他：这是叠加形态的事件类题目，也就是说一定会讲到两个或两个以上时间节点的事情，一定注意时态的运用。另外，不要忽视了这道题的故事特征要求，也就是impressive/memorable，那种没有情节没有转折没有高潮的故事就不要讲了。

I’d like to tell you an anecdote of the eccentric physicist Albert Einstein that I heard from my roommate, a gossipy geek, who’s been a source of news concerning science and technology. But this one is about Einstein, but has nothing to do with physics.

By the 1920s, Albert Einstein was already regarded as the most famous physicist alive. At the end of the year when the brilliant scientist was touring Japan for a lecture series for which he was paid 2,000 pounds by his Japanese publisher, Einstein learned that he had been awarded the Nobel Prize in physics on the way from Europe. News traveled fast and his lectures gathered crowds of thousands of Japanese looking to catch a glimpse of a Nobel laureate in person. One night, exhausted by all the publicity, Einstein was gathering his thoughts in his room at a normal hotel in Tokyo. A messenger knocked on his door with a delivery. But the messenger refused to accept a tip, in line with local practice. Instead, Einstein handed the man two notes, the hotel’s stationery scribbled with his words of wisdom on how to live a happy life.
