The first note simply stated, “a quiet and modest life brings more joy than a pursuit of success bound with constant unrest.” And the second contained a familiar aphorism: “where there’s a will, there’s a way.”

What’s most incredible about the story is that the two notes were sold for $1.8 million combined at a Jerusalem auction house last year. I finally understand why some say Einstein was disappointed by the essence of science and resorted to philosophy in his later years. I mean, look at the words, how cliched and yet powerful!

【P2】事件类：不寻常的一餐

Describe an unforgettable dinner/an unusual meal

  • When the dinner was
  • Where the dinner was
  • Who you dined with
  • And explain why it is unforgettable/unusual
  1. 本题的A是an meal/dinner，描述一餐；B是unforgettable/unusual, 限制特征。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 讲述这一餐的时间地点人物等基本信息，引出难忘、不寻常的特征
    • 详述这一餐的过程
    • 总结这一餐难忘、不寻常的点，升华
  3. 时态建议：过去时，没商量。

  4. 其他：既然是一餐，那免不了要描述吃了什么、聊了什么、做了什么。一个饭局，不能只是吃吃喝喝，还要聊天谈心，甚至还要勾心斗角。把饭局的层次讲出来。

So I was invited to my girlfriend’s apartment for dinner last month, yay! Me and my girlfriend have been dating for 3 years, and that very dinner was unforgettably critical. I was so determined to win over her dad with a finely-tuned display of charm, decorum,machismo and wit. Well, I knew it was not gonna happen, but I got a chance to redeem myself anyway. I got everything ready. I put in the extra time to get that haircut, shaved and pressed my shirt and here we go.

I was welcomed by some sniffing of their Labrador and a slippery carpet, but everything went just fine. Greetings, smiles, firm handshakes. Yeah, that was great. Her mom cooked steamed fish, lovely dim sum and mixed veggies. I complimented on the dishes, sat beside their daughter, and began bonding with them.

We were talking about the music of their choice, some piano pieces of Debussy. I spurted out something about how Impressionism being in-between, then I realized something was wrong before I was reminded by a wink that her dad was an adamant fan of classical music, esp. of French Impressionism, something my girlfriend had told me the previous day. I soon added that Debussy and Ravel were precursors for modern era and things like that. Then her dad just smiled and told me not to worry about it and try less hard to bow and scrape.

I literally breathed a sigh of relief and thought at least we were both classical music buffs and there would be nothing wrong about that.

【每日表达】句型、习语、小幽默

wear someone down

burn someone out

finish someone off

knock yourself out

  • 每天的工作和学习让人劳累不堪。为了自己和家人的未来，拼一拼是值得的。但太大的压力、太多的工作量会让人疲惫不堪、喘不过气来。这四个词组都可以表示这个意思。注意最后一组knock后面只能加反身代词，而其他三组都可以加任意表示人的名词或代词。学习压力大，大家保重身体哦。
  • Stop doing your job like that, or you’ll knock yourself out!
  • With such heavy workloads, I almost burn myself out.
  • Three days of hard work has really finished him off.
  • All the stress and extra travel is beginning to wear him down.

All work and no play makes Jack a dull boy.

  • 这是一句非常好用的谚语，用来说明持续工作没有娱乐的后果。一直工作，没有玩耍，再聪明的人（比如某个Jack）也会变无趣。在使用的时候可以把Jack替换成自己的名字，就会有一种幽默的效果。
  • I really need to get out more often. You know what they say about all work and no play makes Lang a dull boy?

【综合技巧】追加评论与感受，非限定从就足够

  • Young people often shop on Taobao, which is a e-commerce website that has millions of transactions every day. 限制性定语从句
  • In the presence of so many people he was a little tense, which was understandable. 非限制性定语从句
  • Many Chinese people are fond of and are good at playing table tennis, which is quite natural for we win basically all the champions there are every year.
  • My mom claims to be allergic to cats’ fur, which I don’t know whether she’s messing with me or not.
  • My geography teacher was really patient to us, which… I think all teachers should be.
  • I currently get up at 7, do my morning routine exercises, skim the newspapers, feed my cat and go to work. Which was totally unimaginable only 1 year ago when I was still in college.
今日任务：回答下列问题，每题至少使用一次非限定从追加评论或感受。
  • Are teenagers more influenced by their family or their peers?
  • Why should we learn history? (P3)
  • What should you do to prepare for a job interview? (P3)

【纠音跟读】失去爆破1

  • The boy in a re(d) coa(t) was on a bla(ck) bi(ke) jus(t) now
  • An ol(d) lady in the grea(t) bi(g) world
  • What would you like, ho(t) tea or bla(ck) coffee?
  • I bought a chea(p) book, but it’s a goo(d) boo(k) too.
  • Goo(d) morning!
  • Goo(d) luck!

  • I wen(t) there alone a(t) nine las(t) night.

A most curious and useful thing to realize is that one never knows the impression one is creating on other people. One may often guess pretty accurately whether it is good, bad, or indifferent — some people render it unnecessary for one to guess, they practically inform one — but that is not what I mean. I mean much more than that. I mean that one has one’s self no mental picture corresponding to the mental picture which one’s personality leaves in the minds of one’s friends. Has it ever struck you that there is a mysterious individual going around, walking the streets, calling at houses for tea, chatting, laughing, grumbling, arguing, and that all your friends know him and have long since added him up and come to a definite conclusion about him — without saying more than a chance, cautious word to you; and that that person is you? Supposing that you came into a drawing room where you were having tea, do you think you would recognize yourself as an individuality? I think not. You would be apt to say to yourself as guests do when disturbed in drawing rooms by other guests: “Who’s this chap? Seems rather queer. I hope he won’t be a bore.” And yourfirst telling would be slightly hostile. Why, even when you meet yourself in an unsuspected mirror in the very clothes that you have put on that very day and that you know by heart, you are almost always shocked by the realization that you are you. And now and then, when you have gone to the glass to arrange your hair in the full sobriety of early morning, have you not looked on an absolute stranger, and has not that stranger piqued your curiosity? And if it is thus with precise external details of form, colour, and movement, what may it not be with the vague complex effect of the mental and moral individuality?

item.title: Day 11 Speaking
item.description: 【P1】通讯类：信
item.content:encoded:
【P1】通讯类：信

Do you write many letters?

You probably won’t believe me, but I’ve never written any letters in my life and I don’t think I ever will. I send a lot of emails but not traditional hand-written letters, no.

Do you prefer to write letters by hand or use a computer?

Definitely use a computer. If you own a computer, I will suggest you do the same. Writing letters by hand is unproductive and relatively more expensive. Not to mention no one could ever decipher my scrawl.

What do you usually write about?

I write about my latest life to my mom, my academic problems to my advisor, some strong language to spam, all by email.

Is it hard to think of what to write?

As a perfectionist, I would say, yes. A grammatical lapse, a bad word choice, and most of all, spelling mistakes, ewww, all drive me crazy. And that’s why I always revisewhatever I write.

How do you feel when you receive a letter?

Subjunctive mood, please. Since I haven’t received a letter for a million years, I might feel scared, to be honest. What if there’s a letter bomb in it?

Do you think people will still write letters in the future?

Well, we may stick to hand-written letters on negligible occasions, like, I mean, who would wanna receive a love email, right? When you wanna show your sincerity, your affections and your emotions, you’d better use your decrepit pen once again.

【P2】事件类：与老亲人做事

Describe something you enjoy doing with an old person in your family.

  • What it is
  • How often you do it
  • Who you do it with
  • Why you enjoy doing it with this person
  1. 本题的A是something，描述事物；B是you enjoy doing with an old person in your family, 限制内容。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 介绍这件事及其特征，引出与老亲人做
    • 详述做这件事通常的时间、地点、频次，也可以讲一次具体经历
    • 讲述enjoy的表现和原因，收尾
  3. 时态建议：这道题一定是一般现在时为主，因为you enjoy doing，要讲成一个routine。但不代表不能讲过去的某一次，还是可以的，讲的时候用过去时。
  4. 其他：本题千万不能一来就讲与这位老亲人某次做这件事的经历，讲得再精彩也没用。一定要从routine讲起，说成一件经常（或规律性）在一起做的事。然后为了串题方便，还是可以讲某一次。

I really enjoy teaching my grandma how to use her iPhone I bought her for her 70’s birthday. We’ve been doing it on and off for a year now, and she’s already been familiar with sending voice messages via WeChat, the most popular instant messaging app in China with 10 billion users around the world. And next up, I’m gonna teach her to use mapping apps to find her way back home from the park she goes to to practice her Erhu. And my goal is to transform her from a mobile newbie to a smartphone expert!

It’s sort of a routine now, since my parents and I go to her place on last Sunday of each month and have a fancy meal together. After dinner, my mom is in charge of cleaning the pots and pans which is supposed to be my job but I shirk it and resort to helping granny with her new phone. It works all the time. Seeing my grandma and me having fun bonding makes my mom shrug and straight to the kitchen. So, back to Grandma. I always ease her in by doing something she does with her old flip phone, like making a phone call, add a contact and things. Of course, she’s too old to read even the biggest fonts. That’s why I gave up text messages and turned to voice ones.

It can be frustrating sometimes like the time I taught her to send voice messages. I explained how to send one by clicking the green icon, finding my contact, holding the small virtual button on bottom, speaking and releasing it. Baby steps, I thought, would definitely do the trick. But she would always miss a step or two. I was ready to give up when I suddenly received her first 5s message in which she reminded me to put on more clothes cause it’s getting colder.

Turned out she wanted to learn it in order to take better care of me and our family. I felt guilty and so determined that I would spend more time with her with more patience.

【P2】事件类：优质服务

Describe an occasion where you received a good service (from a company or shop)

  • What the service was
  • When and where it happened
  • Whom you were together with
  • How you felt about it
  • And explain why you think it was good service
  1. 本题的A是an occasion，描述一个场合；B是where you received a good service, 限制内容。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 记叙文，开门见山讲事情的时间、地点、人物
    • 详述接受好服务的经过
    • 简述内心感受，可升华
  3. 时态建议：这道题一定是一般过去时，但同时也要注意时态的灵活性。如果说到与去其他店（公司）的对比，那么应该用过去完成时。讲对好服务的一般性评价时，用一般现在时和现在完成时。
  4. 其他：本体是比较简单的记叙文，直接以记叙文的时间地点人物起因经过结果的公式展开叙述即可。当然，好的故事是一定有转折有亮点的。

Well, to be honest, almost everybody has experienced this acute frustration that comes with lousy service. We navigate endless phone menus while in hope of talking to a human. The cable guy who’s supposed to show up between noon and 4 p.m. is late. We sit at a restaurant with a bowl of soup in front of us, but there’s no spoon in sight. And sometimes you will be like, is good service really this rare? Well, it is. But I did experience superb service just last week.

So my little gang and me went to this Italian restaurant near to the campus. It is small but cozy, not at all swanky, and completely busy. We were totally amazed by how wonderful the dishes were, the Panzanella salad, Bruschetta, Focaccia and Carbonara were alluring and palatable. The chef sent out a miniature cornucopia of desserts to our table on the house. It wasn’t big, but did include miscellaneous fruits and nuts.

But what impressed us as much as the generous gift was that, at the start of the meal, a vegetarian roommate asked about meat-free offerings. When those sweets arrived at meal’s end, the server subtly pointed out which dessert was made with gelatin to the meat-eschewing diner. We all felt taken care of, our needs anticipated before we had to voice them.

In all, we were all warm and snug in the chilled atmosphere, eating nice food and served by smiley waiters. No wonder we’ve become regulars.

【P2】事件类：未来旅行计划

Describe a trip that you plan to go on in the near future.

  • Where this place is
  • Who you would like to go with
  • What would you do there
  • And explain why you would like to go there
  1. 本题的A是a trip，描述旅程；B是that you plan to go on in the near future, 限制时间。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 介绍旅程的目的地、时间、跟谁去等基本信息
    • 详述旅程计划的过程、原因以及要做的事，突出特征
    • 简述感受，发表感叹，适度升华
  3. 时态建议：本题时态需要特别注意。Plan to go on in the near future, 既可以用一般现在时讲述“计划”的内容，也可以用将来时或虚拟语气描述这场旅行。
  4. 其他：本题的重点是描述这一trip，一定要注意时态，不是过去时。尤其想串题的话，不要和已经发生过的trips搞混了，当然旅程基本信息仍然能串，但必须改动时态和叙述方式。另外，凡计划的制定，一定有其根据，讲的时候一定要把想法的来源说明白。注意，trip不一定是旅游。

So, I plan to go on a trip to Shandong province, one coastal province in the East of China. As so you know, I was invited to a national summit for videobloggers to Jinan, the capital of Shandong province. It will be a 5-day trip next September, and I’m thinking the first 2 days for the meeting, and the last three, well, I’ll have some fun. A visit to the renowned Mount Tai seems nice.

I would like to go with 2 other excellent vloggers in Beijing, each owning a fantastic account on Weibo, the Chinese equivalent of Twitter. For the summit, I need to prepare for a 10-minute presentation, introducing the development of my account, playing some of my sample videos and sharing my experience on video-shooting and editing. Which will be, well, dull enough, right? I will be sitting in the conference room, or in the audience seats whatever, twiddling with my pen, thinking about what I will go see for the other half of that trip.

Trust me, I’ve already done the elaboration of my itinerary for the other 3 days. Such a shame that I’ve never been to the most prestigious mountain in here, which bears both magnificent natural and cultural significance. What I’m looking forward to the most is the legendary sunrise on the top. I heard from friends that come from Shandong that The best spot for watching the sunrise is Sun-viewing Peak, southeast of Jade Emperor Peak. Waiting for sunrise can be a little boring, but the wait is worth it when you see the glorious sun peaking over the mountains. Just the thought of it makes me zealous and chilled.

In all, I’m so gonna wind down during the trip and I can’t wait.

【每日表达】句型、习语、小幽默

full steam ahead

at full tilt

  • 这是英语中表示速度快的非常有意思的两个相近的表达法，都表示全力以赴，冲刺。为什么说有趣呢，因为它们在表示速度的同时，还表示竭尽全力，用尽力气的意思。在咱们学习英语的过程中，有时候真的需要给自己打打鸡血，看看自己的能力究竟有没有上限。Full steam ahead既可以用作定语，也可以用作状语哦。
  • We’ll have to go full steam ahead if we want to get this done by the deadline!
  • Now that obstacle is out of the way, it’s full steam ahead to get the job finished.
  • Public transportation in my hometown has been developing at full tilt.

anything but

all but

  • 这是一对比较考验逻辑思维的习语，但表示的意思都非常简单。记住公式：anything but = not at all. all but = almost. 仔细分析一下它们的内在逻辑哦。
  • Without you the job would’ve been all but impossible.
  • Though he said he was happy, he looked anything but.
  • anything but… 是“除…之外任何事（物）都…， 根本不…”－－就是我一点都不快乐 nothing but…只有， 只不过； 无非－－－就是我很快乐

【P3技巧】常识类与列举类

基本信息类 Giving basic information

  • 按P1思路回答

常识类 Evoking common sense

  • 按实际情况回答，不逞强，注意现在时态
  • Do Chinese people eat special food in traditional festivals?
  • Is American fast food popular in China?
  • When do Chinese kids start to learn English?

列举类 Enumerating items

  • 按照题目要求列举3个以上（可分类别，也可不分类别），然后详述其中1到2项
  • What wedding gifts are popular in China?
  • What kinds of clothes do people like to wear on formal occasions?
  • What kinds of books can boost people’s imagination?
  • What types of people become famous?
  • What kinds of special food do Chinese eat in traditional festivals?

今日任务：回答下列P3问题，每道题不低于40秒。

  1. Is American fast food popular in China?
  2. Do parents in China allow their children to make important decisions about their future?
  3. What wedding gifts are popular in China?
    1. bedclothes and tableware
  4. What are some examples of electronic or electrical equipment used for communication?

【纠音跟读】连读与变音2

/t/ + /j/ = /ʧ/

  • Don’t you like it?
  • You have come back, haven’t you?
  • Is that your final answer?
  • Actually
  • Natural
  • Congratulations!

/d/ + /j/ = /ʤ/

  • Did you see that?
  • Where did you go?
  • We followed your instructions.
  • Education
  • Individual
  • Gradually

/s/ + /j/ = /ʃ/

  • Am I the only one who’s late here? Yes, youare.
  • You need to pass your test this year.
  • I’m not gonna guess your age.
  • I miss you.
  • Bless you.
  • Insurance

/z/ + /j/ = /ʒ/

  • How’s your family?
  • How was your trip?
  • When’s your birthday?
  • He says you’re ok.
  • Who does your hair?
  • Casual
  • Usually
  • Version

  • Television

If we examine the question from a distance, as if we were viewing the whole country from a long way off,we start to get a clue about what it is that lures us into the big cities.The main point to notice about big cities is that they are big: there are a lot of people,and there are a lot of things going on. If you look down on a city, literally from a great distance,from an airplane at night, you will be struck by the incredible brightness of a city:there are so many lights that you cannot help feeling that all the bright things of life are down there waiting for you.But a feeling of disappointment will set in shortly after you land,because you will discover as you drive into the city center from theairport that the lights are just that:lights, miles and miles of street lights and neon signs.They are notin themselves sources of joy and happiness: city lights are not friendly, they are merely lights.In fact, the effect will probably be to make you feel lonely and isolated.

item.title: Day 10 Speaking
item.description: <h2 id="【P1】人物类：名人"><a href="#【P1】人物类：名人" class="headerlink"
item.content:encoded:
【P1】人物类：名人

一定注意对名气名声的利弊讨论、对名人的态度、名人的社会责任等。

Who is your favorite celebrity in your country?

My favorite celebrity has gotta be Jin Xing, the most illustrious杰出的 ballerina and talk show hostess in China. She was one of the first few transgender women in China to receive the government’s approval to undergo a sex change. I mean, in the 1990s. That alone is inspiring, right?

In general, how do people become famous?

In this information age, people become famous in all sorts of ways. Traditional path is still available. You become famous when you have some special knacks for singing, dancing, performing, and you find the perfect platform on which you shine. Then, don’t be surprised when you hit the stardom after simply uploading some hilarious videos of you and your friends having fun.

Do you think, to be famous, a person needs to have some special talent or abilities?

Well, you still remember the good old days when people needed special talent to get famous, right? Well, they’re gone. In this information age, many people mysteriously become famous by all the hype overnight.

Would you want to be a celebrity?

Nah. I mean, what if I change my mind and wanna lead an ascetic苦行僧 life when I turn 40? Plus, I don’t want my life to be pried too much by paparazzi or inquisitive people.

【P2】事件类：街市购物

Describe a time that you were shopping in a street market

  • When it was
  • Where you were
  • What you bought
  • How you felt about the shopping experience
  1. 本题的A是a time，描述经历；B是you were shopping in a street market, 限制内容。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 可选择直接用时间顺序讲故事，时间地点、开端、起因
    • 详述购物的过程，注意不要变流水账，抓住特征，比如购物的心得、砍价经历、战利品等
    • 简述感受，发表感叹，适度升华
  3. 时态建议：讲故事用过去时，述及购物对现在的影响时可用现在完成时。描述街市基本情况应用一般现在时。
  4. 其他：本题比较单纯可爱，用时间顺序讲一次购物经历即可。注意不要讲成流水账，突出一两个购物中的亮点，比如心得、砍价、战利品、心情变化等。

Well, first of all, I love street markets. They have been centers of commerce for centuries. Even in the age of Walmart and Amazon, these casual retail spaces, usually populated by small, independent vendors, are thriving in tons of parts of the world and are being revisited as viable places to shop in others.

My experience of eating shopping in Shilin Night Market of Taipei is superb. Shilin Night Market is the largest of Taipei’s famous night markets. It is best known for its gigantic food court. Independent vendors sell their specialties in a virtual feeding frenzy, and many locals and visitors consider this one of the best places to eat in all of Taiwan. I was dazzled by neon lights the minute I walked in. grilled squids, fried chicken, pigs blood cakes and other snack stalls were on both sides in order. Trust me, there’s a long queue in front of every single one of them, making the narrow street even more cramped. Let alone the lively bustle and hustle of pedestrians chewing various foods they’d bought and sauntered漫步 along. I soon found myself holding the renowned fried chicken in my right hand and fries in my left.

The best part of it was how I bought a beautiful shirt in one of the stores there with 50% off. So I entered this fashion store and soon couldn’t take my eyes off a red flannel shirt. The owner approached with a smile introducing how they got it with effort from Korea and how there was only one piece of its kind. I asked him to knock a few bucks off before he said a firm no. then I told him that I came from the mainland, which turned out to be a good strategy, since he smiled even more broadly and said yes immediately.

I don’t haggle／bargain a lot, but that felt awesome, to be honest.

【P2】事件类：不得不礼貌

Describe a situation where you had to be polite

  • What it is
  • What you did to be polite
  • Who were there
  • Why you had to be polite
  • And explain how you felt about it afterwards
  1. 本题的A是a situation，描述事件；B是where you had to be polite，限制事件内容。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 先介绍该事件的基本情况（时间、地点、人物），引出需礼貌的原因
    • 按时间顺序讲述事件，要有细节有内心活动
    • 总结事件带来的感受
  3. 时态建议：描述事件基本信息时用一般现在时，紧跟着讲故事用过去时，故事中涉及现在仍未改变的规律性动作时用一般现在时。内心活动及人物对话若用直接引语，用一般现在时。总结感受时用过去时、一般现在时、现在完成时。
  4. 其他：也可以略过提纲a)，直接开门见山讲故事，注意全程用过去时串起来。讲故事讲究细节，所以不要忽略对人物内心活动、动作神态等的描写。不描写事件，只叙述经过也符合题意，但注定得不到高分。另外，讲故事尽量少用直接引语，不符合考官对“即兴”的认知，改为间接引语更合适。

I will describe a miserable experience in a supposedly pleasant dinner. It was a rather formal one to which my dad was invited to conduct his business at work, about his new construction project or something. It has nothing to do with me, right? Well, that’s what I was thinking, until he dragged me to it and asked me a favor. Traditionally, in a Chinese dinner, people urge others to drink, esp. when talking about business. To me It’s never a good idea to consume alcohol during a business meal. The purpose of the meeting is to conduct business, and at the very least alcohol will slow down or cloud the thought process. And my dad needed me to help him decline possible requests.

I got this, I thought to myself. But I was wrong. That dinner was a total debacle崩溃. Despite the fact that I despise social etiquette礼仪, I tried my best to be polite from initial greetings, handshakes, to the end when dad and I lurched out drunk. My smile gradually dwindled away as more and more beer and baijiu were served. They just paid no heed to my warning about how my dad would get a buzz after like only two glasses of wine and made him drink incredibly much. To add insult to injury, they made me drink too. I was fighting back the urge to throw a tantrum and stand up for my dad when he sneakily put an arm on my legs telling me not to.

What the heck was wrong with those people? I complained it to my dad the next day when we were both sober. And my dad gave me a grin and told me that this was adult life, and it sucks, but I had to acclimate myself to it. What a bummer!

【P2】事件类：参加派对

Describe a party that you joined

  • When it took place
  • Where it was
  • Whose party it was
  • What you did there
  • And how you felt about the party
  1. 本题的A是a party，描述经历；B是you joined, 限制内容。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 可选择直接用时间顺序讲故事，时间地点、开端、起因
    • 详述派对的过程，注意不要变流水账，抓住特征
    • 简述感受，发表感叹，适度升华
  3. 时态建议：讲故事用过去时，述及派对对现在的影响时可用现在完成时
  4. 其他：本题比较单纯可爱，用时间顺序讲一个派对就完了。注意不要讲成流水账，把点变少、变大。

I’d like to tell you about a special birthday party I joined this March. So my best friend Jerry who was my roommate back in college called me the other day inviting me to sleep over. I was like, what am I, 12? He laughed and said it would be a blackout sleepover party for his 24th birthday. I thought it was a code for pajamas. Then I wore one, took my present for him and went straight to his apartment.

Well, he meant it. I got there a little early and bumped into his clumsiness of preparations. So I gave him a hand, moved all the furniture out of the way and threw some blankets and pillows on the ground. 20 minutes later, people came, scrambling to sit near Jerry. Alright, so he’s apparently a little bit more popular than I am. We turned off all the lights, computers and TV, and lit up the room using candles. We sat together in a circle, played some music, enjoying drinks and snacks with interesting conversations, fun games and a healthy dose of flirting. It really brought me back to a simpler time, that carefree畅快 childhood which helped minimize stress and allowed me to just laugh and have a great time without worrying about anything.

The honesty in the air made it possible for everyone to confess, of our own volition意志. To our surprise, Eric, the other roommate of ours gave an affectionate monologue in the dark and admitted that he had the hots for Amy, our beautiful monitor back then. Which made us so excited, ecstatic欣喜若狂 actually because we all knew his secret crush all the way through.

Guess what? I really crashed on his couch that night, cause I was hopelessly drunk.

【每日表达】句型、习语、小幽默

rude awakening

  • 有没有这样的经历？自己盲目自信能做好一件事，赢得一个比赛，赢得一个人，结果到见分晓的那一刻才幡然醒悟，原来自己得不到。这种幡然醒悟就叫rude awakening. 搭配have或be in for a rude awakening.
  • He’d thought this would be a cushy job, but then he was in for a rude awakening when he really did it.

a dime a dozen

  • 这个习语是我们中文讲的“烂大街”最好的对应，说一个东西、一种人、一个现象满街都是，一点都不稀奇。在十九世纪的美国，常见的日用品比如苹果鸡蛋牙刷，它们都是a dime a dozen，用一个dime就能买一打。现在引申为比喻那些因为太多而价值不高的东西。
  • Unlike 20 years ago, American fast-food restaurants are a dime a dozen now.
  • The so-called experts are a dime a dozen.

今日任务：回答下列题目，开脑洞，运用以上表达法

  • Do you have any bad experiences of trying new food?
  • Do people still wear watches in your country?

【综合技巧】用填充词提升流畅度

  • ok, so
  • well
  • kinda/sorta
  • or something
  • uh um
  • like
  • ya’know
  • [Ok, so] I bumped into a legit public account the other day. I get to learn [like] tons of useful tips on English speaking there. [Ya’know], just when I thought I might have to resort to the new orient, which I [kinda] hate for its high price. [well], I might as well ask Lang for more suggestions [or something].
今日任务：
  1. 给这个段落加上fillers，并朗读
  • I bet anyone who’s familiar with electronic gadgets would go with an electronic one. Turning to your mapping apps are anything but a hassle. All you need to do is to enter the place you’re heading to, and the recommended routes with the fewest traffic lights and lightest traffic come out. How magical is that!
  1. 回答以下问题，注意在自己卡壳的地方加上fillers，并达到自然的状态
  • How do you feel when you receive a letter?
  • Do you think people these days are as punctual as they were in the past?
  • What events do Chinese people like to celebrate? (P3)

【纠音跟读】连读与变音1

  • I’ve been working > on > it.
  • He called > you half > an > hour >ago.
  • Pick > it > up, please.

  • I looked for > it here > and there.

  • Here > are four > eggs > in the fridge.

  • How > and why on > earth did > you come here?

  • Are you Chinese? I > am.

  • He > is very > attractive to me.

But putting parables aside, I am unable to understand how any one with a knowledge of mankind can imagine that the growth of science can threaten the development of art in any of its forms. If I understand the matter at all, science and art are the obverse and reverse of Nature’s medal; the one expressing the external order of things, in terms of feeling, the other in terms of thought. When men no longer love nor hate; when suffering causes no pity, and the tale of great deeds ceases to thrill, when the lily of the field shall seem no longer more beautifully arrayed than Solomon in all his glory, and the awe has vanished from the snow-capped peak and deep ravine, then indeed science may have the world to itself, but it will not be because the monster has devoured the art, but because one side of human nature is dead, and because men have lost the half of their ancient and present attributes.

item.title: Day 9 Speaking
item.description: 【P1】想象类：（太空）旅行
item.content:encoded:
【P1】想象类：（太空）旅行

Do you want to travel to the outer space?

I mean, duh. Who wouldn’t wanna have that once-in-a-lifetime experience, right? I would be ecstatic欣喜若狂的 to see the breathtaking milky way.

What would you do if you had that opportunity?

I would grasp it and make the best of it. I might figure out how to post a geo-tagged地理标志 tweet there. No one has done it before, right?

Do you think it’s necessary to see other planets?

I actually don’t. we’ve only got this one earth which we should protect and love. Rather than resorting to other habitable planets which we haven’t found yet, we might as well take good care of this one.

When was the last time you went traveling?

It was last summer when I went on a boat trip to Hainan, a tropical island in the South of China. I got to see the breathtaking sunrise on the deck of the cruise ship. it was such a once-in-a-lifetime experience, wasn’t it?

Do you like to travel by air?

Not really. The greatest dilemma in the world is between comfy but costly first class and affordable but narrow economy. Not the case were you a millionaire, of course.

【P2】事件类：特殊衣服

Describe a time you wore a type of clothes for a special occasion (describe something you wear in a special occasion)

  • What the occasion was
  • What you wore
  • Where you bought the clothes
  • And explain what other people at the occasion thought of your clothes
  1. 本题的A是a time，描述经历；B是you wore a type of clothes for a special occasion, 限制内容。实质上是经历+物件类题目
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 可选择直接用时间顺序讲故事，时间地点、开端、起因
    • 详述穿这件衣服的场合、过程，描述这件衣服
    • 简述感受、他人评价，发表感叹，适度升华
  3. 时态建议：讲故事用过去时，描述衣服本身可选用一般现在时。最后述及感受和影响可用现在完成时。
  4. 其他：本题的另一版本在括号中，注意这个版本的时态为现在时，即这件衣服不能只在某个场所穿一次，而应该在特殊场合都穿。因此描述的时候不能只讲故事，还要把事件用现在时讲成时常发生的。

You know how nutty as a fruitcake we used to be when we just entered college and finally got rid of our folks who kept pestering纠缠 us on our looks and outfits, right? Well,turned out I went completely overboard the first day in college. I joined a rock music club, the only one of its kind in my school back then and I was invited to the welcome party. Well, of course I needed to spruce myself up a little bit to impress people. And that was why I bought this black leather jacket in the nearby mall with rivets on the shoulder. It’s got this fully lined interior with one snap-closed pocket and one zippered pocket. A rock star, I murmured to myself in front of the mirror. I got this.

Then I strutted through the door of the bar wearing a crooked cocky smile with my hands in pockets. And that slim-fit jacket glittered. I thought I was the spotlight that night. But things soon went wrong. Instead of getting supposed cheers from the crowd, I was scalded by weird looks and whispers. The performers shouting Linkin Park on stage were staring at me moving closer. And the cute guitarist even winked at me. That’s right, I felt like a rock star now.

After the performances, I felt nervous to mingle with people I didn’t know, but I did it anyway. Most of freshmen were kind enough not to comment on my jacket but their occasional glances sold them out. That was when I decided to take it off with a sigh and fit in better.

【P2】事件类：朋友做过你没做

Describe something interesting that your friend has done but you haven’t

  • What it was
  • Where your friend did it
  • When your friend did it
  • And explain why it was interesting
  1. 本题的A是something，描述事物；B是your friends has done but you haven’t, 限制内容。本题为事物+事件的结合。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 介绍这件事物及其特征
    • 详述朋友参加的经历、有趣的地方
    • 简述感受，发表感叹，适度升华
  3. 时态建议：介绍事物本身可用一般现在时，介绍朋友经历时用过去时，转而说为何有趣时可用一般现在时。
  4. 其他：此题要求的事件经历描述方式是转述，一定注意要时不时安插间接引语标志，提醒考官你仍在转述，而不要用直接引语。

Have you ever heard of hashtag YOLO, an acronym for you only live once which is a call to live life to its fullest extent, even embracing behavior which carries inherent risk? Well my friend Jimmy is a believer. He’s an amateur extreme sports athlete who hasbeen through a lot in life. Trust me, there were cases in which he got on the brink ofdeath and then survived.

So I was not at all surprised when he told me he’d tried hang gliding the other day. He used the stimulating word defying gravity when talking about the sport and his experience. He told me that it’s beyond imagination to harness the power of nature to soar miles across the open countryside, at heights of up to 1,000 meters and speeds of over 20 mph with a hang glider. It’s like using the same naturally occurring currents of air that birds use to fly, and you can take to the skies and enjoy this exhilarating experience, no matter what your age, physical ability or background. He joined a gliding club in Shanghai and had his first try-out 3 days later. He said it felt like heaven flying in the sky. This kind of floating felt exactly like a dream too, one where he was flying above treetops, getting a glimpse of how beautiful life can be. Anytime he got a ripple of wind it was like soaring up in a giant swing.

Well of course I missed the technical part cause it was full of jargons that I could never remember, but I so lusted after it hearing his descriptions alone. I’ve never thought about trying any extreme sport myself, for when it comes to sports, it’s always safe better than sorry, right? But maybe in 5 years I will change. I don’t know.

【P2】事件类：帮助专心

Describe something you do to keep yourself concentrated in work/study.

  • What it is
  • How often you do it
  • When you started doing it
  • And explain why it helped yourself concentrate
  1. 本题的A是something，描述事物；B是you do to keep yourself concentrated in work/study, 限制内容。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 介绍这件事物及其特征
    • 详述使用经过、时间、好处
    • 简述感受，发表感叹，适度升华
  3. 时态建议：介绍事物本身可用一般现在时，介绍经历时用过去时，转而说为何有用可用一般现在时。
  4. 其他：任何地方的题库tips可能都不完全准确。此题题干为现在时，但tips最后一条为过去时，应为谬误。如果真遇到此情况，那么可以只说描述中故事那一次给自己带来的帮助。

I’d like to describe a simple but rather cool productivity method, the Pomodoro Technique, aka tomato method, invented by some Italian, which helps one power through distractions, hyper-focus, and get things done in short bursts, while taking frequent breaks to come up for air and relax. Interestingly, this method got its name after a tomato timer the inventor used as a college student.

Here’s how it works. To implement it, all you’ll need is a timer. Beyond that, there are no special apps, books, or tools required. Choose a task to be accomplished,

set the timer to 25 minutes, work on the task until the timer rings, take a short break for like 5 minutes, and after 4 intervals take a longer break. That’s it. Pomodoro is a cyclical system. You work in short sprints, which makes sure you’re consistently productive. You also get to take regular breaks that bolster your motivation and keep you creative.

Concentration had been so hard for me until I read about this technique 6 years ago and soon realized that it wasn’t my fault. Turned out it’s against human nature to focus on a certain task for more than 25 minutes, right? So back then I was working on my new book and the last half proved a lot easier when I resorted to this magical method.

What’s more interesting about it is that there actually is a website tomato-timer.com on which you have this set timer for 25 minutes. Push the start, stop and reset button just like any other timer and you get it! Not surprisingly, there’s this Youtuber who shared her 2 hours of study process using this method and apparently 1m viewers follow suit. It’s a cult, isn’t it?

【P3技巧】讲故事与讲道理

Story-Telling (concrete examples, life experiences, historical events, imaginations, jokes…)

Vividness is the key

Appropriate tense and mood (did, do, have done, will do, would do…)

Chronological (when, then…)

Practice!

Reasoning (abstract examples, reasons, ideas, speculations, comparisons…)

Logic is the key

Introduction + Body (+Conclusion)

Paralleling, Comparison and Analogy

Support: Phenomena, Reports, Research and data, Personal experiences, Stories of celebrities/historical figures…

What is the difference of traveling with your family members and traveling alone?

下面我提供两种路线的答案。看看他们有什么不同哦

ST: Traveling with your family members is just so much fun, esp. for a family person myself. I can’t live without them! Just like last September when I went on a ferry trip with my family. I couldn’t forget the breathtaking sunrise we appreciated on the first day together on the deck. We were standing abreast, smiling and mimicking seagull’s cry. And we took a selfie of us and those ivory birds. If I had gone there on my own, I would’ve felt so lonely. I mean, who would be there to make fun of my crack of voice?

R: Traveling alone is my cup of tea and something I do at least one time a year. Firstly, you get to carry out your own plan or itinerary and don’t have to compromise. I mean, they’re your family, what if they’re not happy to go to the Met but prefer to picnic in the Central Park? Just be ready to give up your paintings and sculptures. Secondly, traveling alone, you can alter your plan whenever you want. When you don’t feel like going out someday in a journey, just indulge yourself a stay-in at the hotel. Finally, some may say that you will be lonely traveling alone. Well, you may be alone, but you can’t be lonely. Come on, this is the so-called information age, and you can always post the 10-s video of breathtaking sunrise on your Moments and get as many likes as you want, right?

今日任务：回答下列P3问题，分别走叙事和议论路线。（无论人设，都试试看。括号内为P3题型分类，先不管，以后会讲到）

  1. If you were a parent and busy at work, would you take care of your children on your own or hire a babysitter? (Imagining situations)
  2. How should we encourage children to help others? (Providing solutions)

【每日表达】句型、习语、小幽默

better safe than sorry

极限运动敢玩吗？对小部分富有冒险精神的人来说，当然敢。人生就是拿来冒险的，毕竟不去好好体验人生怎么算活过一回呢？但我相信对大部分人来说，还是安安全全的比较重要。那么，我们可以用better safe than sorry来形容这种心理状态。宁愿不那么有趣好玩，宁愿抱憾，也不去冒险，省得出事。

When it comes to sports, it’s always better safe than sorry, right?

not/never in a million years

Hyperbole（夸张）是英文最常见的修辞手法之一，有很多表达法都极其夸张、不符实际，但很好地表达出其中包含的实意。比如not/never in a million years，讲某个事情不可能发生，讲说它一百万年都不可能。这也是常见的表示拒绝和表示否定的表达法。这在英美人士的口语常见句型中起码能排进前50，然而咱却说得很少。学起来吧！

  • when will you consider marrying me?
  • Never in a million years.

【纠音跟读】Pitch Words

I prefer to study in the morning when most people at my age are still in their sweet dreams in the morning, right? I get to breathe in fresh and brisk air and enjoy mild temperature. besides, there are not so many distractions in the morning so that I canconcentrate harder and be more productive.

体会一下把这些词做成pitch words，用改变声调、拖长以及重读的方式，对表达有什么帮助吧。为什么要选这些词呢？选词有没有规则呢？

今日任务：

  1. 朗读以下句子，判断pitch words并使用改变声调、拖长、重读的方式体现出来。
  • The members in the society can get news easily from News Channels and thus keep themselves informed of what’s happening around.
  • Once, I attended a group discussion. Many people were not even discussing. They were just talking about NBA games. When it ended, I got nothing from it.
  • With many scientific databases, we can always find what we need by title, author or even content.
  • I enjoy biography most, because reading biography tells me a life story of a great person.
  • After understanding the things we don’t know previously, we improve.

（提示：pitch words可以为：easily, informed, group discussion, not, NBA games, nothing, always, title, author, content, biography, life story, previously, improve）

  1. 回答下列问题，注意判断出自己答案中的pitch words，并体现出来
  • Do you think people will still write letters in the future?
  • What happened on your first day of high school?

跟读

音频第一遍为美式，第二遍为英式。注意录音中的单词读音、重音、连读、失去爆破、变音、弱读、语调及节奏感。

Yet before the floating impress of the woods could clear itself, suddenly the gladsome light leaped over hill and valley, casting amber, blue, and purple, and a tint of rich red rose, according to the scene they lit on, and the curtain flung around; yet all alike dispelling fear and the cloven hoof of darkness, all on the wings of hope advancing, and proclaiming, “God is here!” Then life and joy sprang reassured from every crouching hollow; every flower and bud and bird had a fluttering sense of them, and all the flashing of God’s gaze merged into soft beneficence.

item.title: Day 8 Speaking
item.description: <h2 id="【P1】人物类：老师"><a href="#【P1】人物类：老师" class="headerlink"
item.content:encoded:
【P1】人物类：老师

中在好老师的特质、不同老师的对比等，既要准备general的话题，又要储备一个具体老师的描述。

Do you have a favorite teacher?

I do indeed. My favorite teacher is my anthropology teacher back in university who is not only the most professional teacher, but also the most charismatic and inspiring person I’ve ever met.

Can you describe one of your high school teachers?

Ok, so I’ll talk about my Chinese teacher Mr. Xie, a middle-aged man who always encouraged us to emulate each other. And also, he kept telling us never lose our bearings in life, no matter what. Look at that!

What kinds of teacher do you like?

I like teachers with erudition. I mean, an excellent teacher should not only be proficientat what they teach, right? What’s more, a passionate teacher sounds nice, too.

What’s the difference between young and old teachers?

Come to think of it, young teachers are often more passionate, dynamic and more likely to embrace new teaching methods like multimedia equipment and things while old teachers are more experienced and usually more proficient.

Do you think teachers should be angry at students or not?

Well, teachers are only human, and they have every right not to be a happy bunny sometimes. But the thing is, I don’t think they should ever show their anger to a student. Trust me, that would be counter-productive适得其反.

Do you want to be a teacher in the future?

I’ve dreamed of being a teacher someday. Look at me, I have what it takes to be a teacher, right? I’m an eloquent speaker, a patient caretaker, and a charismatic leader.

Do you like strict teachers?

I don’t. they freak me out吓我一跳. A negligible lapse失误 may piss them off and then my curiosity may be extinguished. Plus, I never get along with one. Period.

【P2】事件类：快乐时光

Describe a happy experience you had before

  • Where you were
  • When it happened
  • Who you were with
  • What you did
  • And explain why you felt happy
  1. 本题的A是an experience，描述经历；B是happy, you had before, 限制属性和内容。这是最古老的P2题目之一。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 按时间顺序讲述，先讲时间、地点、人物、起因，引出开心
    • 详述事件经过、结果
    • 事件带来的感受，适当升华
  3. 时态建议：过去时
  4. 其他：抽到本题算是口语考试中最幸运的事了吧，实在有太多可以讲的可以串的。要把情绪带起来，把开心表达出来哦。另外，精彩的故事离不了细节，动作神态、环境描述、内心动态，择其一，说出来。

Reminiscence怀旧 once in a while is not a bad thing. A lot of quotidian每日的 things and experiences, like the rain, or a particular song, or a unique smell has the power to take us back to a time when we related these experiences and things to something else. And I sometimes find myself sitting back and reminiscing about falling in love the first time.

People keep talking about how shallow puppy love is, but I’m pretty sure that I was in love at first sight. So it was the orientation day of high school and a scorching summer day. I was fiddling with the piano right in the corner of the teaching building lobby. I was a mediocre player back then and played a little Chopin, clumsily. But no one was listening, so I didn’t care. Then I saw a reflection on the upright lid looming large. It was a girl with her black hair flowing over her shoulders. I was getting a little intense and electricity which got the best of my fingers. I botched the Nocturne. Sorry Chopin.

Turned out she would be my classmate for the next three years and coincidentally a better piano player. She sat on the stool with her back straightened up and began the miraculous神奇的 Fantasie幻想曲 Impromptu即兴. Beautiful Andante was flowing in the air, like her hair, and like my heart.

Soon we clicked and spent a wonderful time together for a whole afternoon. We talked about everything in our life, how she went through the monotonously tough time practicing, and how I picked up the guitar and forgot my lyrics the first time on stage. What a terrific start of school! We felt so connected that we even forgot to deal with the register and formalities. But it was totally worth it!

Although it didn’t work out for us, it was a happy experience that I would remember for the rest of my life.

【P2】事件类：想参加比赛

Describe a competition (talent show, etc) you want to take part in

  • What the competition is
  • How you knew it
  • What you need to prepare for it
  • And explain why you want to attend it
  1. 本题的A是a competition，描述比赛；B是you want to take part in, 限制内容。本题为介绍+期许的结合。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 介绍比赛、赛制、流程、如何得知等相关信息
    • 详细介绍想参加比赛的原因、为此需做的准备
    • 简述感受，发表感叹，适度升华
  3. 时态建议：介绍比赛相关信息用一般现在时，讲述与比赛历史、以往选手先关事项时用过去时。描述原因、自己做准备的打算时用将来时或一般现在时。
  4. 其他：注意在串题过程中灵活转换时态。比如经历类转化为介绍类，一定要从过去时转换为一般现在时，并且介绍时不要加太多个人因素。

I’m an adamant坚定的 fan of singing as well as TV singing competition franchises like American Idol and the X Factor. Watching average people transforming into super stars like Kelly Clarkson, Leona Lewis and One Directions – all big names – is inspiring and stimulating, not to mention my favorite moments when Simon Cowell, a snobby judge in those competitions insults contestants. So funny to watch. As a matter of fact, I myself am a singer. I play the guitar and I write my own originals. I used to sing in pubs back in university and earned some allowances for college.

Just the other day I was watching the finale of X Factor 2017 and Dermot the host was about to announce the winner, it struck me that why don’t I give it shot? I mean, I’m a good singer, and they welcome talents from all over the world, and I did see many singers from the US, Italy and even Philippines on stage and end well.

I rummaged through the snacks for my phone, and soon found out how I should audition for the show. I downloaded the application form online at the website of UK version of the X Factor. Turned out I needed to wait until the next season rolls around, which meant I still had plenty of time to get myself ready and booking a flight tickets can wait too.

Now I’ve been preparing for it. I suppose I need to pick a perfect song and master a verse of it. I kinda have an edge cause I can’t be more familiar with the show. Avoiding being cliched is the key. Technically, I’m not a belter, but an alternative folk artist, which is why I’m thinking maybe an original would do the trick. Besides, I plan to run through my piece every other day or so until I can do it on one leg with my hands tied behind my back.

I got this!

【P2】公共交通旅行

Describe a trip that you went on by public transportation

  • Where you went
  • What means of transportation you took
  • Who you were with
  • And explain how you felt about the trip
  1. 本题的A是a trip，描述经历；B是that you went on by public transportation, 限制内容。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 可按时间顺序讲述，旅程的时间、地点、所选交通工具
    • 详述选取该交通工具的原因、过程
    • 简述感受，发表感叹，适度升华
  3. 时态建议：过去时
  4. 其他：注意本题的特征点B是乘坐公共交通，因此旅程的目的地、所见所闻、游记完全不重要，重要的是乘坐公共交通的过程。

I’d like to describe the trip I went on by high-speed rail, a business trip which was supposed to be by plane and I could get the reimbursement, but tickets were sold out and I was actually quite happy to take the sleek bullet train hurtling between China’s two largest metropolises at speeds of more than 250 kph. The trip was a blurry but comfortable experience lasting less than five hours.

Having said that, I still got to appreciate the breathtaking sceneries outside, and the exuberantly旺盛的 greener-than-green Anhui, a province our train was passing through, had fields still resilient弹性的 green even in November.

Some of the trains’ notable features include seats which can be fully converted into a two-meter-long bed in business class, fold-out TV sets and a free power socket for every passenger. Passenger numbers are growing on the line as the nouveau riche are spreading their wings. And that was the first time I had the privilege to enjoy all that. Again, reimbursement. Thank you, my generous boss.

I call myself a train buff, and when still in university, I always took the regular train to go back home before high-speed rail were put to use. And it often cost me 30 hours on the way. Since smoking wasn’t completely banned back then and doing it in carriage connection areas is allowed, I had nowhere to hide from it, second-hand smoke being a major abomination to non-smokers. And that was why after graduation, I promised myself that I would never take the regular train again.

Back to this trip, five hours passed instantaneously, and I had the best snooze, if not the best sleep, in really a long time.

【每日表达】句型、习语、小幽默

caught between two stools

(fall) between two stools

  • 马戏团有一种杂耍，演员踩在两个翘脚的凳子上，在中间取得平衡。这个动作就叫caught between two stools，用来比喻在两个选项之间无法抉择。相对应的，还有个说法叫(fall)between two stools, 可怜的小丑从两个凳子上摔了下来，比喻两头落空。注意，fall可以省掉，并且介词是between，不是from，也不是off
  • I’m about to graduate but caught between two stools. Should I take the offer to Harvard, or return the call of Google’sHR?
  • He tries to be both teacher and friend, butfalls between two stools.

(to make/cut a) long story short

short and sweet

  • 所谓长话短说，真是一种美好的品德啊。有的人废话连篇，完全没有点，讲了一大堆结果自己都忘了绕了多少圈子岔了多少话题了。长话短说，就叫make a long story short，通常加上to表示自己下面要说的话是精炼的总结的。而精炼总结的话总是让人感到非常愉快，因此可以用short and sweet来形容这种不啰嗦的话或任何不长而又令人愉悦的东西，比如一部微电影。
  • To make a long story short, our governmentshould invest more in education.
  • Did you attend the class yesterday? If not,you should try the recording. The lecture was short and sweet, really awesome.

【P1技巧】四句话公式

  • 第一句，基本介绍+最大的特征
  • 第二句，第一个侧面（细节、特征）
  • 第三句，第二个侧面（细节、特征）
  • 第四局，总结和感受

Who’s your favorite celebrity in China?

My favorite Chinese celebrity is Yao Ming, a high-profile basketball player. He worked so hard to make it to NBA and excelled. A true philanthropist he is, charity work is what he does constantly. He’s long been my idol and as a Chinese, I’m quite proud of him.

今日任务：使用四句话公式，回答下列问题。

  1. What was your favorite sport when you were young?
    . Can you remember any time when it rained particularly heavily in your hometown?
    . What will become the most popular means of transportation in your country?

【纠音跟读】三步跟读法

试读／比较／可以模仿

Three passions, simple but overwhelmingly strong, have governed my life: the longing for love, the search for knowledge, and unbearable pity for the suffering of mankind. These passions, like great winds, have blown me hither and thither, in a wayward course, over a great ocean of anguish, reaching to the very verge of despair.

Three passions↑, simple bu(t)(ə) overwhelmingly strong↑, have(ə) govern(ed) my life: the(ə) long>ing for(ə) love↑, the(ə) search for(ə) knowledge↑, and(>) unbearable pity for(ə) the suffering>of(ə) mankind. These passions↑, li(ke)(ə) grea(t) winds↑, have(ə) blown me hither>and(ə) thither↑, in>a(ə) waywar(d) course↑, over>a(ə) great>ocean>of>anguish↑, reaching to(ə) the very verge>of(ə)despair.

音频第一遍为美式，第二遍为英式。注意录音中的单词读音、重音、连读、失去爆破、变音、弱读、语调及节奏感。

To ponder interminably over the reason for one’s own existence or the meaning of life in general seems to me, from an objective point of view, to be sheer folly. And yet everyone holds certain ideals by which he guides his aspiration and his judgment. The ideals which have always shone before me and filled me with the joy of living are goodness, beauty, and truth. To make a goal of comfort or happiness has never appealed to me; a system of ethics built on this basis would be sufficient only for a herd of cattle.

item.title: Day 7 Speaking
item.description: <h2 id="【P1】爱好类：户外活动"><a href="#【P1】爱好类：户外活动" class="headerlink"
item.content:encoded:
【P1】爱好类：户外活动

这个题季关于运动的题蛮多的，户外活动、身体训练、闲暇时光这些题都牵涉到运动，所以算P1的一个重点。除了经典了问喜好问时间之外，也需要思考一下运动给人带来的好处

What do you do in your spare time?

As a sports buff, I do various kinds of sports, be it collective ones like basketball or volleyball, and fundamental individual ones like jogging or swimming.

Do you like outdoor activities?

Outdoor activities are definitely my cup of tea, a major escape from my stationary work at office which never seems to end. Being outside alone is a breath of fresh air for most of modern people.

How often do you do that?

Pathetic as it may sound, my work takes up most of my time, even leisure time. But I’ve tried to squeeze in some time for exercises since I did the current job.

How much time do you spend outdoors every week?

If the two hours’ commute every day isn’t included, I would say maybe 30 minutes? I routinely take a stroll经常散步 with my wife after dinner.

What outdoor activities do you like to do (the most)?

Outdoor sports are my favorite. Hiking and boating always recharges my batteries on weekends. During weekdays, I often fantasize about scents气味 of flowers and chirping鸣叫 of birds.

【P2】地点类：需改进的场所

Describe a public place (facility) that you think needs improvements

  • Where the place is
  • What it is like
  • Why you think it needs improvements
  • When you go there
  • What you like and dislike in the public place
  • And explain how to make it better
  1. 本题的A是a place (facility)，描述地点；B是public, you think needs improvements，限制属性
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 描述此地点基本信息，位置、样貌、何时去，引出需改进的点
    • 详述改进的原因、方法
    • 评价改进的可能后果，适当升华
  3. 时态建议：描述地点特征时用一般现在时，讲改进的点也用现在时，讲之前的缺点用过去时，改进之后可用将来时，也可用虚拟语气。最后的评价如果涉及到类别问题，可用一般现在时；述及对现在的影响可用现在完成时。
  4. 其他：括号为不同版本。Facility = functional building. 从串题上讲，这道题与a building you dislike 基本可以匹配，只需要加上如何改进的表述即可。

I’d like to describe the artificial turf soccer pitch人造足球草坪 in my university. Since lately people around the world are crazy about the World Cup, this is the first thing that came into my mind. And without doubt, that dilapidated残破 facility really needs an overhaul翻修.

The soccer pitch is right in the center of the campus and not surprisingly has been the least visited place for a long time. I’m not a fan of soccer, but I pass by经过 this place a lot, and I’ve noticed the vicious恶毒 circle that the less it is used, the less possible that they will maintain it. Although this kind of makes me like this place as a great date option.

But not for soccer players of course, who have no other choice but play in the nearby municipal sports center and constantly complain about it. And it’s funny how disappointed you will be when you’re ready to play after class with your best buddies only to find the soccer pitch on campus is in a miserable condition.

It’s got this patchy turf, some parts even completely worn out, making the surface bumpy and when you run on it, you get this weird feeling that you may sprain your ankles and knees anytime. And the peripheral外围设别 guard net has tons of holes on it. So, watch out people passing by, cause you may get hit by a flying football.

Just think about it, the university decided to use synthetic合成的 grass instead of natural turf in the first place because it would be easier and much cheaper to maintain it, right? Then a complete fixup修理 won’t cost too much, all they need to do is to check and replenish补充 the turf in high use areas and fix the surrounding net. Doesn’t sound too much, does it? Or they can always just find a qualified groundsmen.

【P2】事件类：第一支手机

Describe the time you received your first cellphone (describe what difference it made when you first receive your phone)

  • When it was
  • Where you were
  • How you got it
  • Who gave it to you
  • What it was like
  • And explain how you felt about itc
  1. 本题的A是a time，描述事件；B是you received your first cellphone, 限制内容。实际上是一道事件+物品类题目。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 获得第一支手机的时间、地点、方式、人物
    • 详述此手机的外观、功能（抓住特征，不要面面俱到）
    • 使用心得，可与现在的手机对比
  3. 时态建议：获得第一支手机的相关事件叙述用过去时，描述手机可用过去时也可用现在时。述及对现在的影响时用现在完成时。
  4. 其他：作为事件+物品类题目，要一手抓故事、一手抓物品，两手都要硬。若抽到括号中的版本，描述顺序一样，可以强调使用此手机带来的影响。

Oh my god! I love this topic! Just the other day, I smashed my iPhone and its screen was soon into pieces. And that reminded me of my first phone ever, a Nokia brick. That’s right, I call it a brick, cause it was so robust强大的 and reliable I threw it off mischievously欢畅 and it remained intact with only inconspicuous不显眼的 scratches刮痕. It was from my mom for my 15th birthday. I convinced her to get me a phone for a summer break trip to visit my cousin in college while I was still in high school. The phone, arguably, would keep me safe from all that evil partying. That black and white thing really made my high school colorful.

This Nokia was like THE coolest gadgets小工具 in school and many of my classmates lusted after喜欢 it. It was like the size of a clutch purse. But you could change the cover to like pink crystals or alien heads, or whatever. Features included calls, messages, stopwatch, alarm clock, remainders, you name it. But most of all, the ultimate planet saving game – Snake. And of course, I spent hours playing it which sounds stupid today, but I was extremely good at it cause they kept forgetting how an amazing piano player I was and therefore ambidextrous双手灵活的，怀有二心的.

It was not only me back then who called it a brick, but nearly all Chinese Nokia fans did. It was said that it made a perfect weapon, an expedient one in case of emergencies. Just take a look at these fragile models now, and you’ll understand why I miss it so much, not to mention it carried those golden memories of being a teenager.

【P2】事件类：改变计划

Describe a time you had to change your plan (you changed your mind)

  • When this happened

  • What made you change the plan

  • What the new plan was

  • And explain how you felt about the change

  1. 本题的A是a time，描述事件；B是you had to change your plan (you changed your mind)，限制事件内容。

  2. 提纲建议：

    • 用时间顺序，开门见山，先讲改变前
    • 改变中（改变的原因、过程）
    • 改变后（新计划、想法，感受，总结）
  3. 时态建议：若先引出改变本身，那改变前可用可去完成时，改变中用过去时，改变后可用过去时也可用一般现在时，述及对现在的影响可用现在完成时。若不引出改变本身，开门见山一来就讲故事的话，一律使用过去时。
  4. 其他：这是最纯粹的事件类题目，纯记叙文，一定按照时间顺序讲清楚，当然也可以倒叙，从改变后讲起，追溯改变前。讲故事要有细节，才能打动人心，也能把coherence的分得到。

Coincidentally, just last weekend my plans were ruined by the weather. The weekends are usually when I’ll travel back home and visit with my family, which was my plan for last weekend. I’d arranged my schedule and made sure I didn’t have any studying or something else pending. I’d work during the week to free up the 2 days, so I couldunwind and have some quality time with my parents.

But on Friday night, the weather started to change out of the blue and it looked ominous不详的. And by the evening, when I had planned to leave, there was this incredible storm developing. The rain was torrential滂沱 with a gusting wind and tons of roads were reported as being flooded and traffic stopped in various places in the city.

I immediately called home and spoke with my mom who told me it was the same there and that I shouldn’t travel in such miserable weather or I might get delayed. I was a bit sad, but the weather was so bad no one really wanted to go out at all. In fact, the journey from my university back to my apartment which was only like 10 minutes’ walk was horrendous可怕的 with terrible traffic, and I ended up completely soaked浸泡. I had to admit that it would not have been a good idea to travel all the way home.

Well, then I waited for a couple of hours to see if things would be better, but it didn’t, so I eventually gave up on the idea and started to think what I could do instead. Frustrated as I was, I managed to talk a few friends of mine to play a few rounds of a hit online game with me and I won three fourths of the rounds, which was a first. And of course I called my family again to check on them, my mom telling me she learned a new song and sang it over the phone, which was excellent.

【每日表达】句型、习语、小幽默

once in a blue moon

like getting blood from/out of a stone

  • 有些景观，难得一遇，遇上了千万不要错过。蓝月亮虽美，两至三年才能见到一次。不过，至少两三年还能有幸欣赏到。想从石头里得到血液，那基本就是不可能了。因此我们可以用这两个习语表示某件事发生的概率非常低，非常难得罕见。
  • I work in Beijing while my family all live in Lhasa, and we get to meet once in a blue moon.
  • He had never practiced before taking the test, and getting an 8 is like getting blood out of a stone.

last/final straw

  • 听过最后一根稻草压垮骆驼的故事没？最后一根稻草，就叫last或final straw, 你看简不简单，好不好用？你跟男朋友处得本来就不好，他爸妈也不喜欢你。最关键的，他还出轨了，这就是让你跟他分手的最后一根稻草。

  • He’d been unhappy at work for a long time but the last straw came when he was refused promotion.

    【纠音跟读】短成分逗号隔，扬起来！

When he got to the station↑, she had already left.

She may be admired by the world↑, but I know the truth. So does God and so does Oprah. Two of us remain ashamed.

When they realized that I had lied to them↑, they would start not to believe me.

Believe it or not↑, I’ve had a crush on you for a long time.

语调

作家朗读

I feel that this award was not made to me as a man, but to my work - a life’s work in the agony and sweat of the human spirit, not for glory and least of all for profit, but to create out of the materials of the human spirit something which did not exist before. So this award is only mine in trust. It will not be difficult to find a dedication for the money part of it commensurate with the purpose and significance of its origin. But I would like to do the same with the acclaim too, by using this moment as a pinnacle from which I might be listened to by the young men and women already dedicated to the same anguish and travail, among whom is already that one who will some day stand here where I am standing.

item.title: Something you must know about JS
item.description: JavaScript专题之深浅拷贝
item.content:encoded:
JavaScript专题之深浅拷贝

https://github.com/mqyqingfeng/Blog/issues/32

JavaScript深入之call和apply的模拟实现

call

一句话介绍 call：

call() 方法在使用一个指定的 this 值和若干个指定的参数值的前提下调用某个函数或方法。

举个例子：

注意两点：

  1. call 改变了 this 的指向，指向到 foo
  2. bar 函数执行了

模拟实现第一步

那么我们该怎么模拟实现这两个效果呢？

试想当调用 call 的时候，把 foo 对象改造成如下：

这个时候 this 就指向了 foo，是不是很简单呢？

但是这样却给 foo 对象本身添加了一个属性，这可不行呐！

不过也不用担心，我们用 delete 再删除它不就好了~

所以我们模拟的步骤可以分为：

  1. 将函数设为对象的属性
  2. 执行该函数
  3. 删除该函数

以上个例子为例，就是：

fn 是对象的属性名，反正最后也要删除它，所以起成什么都无所谓。

根据这个思路，我们可以尝试着去写第一版的 call2 函数：

正好可以打印 1 哎！是不是很开心！(～￣▽￣)～

模拟实现第二步

最一开始也讲了，call 函数还能给定参数执行函数。举个例子：

注意：传入的参数并不确定，这可咋办？

不急，我们可以从 Arguments 对象中取值，取出第二个到最后一个参数，然后放到一个数组里。

比如这样：

不定长的参数问题解决了，我们接着要把这个参数数组放到要执行的函数的参数里面去。

也许有人想到用 ES6 的方法，不过 call 是 ES3 的方法，我们为了模拟实现一个 ES3 的方法，要用到ES6的方法，好像……，嗯，也可以啦。但是我们这次用 eval 方法拼成一个函数，类似于这样：

这里 args 会自动调用 Array.toString() 这个方法。

所以我们的第二版克服了两个大问题，代码如下：

(๑•̀ㅂ•́)و✧

模拟实现第三步

模拟代码已经完成 80%，还有两个小点要注意：

1.this 参数可以传 null，当为 null 的时候，视为指向 window

举个例子：

虽然这个例子本身不使用 call，结果依然一样。

2.函数是可以有返回值的！

举个例子：

不过都很好解决，让我们直接看第三版也就是最后一版的代码：

到此，我们完成了 call 的模拟实现，给自己一个赞 ｂ（￣▽￣）ｄ

apply的模拟实现

apply 的实现跟 call 类似，在这里直接给代码，代码来自于知乎 @郑航的实现：

JavaScript深入之bind的模拟实现

bind

一句话介绍 bind:

bind() 方法会创建一个新函数。当这个新函数被调用时，bind() 的第一个参数将作为它运行时的 this，之后的一序列参数将会在传递的实参前传入作为它的参数。(来自于 MDN )

由此我们可以首先得出 bind 函数的两个特点：

  1. 返回一个函数
  2. 可以传入参数

返回函数的模拟实现

从第一个特点开始，我们举个例子：

关于指定 this 的指向，我们可以使用 call 或者 apply 实现，关于 call 和 apply 的模拟实现，可以查看《JavaScript深入之call和apply的模拟实现》。我们来写第一版的代码：

此外，之所以 return self.apply(context)，是考虑到绑定函数可能是有返回值的，依然是这个例子：

传参的模拟实现

接下来看第二点，可以传入参数。这个就有点让人费解了，我在 bind 的时候，是否可以传参呢？我在执行 bind 返回的函数的时候，可不可以传参呢？让我们看个例子：

函数需要传 name 和 age 两个参数，竟然还可以在 bind 的时候，只传一个 name，在执行返回的函数的时候，再传另一个参数 age!

这可咋办？不急，我们用 arguments 进行处理：

构造函数效果的模拟实现

完成了这两点，最难的部分到啦！因为 bind 还有一个特点，就是

一个绑定函数也能使用new操作符创建对象：这种行为就像把原函数当成构造器。提供的 this 值被忽略，同时调用时的参数被提供给模拟函数。

也就是说当 bind 返回的函数作为构造函数的时候，bind 时指定的 this 值会失效，但传入的参数依然生效。举个例子：

注意：尽管在全局和 foo 中都声明了 value 值，最后依然返回了 undefind，说明绑定的 this 失效了，如果大家了解 new 的模拟实现，就会知道这个时候的 this 已经指向了 obj。

(哈哈，我这是为我的下一篇文章《JavaScript深入系列之new的模拟实现》打广告)。

所以我们可以通过修改返回的函数的原型来实现，让我们写一下：

如果对原型链稍有困惑，可以查看《JavaScript深入之从原型到原型链》。

构造函数效果的优化实现

但是在这个写法中，我们直接将 fBound.prototype = this.prototype，我们直接修改 fBound.prototype 的时候，也会直接修改绑定函数的 prototype。这个时候，我们可以通过一个空函数来进行中转：

到此为止，大的问题都已经解决，给自己一个赞！o(￣▽￣)ｄ

三个小问题

接下来处理些小问题:

1.apply 这段代码跟 MDN 上的稍有不同

在 MDN 中文版讲 bind 的模拟实现时，apply 这里的代码是：

多了一个关于 context 是否存在的判断，然而这个是错误的！

举个例子：

以上代码正常情况下会打印 2，如果换成了 context || this，这段代码就会打印 1！

所以这里不应该进行 context 的判断，大家查看 MDN 同样内容的英文版，就不存在这个判断！

(2018年3月27日更新，中文版已经改了😀)

2.调用 bind 的不是函数咋办？

不行，我们要报错！

3.我要在线上用

那别忘了做个兼容：

当然最好是用 es5-shim 啦。

最终代码

所以最最后的代码就是：

JavaScript深入之从原型到原型链

构造函数创建对象

我们先使用构造函数创建一个对象：

在这个例子中，Person 就是一个构造函数，我们使用 new 创建了一个实例对象 person。

很简单吧，接下来进入正题：

prototype

每个函数都有一个 prototype 属性，就是我们经常在各种例子中看到的那个 prototype ，比如：

那这个函数的 prototype 属性到底指向的是什么呢？是这个函数的原型吗？

其实，函数的 prototype 属性指向了一个对象，这个对象正是调用该构造函数而创建的实例的原型，也就是这个例子中的 person1 和 person2 的原型。

那什么是原型呢？你可以这样理解：每一个JavaScript对象(null除外)在创建的时候就会与之关联另一个对象，这个对象就是我们所说的原型，每一个对象都会从原型”继承”属性。

让我们用一张图表示构造函数和实例原型之间的关系：

在这张图中我们用 Object.prototype 表示实例原型。

那么我们该怎么表示实例与实例原型，也就是 person 和 Person.prototype 之间的关系呢，这时候我们就要讲到第二个属性：

proto

这是每一个JavaScript对象(除了 null )都具有的一个属性，叫proto，这个属性会指向该对象的原型。

为了证明这一点,我们可以在火狐或者谷歌中输入：

于是我们更新下关系图：

既然实例对象和构造函数都可以指向原型，那么原型是否有属性指向构造函数或者实例呢？

constructor

指向实例倒是没有，因为一个构造函数可以生成多个实例，但是原型指向构造函数倒是有的，这就要讲到第三个属性：constructor，每个原型都有一个 constructor 属性指向关联的构造函数。

为了验证这一点，我们可以尝试：

所以再更新下关系图：

综上我们已经得出：

了解了构造函数、实例原型、和实例之间的关系，接下来我们讲讲实例和原型的关系：

实例与原型

当读取实例的属性时，如果找不到，就会查找与对象关联的原型中的属性，如果还查不到，就去找原型的原型，一直找到最顶层为止。

举个例子：

在这个例子中，我们给实例对象 person 添加了 name 属性，当我们打印 person.name 的时候，结果自然为 Daisy。

但是当我们删除了 person 的 name 属性时，读取 person.name，从 person 对象中找不到 name 属性就会从 person 的原型也就是 person.proto ，也就是 Person.prototype中查找，幸运的是我们找到了 name 属性，结果为 Kevin。

但是万一还没有找到呢？原型的原型又是什么呢？

原型的原型

在前面，我们已经讲了原型也是一个对象，既然是对象，我们就可以用最原始的方式创建它，那就是：

其实原型对象就是通过 Object 构造函数生成的，结合之前所讲，实例的 proto 指向构造函数的 prototype ，所以我们再更新下关系图：

原型链

那 Object.prototype 的原型呢？

null，我们可以打印：

然而 null 究竟代表了什么呢？

引用阮一峰老师的 《undefined与null的区别》 就是：

null 表示“没有对象”，即该处不应该有值。

所以 Object.prototype.proto 的值为 null 跟 Object.prototype 没有原型，其实表达了一个意思。

所以查找属性的时候查到 Object.prototype 就可以停止查找了。

最后一张关系图也可以更新为：

顺便还要说一下，图中由相互关联的原型组成的链状结构就是原型链，也就是蓝色的这条线。

补充

最后，补充三点大家可能不会注意的地方：

constructor

首先是 constructor 属性，我们看个例子：

当获取 person.constructor 时，其实 person 中并没有 constructor 属性,当不能读取到constructor 属性时，会从 person 的原型也就是 Person.prototype 中读取，正好原型中有该属性，所以：

proto

其次是 proto ，绝大部分浏览器都支持这个非标准的方法访问原型，然而它并不存在于 Person.prototype 中，实际上，它是来自于 Object.prototype ，与其说是一个属性，不如说是一个 getter/setter，当使用 obj.proto 时，可以理解成返回了 Object.getPrototypeOf(obj)。

真的是继承吗？

最后是关于继承，前面我们讲到“每一个对象都会从原型‘继承’属性”，实际上，继承是一个十分具有迷惑性的说法，引用《你不知道的JavaScript》中的话，就是：

继承意味着复制操作，然而 JavaScript 默认并不会复制对象的属性，相反，JavaScript 只是在两个对象之间创建一个关联，这样，一个对象就可以通过委托访问另一个对象的属性和函数，所以与其叫继承，委托的说法反而更准确些。

JavaScript深入之词法作用域和动态作用域

作用域

作用域是指程序源代码中定义变量的区域。

作用域规定了如何查找变量，也就是确定当前执行代码对变量的访问权限。

JavaScript 采用词法作用域(lexical scoping)，也就是静态作用域。

静态作用域与动态作用域

因为 JavaScript 采用的是词法作用域，函数的作用域在函数定义的时候就决定了。

而与词法作用域相对的是动态作用域，函数的作用域是在函数调用的时候才决定的。

让我们认真看个例子就能明白之间的区别：

假设JavaScript采用静态作用域，让我们分析下执行过程：

执行 foo 函数，先从 foo 函数内部查找是否有局部变量 value，如果没有，就根据书写的位置，查找上面一层的代码，也就是 value 等于 1，所以结果会打印 1。

假设JavaScript采用动态作用域，让我们分析下执行过程：

执行 foo 函数，依然是从 foo 函数内部查找是否有局部变量 value。如果没有，就从调用函数的作用域，也就是 bar 函数内部查找 value 变量，所以结果会打印 2。

前面我们已经说了，JavaScript采用的是静态作用域，所以这个例子的结果是 1。

动态作用域

也许你会好奇什么语言是动态作用域？

bash 就是动态作用域，不信的话，把下面的脚本存成例如 scope.bash，然后进入相应的目录，用命令行执行 bash ./scope.bash，看看打印的值是多少。

这个文件也可以在 Github 博客仓库中找到。

思考题

最后，让我们看一个《JavaScript权威指南》中的例子：

猜猜两段代码各自的执行结果是多少？

这里直接告诉大家结果，两段代码都会打印：local scope。

原因也很简单，因为JavaScript采用的是词法作用域，函数的作用域基于函数创建的位置。

而引用《JavaScript权威指南》的回答就是：

JavaScript 函数的执行用到了作用域链，这个作用域链是在函数定义的时候创建的。嵌套的函数 f() 定义在这个作用域链里，其中的变量 scope 一定是局部变量，不管何时何地执行函数 f()，这种绑定在执行 f() 时依然有效。

JavaScript专题之函数柯里化

定义

维基百科中对柯里化 (Currying) 的定义为：

In mathematics and computer science, currying is the technique of translating the evaluation of a function that takes multiple arguments (or a tuple of arguments) into evaluating a sequence of functions, each with a single argument.

翻译成中文：

在数学和计算机科学中，柯里化是一种将使用多个参数的一个函数转换成一系列使用一个参数的函数的技术。

举个例子：

用途

我们会讲到如何写出这个 curry 函数，并且会将这个 curry 函数写的很强大，但是在编写之前，我们需要知道柯里化到底有什么用？

举个例子：

想想 jQuery 虽然有 $.ajax 这样通用的方法，但是也有 $.get 和 $.post 的语法糖。(当然 jQuery 底层是否是这样做的，我就没有研究了)。

curry 的这种用途可以理解为：参数复用。本质上是降低通用性，提高适用性。

可是即便如此，是不是依然感觉没什么用呢？

如果我们仅仅是把参数一个一个传进去，意义可能不大，但是如果我们是把柯里化后的函数传给其他函数比如 map 呢？

举个例子：

比如我们有这样一段数据：

如果我们要获取所有的 name 值，我们可以这样做：

不过如果我们有 curry 函数：

我们为了获取 name 属性还要再编写一个 prop 函数，是不是又麻烦了些？

但是要注意，prop 函数编写一次后，以后可以多次使用，实际上代码从原本的三行精简成了一行，而且你看代码是不是更加易懂了？

person.map(prop('name')) 就好像直白的告诉你：person 对象遍历(map)获取(prop) name 属性。

是不是感觉有点意思了呢？

第一版

未来我们会接触到更多有关柯里化的应用，不过那是未来的事情了，现在我们该编写这个 curry 函数了。

一个经常会看到的 curry 函数的实现为：

我们可以这样使用：

已经有柯里化的感觉了，但是还没有达到要求，不过我们可以把这个函数用作辅助函数，帮助我们写真正的 curry 函数。

第二版

我们验证下这个函数：

效果已经达到我们的预期，然而这个 curry 函数的实现好难理解呐……

为了让大家更好的理解这个 curry 函数，我给大家写个极简版的代码：

大家先从理解这个 curry 函数开始。

当执行 fn1() 时，函数返回：

当执行 fn1()() 时，函数返回：

当执行 fn1()()() 时，函数返回：

当执行 fn1()()()() 时，因为此时 length > 2 为 false，所以执行 fn()：

再回到真正的 curry 函数，我们以下面的例子为例：

当执行 fn1(“a”, “b”) 时：

当执行 fn1(“a”, “b”)(“c”) 时，函数返回：

当执行 fn1(“a”, “b”)(“c”)(“d”) 时，此时 arguments.length < length 为 false ，执行 fn(arguments)，相当于：

函数执行结束。

所以，其实整段代码又很好理解：

sub_curry 的作用就是用函数包裹原函数，然后给原函数传入之前的参数，当执行 fn0(…)(…) 的时候，执行包裹函数，返回原函数，然后再调用 sub_curry 再包裹原函数，然后将新的参数混合旧的参数再传入原函数，直到函数参数的数目达到要求为止。

如果要明白 curry 函数的运行原理，大家还是要动手写一遍，尝试着分析执行步骤。

更易懂的实现

当然了，如果你觉得还是无法理解，你可以选择下面这种实现方式，可以实现同样的效果：

或许大家觉得这种方式更好理解，又能实现一样的效果，为什么不直接就讲这种呢？

因为想给大家介绍各种实现的方法嘛，不能因为难以理解就不给大家介绍呐~

第三版

curry 函数写到这里其实已经很完善了，但是注意这个函数的传参顺序必须是从左到右，根据形参的顺序依次传入，如果我不想根据这个顺序传呢？

我们可以创建一个占位符，比如这样：

我们直接看第三版的代码：

JavaScript专题之数组去重

前言

数组去重方法老生常谈，既然是常谈，我也来谈谈。

双层循环

也许我们首先想到的是使用 indexOf 来循环判断一遍，但在这个方法之前，让我们先看看最原始的方法：

在这个方法中，我们使用循环嵌套，最外层循环 array，里面循环 res，如果 array[i] 的值跟 res[j] 的值相等，就跳出循环，如果都不等于，说明元素是唯一的，这时候 j 的值就会等于 res 的长度，根据这个特点进行判断，将值添加进 res。

看起来很简单吧，之所以要讲一讲这个方法，是因为——————兼容性好！

indexOf

我们可以用 indexOf 简化内层的循环：

排序后去重

试想我们先将要去重的数组使用 sort 方法排序后，相同的值就会被排在一起，然后我们就可以只判断当前元素与上一个元素是否相同，相同就说明重复，不相同就添加进 res，让我们写个 demo：

如果我们对一个已经排好序的数组去重，这种方法效率肯定高于使用 indexOf。

unique API

知道了这两种方法后，我们可以去尝试写一个名为 unique 的工具函数，我们根据一个参数 isSorted 判断传入的数组是否是已排序的，如果为 true，我们就判断相邻元素是否相同，如果为 false，我们就使用 indexOf 进行判断

优化

尽管 unqique 已经可以试下去重功能，但是为了让这个 API 更加强大，我们来考虑一个需求：

新需求：字母的大小写视为一致，比如’a’和’A’，保留一个就可以了！

虽然我们可以先处理数组中的所有数据，比如将所有的字母转成小写，然后再传入unique函数，但是有没有方法可以省掉处理数组的这一遍循环，直接就在去重的循环中做呢？让我们去完成这个需求：

在这一版也是最后一版的实现中，函数传递三个参数：

array：表示要去重的数组，必填

isSorted：表示函数传入的数组是否已排过序，如果为 true，将会采用更快的方法进行去重

iteratee：传入一个函数，可以对每个元素进行重新的计算，然后根据处理的结果进行去重

至此，我们已经仿照着 underscore 的思路写了一个 unique 函数，具体可以查看 Github。

filter

ES5 提供了 filter 方法，我们可以用来简化外层循环：

比如使用 indexOf 的方法：

排序去重的方法：

Object 键值对

去重的方法众多，尽管我们已经跟着 underscore 写了一个 unqiue API，但是让我们看看其他的方法拓展下视野：

这种方法是利用一个空的 Object 对象，我们把数组的值存成 Object 的 key 值，比如 Object[value1] = true，在判断另一个值的时候，如果 Object[value2]存在的话，就说明该值是重复的。示例代码如下：

我们可以发现，是有问题的，因为 1 和 ‘1’ 是不同的，但是这种方法会判断为同一个值，这是因为对象的键值只能是字符串，所以我们可以使用 typeof item + item 拼成字符串作为 key 值来避免这个问题：

然而，即便如此，我们依然无法正确区分出两个对象，比如 {value: 1} 和 {value: 2}，因为 typeof item + item 的结果都会是 object[object Object]，不过我们可以使用 JSON.stringify 将对象序列化：

ES6

随着 ES6 的到来，去重的方法又有了进展，比如我们可以使用 Set 和 Map 数据结构，以 Set 为例，ES6 提供了新的数据结构 Set。它类似于数组，但是成员的值都是唯一的，没有重复的值。

是不是感觉就像是为去重而准备的？让我们来写一版：

甚至可以再简化下：

还可以再简化下：

此外，如果用 Map 的话：

JavaScript 的进化

我们可以看到，去重方法从原始的 14 行代码到 ES6 的 1 行代码，其实也说明了 JavaScript 这门语言在不停的进步，相信以后的开发也会越来越高效。

特殊类型比较

去重的方法就到此结束了，然而要去重的元素类型可能是多种多样，除了例子中简单的 1 和 ‘1’ 之外，其实还有 null、undefined、NaN、对象等，那么对于这些元素，之前的这些方法的去重结果又是怎样呢？

在此之前，先让我们先看几个例子：

那么，对于这样一个数组

以上各种方法去重的结果到底是什么样的呢？

我特地整理了一个列表，我们重点关注下对象和 NaN 的去重情况：

方法结果说明
for循环[1, “1”, null, undefined, String, String, /a/, /a/, NaN, NaN]对象和 NaN 不去重
indexOf[1, “1”, null, undefined, String, String, /a/, /a/, NaN, NaN]对象和 NaN 不去重
sort[/a/, /a/, “1”, 1, String, 1, String, NaN, NaN, null, undefined]对象和 NaN 不去重 数字 1 也不去重
filter + indexOf[1, “1”, null, undefined, String, String, /a/, /a/]对象不去重 NaN 会被忽略掉
filter + sort[/a/, /a/, “1”, 1, String, 1, String, NaN, NaN, null, undefined]对象和 NaN 不去重 数字 1 不去重
优化后的键值对方法[1, “1”, null, undefined, String, /a/, NaN]全部去重
Set[1, “1”, null, undefined, String, String, /a/, /a/, NaN]对象不去重 NaN 去重

想了解为什么会出现以上的结果，看两个 demo 便能明白：

indexOf 底层还是使用 === 进行判断，因为 NaN === NaN的结果为 false，所以使用 indexOf 查找不到 NaN 元素

Set 认为尽管 NaN === NaN 为 false，但是这两个元素是重复的。

JavaScript深入之创建对象的多种方式以及优缺点

写在前面

这篇文章讲解创建对象的各种方式，以及优缺点。

但是注意：

这篇文章更像是笔记，因为《JavaScript高级程序设计》写得真是太好了！

1. 工厂模式

缺点：对象无法识别，因为所有的实例都指向一个原型

2. 构造函数模式

优点：实例可以识别为一个特定的类型

缺点：每次创建实例时，每个方法都要被创建一次

2.1 构造函数模式优化

优点：解决了每个方法都要被重新创建的问题

缺点：这叫啥封装……

3. 原型模式

优点：方法不会重新创建

缺点：1. 所有的属性和方法都共享 2. 不能初始化参数

3.1 原型模式优化

优点：封装性好了一点

缺点：重写了原型，丢失了constructor属性

3.2 原型模式优化

优点：实例可以通过constructor属性找到所属构造函数

缺点：原型模式该有的缺点还是有

4. 组合模式

构造函数模式与原型模式双剑合璧。

优点：该共享的共享，该私有的私有，使用最广泛的方式

缺点：有的人就是希望全部都写在一起，即更好的封装性

4.1 动态原型模式

注意：使用动态原型模式时，不能用对象字面量重写原型

解释下为什么：

为了解释这个问题，假设开始执行var person1 = new Person('kevin')。

如果对 new 和 apply 的底层执行过程不是很熟悉，可以阅读底部相关链接中的文章。

我们回顾下 new 的实现步骤：

  1. 首先新建一个对象
  2. 然后将对象的原型指向 Person.prototype
  3. 然后 Person.apply(obj)
  4. 返回这个对象

注意这个时候，回顾下 apply 的实现步骤，会执行 obj.Person 方法，这个时候就会执行 if 语句里的内容，注意构造函数的 prototype 属性指向了实例的原型，使用字面量方式直接覆盖 Person.prototype，并不会更改实例的原型的值，person1 依然是指向了以前的原型，而不是 Person.prototype。而之前的原型是没有 getName 方法的，所以就报错了！

如果你就是想用字面量方式写代码，可以尝试下这种：

5.1 寄生构造函数模式

寄生构造函数模式，我个人认为应该这样读：

寄生-构造函数-模式，也就是说寄生在构造函数的一种方法。

也就是说打着构造函数的幌子挂羊头卖狗肉，你看创建的实例使用 instanceof 都无法指向构造函数！

这样方法可以在特殊情况下使用。比如我们想创建一个具有额外方法的特殊数组，但是又不想直接修改Array构造函数，我们可以这样写：

你会发现，其实所谓的寄生构造函数模式就是比工厂模式在创建对象的时候，多使用了一个new，实际上两者的结果是一样的。

但是作者可能是希望能像使用普通 Array 一样使用 SpecialArray，虽然把 SpecialArray 当成函数也一样能用，但是这并不是作者的本意，也变得不优雅。

在可以使用其他模式的情况下，不要使用这种模式。

但是值得一提的是，上面例子中的循环：

可以替换成：

5.2 稳妥构造函数模式

所谓稳妥对象，指的是没有公共属性，而且其方法也不引用 this 的对象。

与寄生构造函数模式有两点不同：

  1. 新创建的实例方法不引用 this
  2. 不使用 new 操作符调用构造函数

稳妥对象最适合在一些安全的环境中。

稳妥构造函数模式也跟工厂模式一样，无法识别对象所属类型。

JavaScript深入之继承的多种方式和优缺点

写在前面

本文讲解JavaScript各种继承方式和优缺点。

但是注意：

这篇文章更像是笔记，哎，再让我感叹一句：《JavaScript高级程序设计》写得真是太好了！

1.原型链继承

问题：

1.引用类型的属性被所有实例共享，举个例子：

2.在创建 Child 的实例时，不能向Parent传参

2.借用构造函数(经典继承)

优点：

1.避免了引用类型的属性被所有实例共享

2.可以在 Child 中向 Parent 传参

举个例子：

缺点：

方法都在构造函数中定义，每次创建实例都会创建一遍方法。

3.组合继承

原型链继承和经典继承双剑合璧。

优点：融合原型链继承和构造函数的优点，是 JavaScript 中最常用的继承模式。

4.原型式继承

就是 ES5 Object.create 的模拟实现，将传入的对象作为创建的对象的原型。

缺点：

包含引用类型的属性值始终都会共享相应的值，这点跟原型链继承一样。

注意：修改person1.name的值，person2.name的值并未发生改变，并不是因为person1和person2有独立的 name 值，而是因为person1.name = 'person1'，给person1添加了 name 值，并非修改了原型上的 name 值。

5. 寄生式继承

创建一个仅用于封装继承过程的函数，该函数在内部以某种形式来做增强对象，最后返回对象。

缺点：跟借用构造函数模式一样，每次创建对象都会创建一遍方法。

6. 寄生组合式继承

为了方便大家阅读，在这里重复一下组合继承的代码：

组合继承最大的缺点是会调用两次父构造函数。

一次是设置子类型实例的原型的时候：

一次在创建子类型实例的时候：

回想下 new 的模拟实现，其实在这句中，我们会执行：

在这里，我们又会调用了一次 Parent 构造函数。

所以，在这个例子中，如果我们打印 child1 对象，我们会发现 Child.prototype 和 child1 都有一个属性为colors，属性值为['red', 'blue', 'green']。

那么我们该如何精益求精，避免这一次重复调用呢？

如果我们不使用 Child.prototype = new Parent() ，而是间接的让 Child.prototype 访问到 Parent.prototype 呢？

看看如何实现：

最后我们封装一下这个继承方法：

引用《JavaScript高级程序设计》中对寄生组合式继承的夸赞就是：

这种方式的高效率体现它只调用了一次 Parent 构造函数，并且因此避免了在 Parent.prototype 上面创建不必要的、多余的属性。与此同时，原型链还能保持不变；因此，还能够正常使用 instanceof 和 isPrototypeOf。开发人员普遍认为寄生组合式继承是引用类型最理想的继承范式。

item.title: Day 6 Speaking
item.description: <h2 id="【P1】工具类：地图"><a href="#【P1】工具类：地图" class="headerlink"
item.content:encoded:
【P1】工具类：地图

地图与字典的出题方向比较像，有传统与现代的碰撞，有基本使用情况，必要程度等。Again, 按实际情况回答哦。

Do you use maps frequently?

I use maps whenever I go out for both short-haul短途 and long-haul trips since I have such a bad sense of direction. I can’t live without them.

Do you prefer electronic maps or paper maps?

I bet anyone who’s familiar with electronic gadgets would go with an electronic one. Turning to your mapping apps are anything but a hassle. All you need to do is to enter the place you’re heading to, and the recommended routes with the least traffic lights and lightest traffic will come out. How magical is that!

Who taught you how to use a map?

No one. I taught myself. I’m terrible with directions but surprisingly talented in using a map, a digital one esp. I mean, using one of those mapping apps to find the best restaurants nearby is my forte! How about that?

Do you think maps are important?

Certainly, well at least for me. I can’t think of anything more appalling than to have left your phone at home when you’re out. I mean, not because I need it to text my friends or anything, but, I will surely get lost!

【P2】地点类：友人同行之地

Describe a place (in your city or town) that you like to visit with your friends (or family).

  • Where this place is
  • When the last time you went there was
  • Who you like to go with
  • And explain why you like to go there.

  1. 本题的A是a place，描述地点；B是you like to visit with your friends (or family) 以及 (in your city or town)，限制属性。实质上是地点+规律性事件结合类题目。

  2. 提纲建议：

    1. 描述这一地方的位置等相关信息（同谁去、特征等），引出喜欢去，概述原因
    2. 可选择用规律性语气详述每次去的routine，也可以选择讲述上次去的经历
    3. 对此地的评价

  3. 时态建议：通篇以一般现在时为主，无论是对地点的介绍还是说自己与朋友同去的经历。介绍地点的历史、自己去的某一次特定的经历，应当用过去时。

  4. 其他：这种题是地点类中最简单的一种，几乎不需要太多特别的思考，并且能串过来的题也非常多。脑洞打开哦。

I’m gonna describe one of the coolest areas of Beijing, the 798 Art District. A paradise for art lovers and youngsters who enjoy an artistic vibe. I’m a member of the club of contemporary art in my university and we have this little gang that regularly go there, like once in two weeks, give or take, to see different exhibitions and have a little relax of our own. Coincidentally, we went there for the 10th time yesterday.

A formidable area of disused, Mao-era factories built with East German expertise, 798 Art District, is Beijing’s main concentration of contemporary art galleries, art bookshops and cafes. The site first rose to prominence in the late 1990s, its Bauhaus-style former factory workshops ideally suited to ambitious art installations. You could easily spend half a day wandering around the complex. Galleries not to miss include the 798 Art Factory, a Bauhaus, hangar-like space with its concrete wall columns emblazoned in 1950s Maoist slogans, “Long Live Chairman Mao”, and industrial machinery scattered amid whichever Chinese or international art exhibition is currently in situ.

The reason why we go there frequently is that there’s always been some different special exhibitions going on there. One of the difficulties many people have in approachingcontemporary artwork is its diversity—diversity of material, form, subject matter, and even time periods. It is distinguished by the very lack of a uniform organizing principle, ideology, or -ism. And this is exactly what’s unique and appealing about it. After appreciating those excellent works, we often end up in a café to unwind a little bit, chatting about what we’ve seen and what we’re gonna see next. It’s such a blast.

Such a wonderful art resort. I love it.

【P2】地点类：定居之地

Describe a city or country you want to live (or work) (the most) in the future

  • Where the country or city is
  • Why you want to live or work there
  • Who you like to live or work with
  • And explain what you will do to achieve your dreams
  1. 本题的A是a city or country，描述地点；B是you want to live or work (the most) in the future，限制属性，时间。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 描述此地的基本信息，位置、特征、你与它的渊源等，引出想定居或工作
    • 详述想定居或工作于此的原因
    • 简述努力的方向，总结升华
  3. 时态建议：描述地点信息时用一般现在时，如果要讲故事用过去时。憧憬未来可用将来时或虚拟语气，但当成计划描述时应用现在时。最后的评价如果涉及到类别问题，可用一般现在时；述及对现在的影响可用现在完成时。
  4. 其他：括号为不同版本的题目，本题版本不影响素材。注意串题时不要把曾去过、旅游过的地方直接拿来讲，必须增添想“定居”“长时间工作”于此等内容，否则容易跑题。对居住和工作来说，风景没那么重要，宜居、房价、空气、就业机会、前景等更重要。

So, here’s a city that I really want to move to and settle in, like in 10 years. Well, let’s say, there is an old Chinese saying that can be translated as “Up in the sky is heaven, and down here we have Suzhou and Hangzhou”. So here it is, Hangzhou is the city I’m dying to live in. It is renowned for its historical relics and natural beauty and became one of China’s most prosperous cities partly due to its location at the southern end of the Grand Canal and partly because of its natural sceneries too.

I had lived there for a month before I went to university, to lodge with an old friend. And I found it extremely livable, first because of the exuberant urban greening. Walking on the streets, there is a sense of open space here and an incredible proliferation of green wherever you look. I believe they have some innovations too, and the city is doing its best to green areas that normally are barren and lifeless. Not to mention the West Lake area.
