The rather vast area of picturesque landscape and mountains surrounding the lake give Hangzhou a green heart and I think may be the inspiration for the well-tended greenery throughout the city. I love the city second because it’s got the best affordability than most of big cities in the country. If I moved there from Beijing where I’m currently living, my expenses would drop like a rock overnight. Living expenses are cut in half, and don’t get me started on the house price.

I might live there alone, with occasional visits to my family, since I have this independent streak in me and don’t want my life to be disturbed too much. Moving there is actually an easy decision to make, and all I have to do is graduate and find a satisfying job there. Wish me luck.

【P2】地点类：想要的休闲设施

Describe a leisure facility (cinema theater sports center) you would like to have in your hometown

  • What it is
  • Where you would like to have it
  • When you would go there
  • Why you would like to have this facility in your hometown
  • How the facility would change your life
  • And explain how you would feel about it
  1. 本题的A是a leisure facility，描述设施；B是you would like to have in your hometown，属性、地点。
  2. 提纲建议：
    1. 描述此设施的基本信息，你想要它出现的位置、特征、，引出你想要它的原因
    2. 详细讨论拥有此设施的好处、作用
    3. 总结、感叹、升华。
  3. 时态建议：此题比较特殊，可全程使用虚拟语气。描述别的地方的类似设施时可使用一般现在时及过去时（去过的经历等）。

  4. 其他：括号中提示了典型的娱乐设施，但我们可以选取的不止于此。娱乐设施还包括：

    • Visitor attractions such as museums, galleries and theme parks
    • Public areas such as parks, playgrounds and community centres
    • Natural features such as beaches, caves and lakes
    • Sports facilities such as stadiums, pitches, gyms and health clubs
    • Cinemas, theatres and concert venues

此题全程用虚拟语气是比较理想的，也可以顺便把语法多样性的分数拿到手。注意不同时态虚拟语气的使用。另外，本题限制地点为你的家乡，如果把自己家乡说成一个小城市，那么常见娱乐设施就都能讲了。

Well I live in a beautiful city called xxx in which all basic and modern amenities设施 are available. It has also gone through a number of changes and some new facilities are also added in it. But here I’d like to talk about a leisure facility I think this lovely town is missing – a theatre where people can go to let their hair down.

We know how important the location of a facility is, right? I suppose it should be in the heart of the city where it would be easy for different age groups to commute there hassle free. Just like in any other theaters in the world, live performers, dancers, drama actors would finally find their stage. Some facilities should be available there to make people’s experiences more memorable. A lovely café providing scrumptious cuisines and beverages should be available that would act as a feather in the cap. Plus, the sitting arrangement should be in apple pie order which can accommodate a number of individuals.

I believe that providing these recreational facilities would help people to get an escape from the bustle and hustle of the world and to de-stress themselves. Moreover, local people could not only appreciate first-class performances, but also demonstrate their own talents. It would also provide an amazing chance to the people to spend some quality time with each other. All in all, providing this facility to the people would help them to get a break from the monotony.

I’m currently working in Shanghai, one of the metropolises of China, if this theater thing should be coming into reality, I would definitely go back to my hometown more frequently.

【综合技巧】人物主宾换着说，句子多样又灵活

  • I really enjoy trying new food. People around me all embrace new things in life too. I usually hang out with them exploring fancy new restaurants.
  • I really enjoy trying new food. New things in life are something people around me all embrace. I usually…
  • Hanging out with them and exploring fancy new restaurants is just so much fun.

今日任务：

  1. 使用人物主宾换着说的技巧，paraphrase这个段落吧：

    I work in a state-owned insurance company as an actuary. I calculate risks and premiums and determine how we should charge people for insurance. I love my job because it’s fairly important. I love my colleagues too. They are very nice.

  2. 回答下列问题，注意句子多样性：

    • Do people in your country still wear watches?
    • Would you want to be a celebrity in the future?

【每日表达】句型、习语、小幽默

to hem/hum and haw

at the drop of a hat

  • 再果决的人，有些事还是会犹犹豫豫；再谨慎的人，面对一些人仍然斩钉截铁。我们时常想表达做一件事、说一些话、爱一个人，是思忖再三裹足不前，还是风风火火当机立断。今天介绍的两个习语，就分别表达了这两个意思。Hem（美式）/hum（英式）and haw, 动词短语，表示思考良久，一直不做决定；at the drop of a hat，介词短语，表示毫不犹豫，立刻行动。注意它们的词性和应充当的句子成分。
  • The government hemmed and hawed for so long before finally deciding to build the sanctuary.
  • He’s such a helpful friend who’s always ready to offer a helping hand at the drop of a hat.

best of both worlds

double whammy

  • 既想轻松，又想快速考到理想分数，于是有人开始剑走偏锋，企图get the best of both worlds. 这当然是不可能的。更可能的情况是，既没考到好分数，钱又花出去一大把，赔了分数又折钱。这叫祸不单行，double whammy. Again, 注意它们的词性。
  • When you work in the city and live in the country, you get the best of both worlds.
  • With the cold weather and the high cost of natural gas, homeowners in the suburbs were hit with a double whammy this winter.

今日任务：

  • 结合本季P1~P3题目，用这些表达法造句，并拓展成段落。

第6天【纠音跟读】常错辅音2

[ʃ]

  • Hush
  • she finish shadow shape
  • wish ship
  • action social ancient
  • She finished fishing and wished to have aship.
  • the shaping of ancient social actions

[tʃ]

  • rich March much cheap
  • natural future catch
  • situation
  • Situation is the rich Chandler will be muchcheaper in March.
  • Catch that moment and be natural in thefuture.

The art of living is to know when to hold fast and when to let go. For life is a paradox: it enjoins us to cling to its many gifts even while it ordains their eventual relinquishment. The rabbis of old put it this way: “A man comes to this world with his fist clenched, but when he dies, his hand is open.”

item.title: Day 5 Speaking
item.description: <h2 id="【P1】工具类：字典"><a href="#【P1】工具类：字典" class="headerlink"
item.content:encoded:
【P1】工具类：字典

字典作为学习工具，基本功能要清楚。科技日新月异，电子字典的新功能也要会讲。不一定要把自己塑造成一个勤查字典的好学生，但不要把自己塑造成完全没用过字典的人。

Do you prefer paper dictionaries or electronic ones?

This is a no-brainer for me. Electronic dictionaries have my vote. I mean, who would still carry those weighty tomes in your bag and go to school or work in this information age? Electronic dictionaries are convenient to use and highly portable. Looking up an unfamiliar word has never been this easy.

How will dictionaries change in the future?

I’m currently satisfied with the ones I’m using. But I do expect some fancy new features added to them. I mean we’ve already had smartphones, smart watches and the like. How about a smart dictionary with auto translation of all texts we see all day every day?

What kinds of dictionaries have you used before?

I’ve basically used all kinds of them, esp. when I’ve been preparing for this test. English-english, thesauruses, dictionaries of idioms, collocations, phrasal verbs, usages. You name it.

How often do you use your dictionary?

I use my dictionary all day every day. I’ve already been used to looking up everything I bump into in my daily life, be it unfamiliar words, expressions or idioms. I can’t live without it.

【P2】人物类：运动员

Describe a sportsperson (athlete) you admire (you know)

  • Who he/she is
  • What he/she does
  • How you knew him/her
  • What you know about his/her life story
  • Why he is famous
  • And explain how much you know about sports

  1. 本题的A是a sportsperson (athlete)，描述人；B是you admire (you know)，限制属性。

  2. 提纲建议：

    • 描述这位运动员的基本信息，外貌、性格，引出最大特征及钦佩
    • 这位运动员的人生故事，成名经历，值得敬佩的地方
    • 对ta的评价，适当升华

  3. 时态建议：描述人物特征时用一般现在时，讲人生故事用过去时，最后的评价如果涉及到类别问题，可用一般现在时；述及对现在的影响可用现在完成时。

  4. 其他：sportsperson = athlete, admire < you know, 因此说成admire比较保险。

Sports are not my thing, and I’m often met with a confused or indifferent look when I reply “I don’t watch sports” to the regular icebreaker “What’s your favorite sport to watch?”.

But I do follow some sports news especially during an Olympic season. And here’s one talented and eccentric American figure skater that I really admire in the 2018 Winter Olympics – Adam Rippon, America’s new sweetheart. He won a bronze medal as part of the figure skating team event, thus becoming the first openly gay U.S. male athlete to win a medal in a Winter Olympics.

Rippon can figure skate better than the rest of us can walk. He finished strong in his Olympics debut, scoring high points in his free skate, which helped the U.S. take home the bronze in the team event. In the final round, he did his signature move, a Triple Lutz that he executes with both arms above his head, colloquially dubbed the “Rippon Lutz”.

He’s no regular athlete, or otherwise he wouldn’t have been this illustrious. Rippon was the first openly gay athlete to nab a spot on this year’s U.S. Olympics roster — a distinction he proudly owns. Before the Olympics began, Rippon made headlines for criticizing US Vice-President Mike Pence’s homophobic views and actually refused to meet him.

In addition, Rippon’s got a wry sense of humor which can be easily seen in his epic Tweets. And in an interview, he was asked what it’s like to be a gay athlete in sports. He said that it’s exactly like being a straight athlete. Lots of hard work but usually done with better eye brows. I mean, score!

【P2】地点类：安静之地

Describe a quiet place you found

  • Where it is
  • When you found it
  • How you found it
  • How often you go there
  • What it is like
  • And explain how you felt about the place
  1. 本题的A是a place，描述地点；B是quiet, you found，限制属性。
  2. 提纲建议：
    1. 描述此地的基本信息，地理位置、样貌，引出安静的特征
    2. 详述发现经过、安静的表现
    3. 对此地的感受
  3. 时态建议：描述地点位置、样貌时用一般现在时，讲故事用过去时，最后的评价如果涉及到类别问题，可用一般现在时；述及对现在的影响可用现在完成时。
  4. 其他：此题容易跑题。这里的B是quiet，一定要重点描述quiet的场面，不能一带而过，却把重点放在此地的其他方面。

wide／cozy／simple decorated／not far away from dorm

Well, this topic reminds me of the film I recently watched about a family being forced to live in silence while hiding from monsters with ultra-sensitive超灵敏 hearing in a post-apocalyptic后世界末日 world. If you haven’t seen it, I highly recommend it. Anyway, I wanna describe a much less frightening place. Actually, a quite peaceful one – our campus library.

I still remember how astonished I was the first time I went there and found out how perfectly tranquil宁静的 the atmosphere there was. That was on the orientation day, after registering, I rushed to the library which to me was supposed to be a place of pilgrimage for university students with the help of that printed campus map. And I wasn’t disappointed. With a splendidly huge metallic dome on the top, the building is outstanding from campus architectures. Stepping inside, I was welcomed by the scent of old books and I soon surprisingly found that it was equipped with a security check machine seen in airports, several multi-media rooms, and even a book sterilizer thing, like an oven.

But the part that impressed me the most was that the reading rooms where many students chose to study. Some were reading, contemplating, writing and scratching their heads, others typing and clicking. But surprisingly, silence filled my ears. It was so quiet that you could even hear the pin drop. People searching for books were almost tiptoeing, moving swiftly from one shelf to another. It felt magical, cause outside the building, freshmen were romping around, immersed in excitement.

Undoubtedly, this library has been the place I’ve been to the most regularly, even when I don’t need or want to study. Sitting there alone, hearing the rustling of papers and smelling the intriguing奇妙的 fragrance香味 of pages with my eyes closed feels like heaven to me. Well no wonder they say Paradise will be a kind of library. absorbing konwledges

【P2】地点类：爱与恨的建筑

Describe a building you like (dislike)

  • Where it is
  • What it is used for
  • What it looks like
  • And explain why you like or dislike it

  1. 本题的A是a building，描述建筑物；B是you like (dislike)，限制情感走向。

  2. 提纲建议：

    • 描述该建筑的基本信息，位置、外观、功能，引出亮点（最大特征）
    • 详述最大特征，评价喜爱（或讨厌）
    • 评价与升华

  3. 时态建议：描述建筑特征时用一般现在时，如果要讲故事用过去时，最后的评价如果涉及到类别问题，可用一般现在时；述及对现在的影响可用现在完成时。

  4. 其他：括号为不同版本的题目，不代表可以选择喜欢或不喜欢。若抽到括号内题目，则情感走向完全相反，但中性特征仍可保留。准备时可分别储备喜欢的点和不喜欢的点，考场上视情况选取。

I’d like to describe the Beijing Olympic Observation Tower which is the second tallest tower in Beijing after the Central Radio & TV Tower, and the fourth-tallest structure in the city. It was finished in 2015, 7 years after the Olympics, and soon became a new landmark of Beijing. It’s located in the north of Beijing, being part of the Olympic Green in the Chaoyang District.

From the outside it is quite striking, a very narrow cluster of five grass-like stems or five needle mushrooms and there is nothing else around it which really makes it stand out. Its design, with four smaller circular floors at different heights and positions below a larger top floor, is meant to echo the Olympic rings.

The name says it all, and it’s used for observing the city. Since it’s near the breathtaking Olympic Forest Park, one can enjoy an incredible panorama全景 of it, the lovely lake and luxuriant vegetation, etc. Thanks to it’s height, sceneries as far as the south 4th ring road can be spotted, well, when the air’s clear.

If you ask me whether I like it or not, I’ll say I’m on the fence about this one. On one hand, it’s an amazing architecture with its magnificence and height and everything, not to mention the breathtaking view you get on the opening observation deck on top. And it’s nearly always empty with few visitors. But on the other, it takes you 138 yuan to get in, which is not at all a steal and the reason why it’s rarely visited. Besides, the facilities inside are mysteriously worn out, with a 70’s vibe.

Having said that, if you already find yourself in the Olympic Park and you have a bunch of hours to spend, then I’ll recommend it.

【每日表达】句型、习语、小幽默

A be the most B C I’ve ever D

  • 这是一个用最高级表达情感的句子。She’s the prettiest girl I’ve ever met. 简简单单一个最高级，把这里的对象夸到了极致。这种最高级也许不是真实情况，毕竟，well，你看过了许多美女，也看过了许多美景，哪有那么容易就是“最”，对吧？但是，这种最高级还是经常出现在口语表达中，借以抒发某种感情。A是对象，B是个形容词，注意用最高级，C是对象所属的类别，D则是一个动词，注意用过去分词。Be动词注意根据时态进行变换。
  • The meditation rooms in the backyard are the quietest places I’ve ever been to.
  • Jia Ling is the most hilarious comedian I’ve ever seen!

make ends meet

tighten one’s belt

pinch pennies

  • 钱从哪来？应该是很多现代人最常思考的哲学命题。在这个人类究极问题被你回答出来之前，日子好像不太好过，好一点叫收支刚好抵平，凑合能过下去，这时候叫make ends meet, 再惨一点得拮据着过，勒紧裤腰带，tighten your belt, 再惨一点，零钱都得要挤挤才出得来，叫pinch pennies。祝大家发财。
  • Living in metropolises like Shanghai is so hard. I can roughly make ends meet.
  • You’ve been doing great with your job and promotion is around the corner. But you gotta tighten your belt before that.
  • In the Great Recession, people needed to pinch pennies to survive.
  • I don’t need to make ends meet 表示我的收支很宽裕 / have a tight budget

【P2技巧】P2拆题解析法

Desribe A that B

  • 文体段+特征段+拓展段
  • Describe a popular comic actor/actress in your country.
  • Describe a quiet place you found.
  • Describe someone who’s talkative.

I’m gonna describe one roommate of mine back in university, Jerry, an impressive blabbermouth, maybe the mouthiest person I’ve ever met. It’s a miracle that we remain friends, right? He is tall and lanky with huge glasses on his face, which make his face even smaller.

The story of how we met is a funny one. So, it was our orientation day, I got to see my 5 other roommates for the first time. We decided to have lunch together in the cramped campus canteen. Sitting around a table, we didn’t know how to start this conversation, cause we weren’t so familiar and things. But here comes Jerry, carrying his tray approaching. Well all I can say is that, unlike most of us, cracking a conversation with someone he doesn’t know isn’t really a task on him. He began by complaining about how hot the day was, and how tiny our dorm room was. Apparently, the girls sitting next table were the cutest he’d ever seen, and the food, tastiest he’d ever tasted. Just when you wonder where all these topics come from, he initiated a discussion about geopolitics of the Pacific. Amazing, huh?

So each meal we had together since that first day of university literally remained the same. When Jerry was not around, the deafening awkwardness kept creeping in. basically, we can’t live without him. Then one day, I know it was a bad idea, but I couldn’t help asking him about his eloquence and stuff. He told me that talking, to him, is a cakewalk, no matter with strangers or with friends & family. And for an extrovert like him, sharing everything is a must, or otherwise he would be depressed.

He’s my pal, and we’re gonna hang out no matter what, and I actually kind of like him for who he really is. As an introvert myself, he is the perfect role model I should talk to and learn from.

今日任务：根据以上讲解，选择一道自己已经练习过的P2，重新整理结构、突出重点，再次练习并录音。（可选择使用文体段+特征段+拓展段的结构）

【纠音跟读】常错辅音1

/ʒ/

  • pleasure usual television
  • occasion garage measure
  • He likes watching television in the garage as usual, which on any occasion is a guilty pleasure.

/ʤ/

  • joke danger strange
  • jade judge schedule
  • Jake made a strange schedule and told the judge a dangerous joke.

跟读

  • 音频第一遍为美式，第二遍为英式。注意录音中的单词读音、重音、连读、失去爆破、变音、弱读、语调及节奏感。

  • The Olympics represents the noble ideal of sports overcoming the barriers of politics with champion athletes of all nations gathering in the spirit of sportsmanship. However, the stakes go beyond who wins the gold medal. Shortly after each competition, nations begin to vie afresh for the bid to host the next game. Winning the vote to host is not merely an honor, it is a political conquest in global recognition. It also spins revenue from the influx of tourists, participants and Olympic related paraphernalia用具.

item.description: <h2 id="【P1】日常类：礼物"><a href="#【P1】日常类：礼物" class="headerlink"
item.content:encoded:
【P1】日常类：礼物

本题为日常类话题，问题集中在送礼物的场合、礼物类型价格、对礼物的态度等。注意积累自己送和收的礼物及常见礼物类型。

What kind of gifts do you prefer to receive? Why?

As a stationery buff, an extravagant diamond fountain pen would be nice. Thank you very much. I was kidding. I would feel understood if I received a regular but durable pen since I’ve been practicing calligraphy and the repairer botched my last one.

Do you like to send gifts to others?

I’m afraid not. Buying gifts for someone else never ranked as one of my top 10 most fun things to do. So unless it’s on some specially important occasions and for people I really care about, I’d rather not squander my time being excessively considerate.

Do you like to send expensive gifts?

Certainly not. I mean, sending expensive gifts means the recipient would better send back something even more expensive. And that would definitely add up. By the way, in China, receiving costly gifts without returning one is considered a major social faux pas. The price is still a bit over my budget

What kinds of gifts are popular in your country?

I suppose fine teas, fruit baskets and regional specialty foods are my top three here. Fine teas are a traditionally lavish gifts and some brands can be really expensive. Xinjiang nuts and dried fruits or any other treats from your hometown as gifts show your sincerity. Besides, you can never go wrong to send fruit baskets when visiting people.

Why do people send gifts?

Cause we care. About the wellbeing of our friends & family. You never give them gifts on their birthdays, anniversaries or any other special occasions involuntarily, don’t you?

【P2】人物类：喜剧演员

Describe a popular comic actor/actress in your country
You should say:
Who he/she is
How you knew him/her
What comedies have he/she performed
And explain why he/she is popular?

  1. 本题的A是a comic actor/actress, 描述人；B是popular+in your country，加以限制。
  • 描述这位comedian的基本信息，外貌、性格特征、作品等，引出popular
  • 介绍其主要作品、走红程度、走红原因
  • 评价与升华
  • 时态建议：描述人物特征、状态、走红程度、评价等均用一般现在时，若描述其做过的事、奋斗史等用过去时。
  1. 其他：注意本题的B，一定要详述popular（表现、原因等），否则算跑题。

Meet Papi Jiang, China’s number one internet celebrity, a comedian known for her comedy and parodies滑稽模仿 on video blogs, where she pokes fun at everyday topics. She’s basically the girl next door who has bangs前刘海, wears minimal makeup and keeps two cats.

The series of humorous video posts feature Papi mocking everything from relationships to regional accents in high pitched高音调的区域口音, rapid fire Mandarin. A popular example would be a video she released on the run up to China’s main holiday, Spring Festival which is also the first video I watched of her. The video, entitled, “Are You Ready For The Upcoming Spring Festival?”honed in on the dread在恐惧中磨练 that many young people feel when they arrive back in their hometowns, only to be rigorously严格 grilled by intrusive打扰的 and inquisitive family members. Like all of her posts, this one was embellished点缀 by curse words and contained her trademark catchphrase口号: “I am Papi Jiang, a woman who combines beauty and talent.” I can feel this vicarious替代的 agony from that kind of encounter. This spring festival, I was interrogated审问 nonstop by annoying relatives.

It’s estimated that she’s got 60 million followers across a range of platforms, making her a truly household name. Her popularity is the perfect reply to those comments about how Chinese people are lacking in a sense of humor. This down-to-earth朴实的 superstar has got this savage野蛮的 humor, complemented with hilarious滑稽的, gags and acting. And I guess this is her secret.

Stephen Chow Sing Chi is highly prestigious comedian in China, Chinese film director, actor, film producer, martial artist and charitarian

【P2】人物类：受欢迎人物

Describe a popular person (you know)
You should say:
Who this person is
What kind of person he/she is
Why you think this person is popular
How you got to know him/her
How this person influences the public
And explain how you feel about this person

  1. 本题的A是a person，描述人；B是popular，限制属性。
  2. 人物基本信息、外貌性格特征、引出popular
  3. 互动（认识的过程等）、popular的表现、如何影响大众
  4. 评价与升华
  5. 时态建议：以一般现在时为主即可，认识的过程用过去时，可辅之以完成时作为加分点。
  6. 其他：题目比较宽泛，自由度较高。与喜剧演员完美匹配。串题时注意不要出现其他题目的特征词。

I’m gonna describe Jane Zhang, a 34 year-old Chinese pop singer, who is arguably the most popular female singer born in mainland China. She is the first Chinese artist to sing on the Vitoria’s Secret Fashion Show and the first singer to be invited to appear in the Oprah Winfrey Show. You may get a hint of how popular she is by noticing that she’ got more than 40 million followers on Weibo, the Chinese equivalent of Twitter.

I first knew about her when she took part in a singing competition, Super Voice Girl in 2005. Back then, she was a girl next door, a quiet, intimidated local pub singer in Chengdu, a survivor of divorced parents like me, who would stand on the stage, singing Mariah Carey and Tina Turner. I didn’t notice this angelic girl until she sang Loving You and belted that signature whistle register, which won her this affectionate nickname “Dolphin Princess”. Although she didn’t win, she was the one who shot the stardom and managed to land a recording contract which started her incredible singing career.

She is such a versatile singer-songwriter who is probably the first talent show contestant ever to insist on singing English songs all the way to the finale. I still remember how the judge panel back then suggested that she sing more Chinese songs to attract more public votes. Well, she just smiled to those comments, paid no heed to them and performed one classic after another. Admittedly, it was because of her that I, a junior high student back then, decided to learn English harder. And more interestingly, an astonishingly number of singers began singing English songs in talent shows.

She has remained my favorite Chinese pop artist and a respected role model until now. whenever I get lost in what I do, I’m gonna be like, hold on to it, just like Jane once did in her twenties.

After receiving no athletic scholarship offers, he attended Harvard University

Jeremy Lin He unexpectedly led a winning turnaround with the New York Knicks in 2012, which generated a global craze known as “Linsanity“

He ended the injury-plagued season with a total of only 36 games played

On October 18, 2017, during the Nets’ season opener against the Indiana Pacers, Lin injured his right knee after he landed hard from a layup.

【P2】人物类：童年基友

Describe a childhood friend of yours (Describe a time you spent with your childhood friend)

  • When you met him/her
  • Where you met him/her
  • What you did with your friend
  • How you felt about the friend
  • (Who this friend is
  • How you knew about each other
  • What you did together

  • And explain why you remembered it.)

    . A是a friend, 描述人；B是childhood + of yours, 限定范围。可以解读为人物类题目，也可以解读为人物+事件类。

  • 描述童年基友的基本情况、外貌性格特征，最大特点
  • 扩大最大特点，分析或小故事
  • 总结，拓展现状，适当升华
  1. 时态建议：描述人物特征时用一般现在时，讲故事用过去时，最后的评价如果涉及到类别问题，可用一般现在时；述及对现在的影响可用现在完成时。
  2. 其他：注意括号中的版本，以事件为主，可以略讲人物特征。

I remember there’s this bad pun in Friends going “The ship that never sinks is friendship.” I’ll never forget the day I met my best friend. I was in fourth grade back then, and it was in one of the most tedious science classes. Before it began, I immediately noticed we had a new classmate. She had blonde streaks in her hair and glitter under her eyes, and she was as smiley as can be. I thought to myself, I have to be her friend. I later learned her name was Kelly and it wasn’t long before she was coming over after school almost every day and I was claiming her as my best friend.

There was something about our personalities that just clicked. She was always bubbly and upbeat and had a way of instantly changing my mood on a bad day. She became a part of my family and I a part of hers — we were inseparable.

But, we have a major difference. I was like, a star in school back then, arguably the best singer our elementary school has to offer. She, on the other hand, knows nothing about music. So the time I tried to teach her to read sheet music and sing Danny Boy for our chorus competition is a funny one. Trust me, that was a hilarious disaster. Turned out she was completely tone-deaf! Finding the right pitch is purgatory for her. No matter how many times we tried, she just couldn’t sing do re mi right. Having said that, we still had a great time singing together. Picture the best singer singing with the worst, and you’ll know. Heavenly harmony, right?

Luckily, she still remains my best friend, and although we hang out less often than before, she takes up a special spot in my heart. And that’s one thing I wouldn’t trade.

【纠音跟读】schwa的发音

发音

  • /ə/
  • above
  • -ous -a
  • nervous serious famous dangerous vigorous蓬勃, 大力, 积极 glorious辉煌, 锦绣, 美好
  • idea China America agenda议程 extra
  • The famous scientist is making me nervous cause he’s dangerous, serious and too vigorous.
  • I have no idea about the trade war between China and America and their extra agenda.

勇气

The courage of life is often a less dramatic spectacle场面 than the courage of a final moment; but it is no less a magnificent华丽的 mixture of triumph and tragedy. A man does what he must - in spite of personal consequences, in spite of obstacles and dangers and pressures - and that is the basis of all human morality.

【每日表达】句型、习语、小幽默

twiddle one’s thumbs
fidget with something
fiddle with (something)

人生就是有永远都开不完的会，领导在上面大放厥词，blablabla，你在台下坐着，思考等会儿要吃什么。领导没有要停的意思，于是你只好开始摆弄手机，又不能被HR看见，只好玩自己的手指。这种无聊、烦躁或紧张的时候开始摆弄什么东西，就可以用这三个表达法，非常生动。

  • After my Gaokao, I spent every day twiddling my thumbs and waiting for the postman.
  • That was the first time I met my girlfriend’s dad, and I was idly fidgeting with my phone before he came.
  • After getting my first watch, I was so excited that I fiddled with it and broke it.
Confucius says
Confucius he says

这是一个很棒的小幽默。你一定认为后面应该加一句论语里面的话对不对？那你就错了，后面可以加任何话，达成的效果就比较像“有位哲人曾经说过，少一点套路，多一点真诚。”这种感觉，对方会认为这个玩笑很可爱。不过还要看自己是不是这种人设哦。
I miss being a student so much. You know Confucius says, adults are just wrinkled kids who owe money. (讲完之后吐个舌头)

【P1技巧】更多原则

原则一: 开门见山 Get to the point!

  • Do you like outdoor activities?
  • Eg.1 Well, when I was young, my mom told me that it’s important that I prioritize my health and that I practice emotional hygiene. Then, I always take care of myself following my mom’s instructions and that’s why I enjoy doing outdoor activities a lot.
  • Eg.2 I enjoy outdoor activities a lot, causeyou know when I was young, …

原则二：讲实话 Tell the truth!

  • Do you like outdoor activities?
  • Eg. not al all. I mean, I’m a homebody喜欢在家消遣的男人；家庭第一主义的男人, and wheneverI have a chance to unwind放松, I’m gonna stay at home binge狂欢 watching my favorite reality show, The Running Man.

原则三：个人化与趣味化 Make it personal and interesting!

  • Do you like outdoor activities?
  • Eg Well, outdoor activities are my thing, cause as so you know, I am president of the hiking society in my university and we organize hiking tours to nearby mountains in the suburbs of Beijing every week. You’re more than welcome to join us.

今日任务：运用P1四大原则（三句话、开门见山、讲实话、个人化与趣味化）：

  1. Are you close to all of your family members?
  2. Why are some people always late?
  3. Would you like to have your own boat?

item.title: Day 3 Speaking
item.description: <h2 id="【P1】市政类：交通"><a href="#【P1】市政类：交通" class="headerlink"
item.content:encoded:
【P1】市政类：交通

本题为市政类话题，主要集中在城市公共交通建设、私家车现状、远距离轨道交通、飞机等。答题前可试着总结各交通工具的优缺点和异同，即可轻松应对。

Do you like travelling by train, by car or by air? Why?

Call me old fashioned, but, Travelling by train has my vote. I’m kind of claustrophobic幽闭恐惧症, and being trapped in the narrow space and seat of a cabin drives me crazy. Don’t get me started on how terrible it is to get carsick. Travelling by train, you get to hear the rumbling rhythm and enjoy the scenery outside.

What’s the most popular means of transportation in your hometown?

I suppose it’s gotta be private vehicles since the public transportation system’s a mess. And for a super tiny city like my hometown, traffic congestion is weirdly common. I guess that says something, doesn’t it?

How often do you travel by train?

When I’m traveling, the train is always my priority in terms of transportation. So like 3 or 4 times a year, give or take.

Do people in your city travel by taxi?

Well, some of them do commute by taxi, mostly when they can get a reimbursement for the travel expenses. but in rush hours, it’s never a good idea.

When was the last time you took a taxi?

It was like 2 hours ago when I took a taxi from my hotel to get here. And to be honest, I had been so nervous that I almost couldn’t fall asleep last night and got up a little bit late this morning. I had no choice.

Is traveling by train very popular in China?

You can say that. The high-speed rail is actually the best choice for a long-distance journey. It’s estimated that Chinese people will make nearly 400 million train trips over Chinese New Year.

Should everyone have their own car?

Well, not necessarily. Not everyone is suitable to drive. like me. I have such a bad sense of direction, and I will make a terrible driver. Besides, Driving should not be encouraged, considering rampant adj. (尤指疾病, 社会弊端)猖獗的; 无法控制的 air pollutions in the world. But of course, you can always turn to one of those electric cars. aggravate the problem of traffice congestion and the rampant air pollutions

Would you ride bikes to work in the future?

That’s exactly what I am doing on a daily basis. I ride to work with a cheerful mood esp. when seeing those vehicles stuck in the traffic. I will definitely keep doing it.

【P2】人物类：名人

Describe a famous person (that you are interested in) (in your country).
You should say:
Who this person is;
How you know this person;
How this person looks like;
What this person is famous for;
What sort of life they had before they became famous;
How this person became famous;
And explain why you like this person. (why he/she is important)
提示：

  1. 本题的A是a person，描述人；B是famous, (that you’re interested in, in your country)，限制属性（地点）。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 描述此人的基本信息，外貌、性格特征，引出famous
    • 详述成名过程、成名前故事等
    • 对ta的评价，为何喜欢，适当升华
  3. 时态建议：描述人物特征时用一般现在时，讲故事用过去时，最后的评价如果涉及到类别问题，可用一般现在时；述及对现在的影响可用现在完成时。
  4. 其他：说一位国内有名的人，即可涵盖所有题目版本

Ok, I’m gonna describe this highly controversial celebrity in China, Jin Xing, China’s most popular TV hostess, who has been many things in her life: dance prodigy, prima ballerina,decorated colonel in the People’s Liberation Army, choreographer, actress, wife and mother of three. She also has been a man.

She often appears on TV an elegant woman with artistic temperament with black curly hair and a long crooked nose. It’s actually hard to tell what she has been through. As early as 4 she felt she was different, not just in terms of her sexual identity but also thanks to a precocious talent for dance that would, by age 9, result in her admission to a prestigious troupe within the People’s Army. She not only was trained hard to be a ballerina, but became proficient with machine guns and learned how to place bombs delicately under bridges as well.

Jin went to New York to study modern dance, traveled and performed in Europe, and taught dance in Rome, followed by a world tour, and returned to China in her twenties. Then she underwent sex reassignment surgery after which her left leg was paralyzed for a while. Soon she established her own modern dance troupe, got married and adopted 3 sons. Her career as a TV personality skyrocketed thanks to regular appearances as a judge on a local version of So You Think You Can Dance, where she was a fan favorite for her withering takedowns and won the nick name “poisonous tongue”. Her popularity eventually led to The Jin Xing Show, a wildly successful talk show — it’s viewed by an estimated 100 million every week.

She’s probably my favorite Chinese celebrity for her unparallel courage to go through gender transformation, let alone her superb talent in dancing, hosting and language learning. That’s right, she speaks 7 languages.

【P2】人物类 话唠

Describe someone who is talkative
You should say:
Who this person is
How you knew this person
What this person likes to talk about
How you feel about this person

  1. 本题的A是someone, 描述人；B是talkative, 属性。基本思路为两类：拓展talkative属性并举例；以故事体现talkative。
  2. 提纲建议：
    • 人物基本信息、外貌性格特征，引出talkative总述
    • 详述talkative的表现，或讲故事
    • 对ta的感受、评价，适当升华
  3. 时态建议：描述人物特征时用一般现在时，讲故事用过去时，最后的评价如果涉及到类别问题，可用一般现在时；述及对现在的影响可用现在完成时。
  4. 其他：talkative本身是中性词，不见得必须理解为贬义的“话唠”，也可以理解为健谈、爱说话等。描述时注意使用talkative的各种同义词，尽量避免反复。

I’m gonna describe one roommate of mine back in university, Jerry, an impressive blabbermouth长舌者, maybe the mouthiest person I’ve ever met. It’s a miracle that we remain friends, right? He is tall and lanky with huge glasses on his face, which make his face even smaller.

The story of how we met is a funny one. So, it was our orientation day, I got to see my 5 other roommates for the first time. We decided to have lunch together in the cramped campus canteen. Sitting around a table, we didn’t know how to start this conversation, cause we weren’t so familiar and things. But here comes Jerry, carrying his tray approaching. Well all I can say is that, unlike most of us, cracking a conversation with someone he doesn’t know isn’t really a task on him. He began by complaining about how hot the day was, and how tiny our dorm room was. Apparently, the girls sitting next table were the cutest he’d ever seen, and the food, tastiest he’d ever tasted. Just when you wonder where all these topics come from, he initiated a discussion about geopolitics of the Pacific. Amazing, huh?

So each meal we had together since that first day of university literally remained the same. When Jerry was not around, the deafening awkwardness kept creeping in. basically, we can’t live without him. Then one day, I know it was a bad idea, but I couldn’t help asking him about his eloquence and stuff. He told me that talking, to him, is a cakewalk, no matter with strangers or with friends & family. And for an extrovert like him, sharing everything is a must, or otherwise he would be depressed.

He’s my pal, and we’re gonna hang out no matter what, and I actually kind of like him for who he really is. As an introvert myself, he is the perfect role model I should talk to and learn from.

I’ve got the juiciest bit of gossip ever/would be boring without gossip

【P1技巧】三句话原则+公式

Make it three sentences. 这是回答P1的第一大原则。所有问题，再简单，都一定要答到三句话以上。除非只是考官随口一问的题目，比如 My I have your full name please? How are you today? 这类程序性问题。

而复杂一些的问题则需要动用四句话公式，以后会讲到。若碰到显然应该在P3出现的问题，那么就做好准备用P3的思路和方案作答，比如比较类的Please compare traveling by train and by air。这里的三句话指的不是三个句号，而是三个包含谓语动词的独立意思。如果能用连词等把三句话连成一句话，那是更好的。

三句话公式：intro + e.g.1 + e.g.2 或 intro + e.g. + outro
e.g. Are there many facilities in the place you live in?

Definitely. There are so many amenities in our community, like a swimming pool, a bank, a supermarket, a playgound and a health center (1). I feel so lucky to live here (2) and I can’t think of any other places more convenient than this one. (3)

今日任务：用三句话原则和公式回答以下问题。

  • Do you like your major/job?
  • Do parents give children pocket money in China?

【每日表达】习语、谚语、小幽默

be a rip-off

be a steal

这一组表达法用来表示东西太贵/太便宜非常棒，而且很日常。第一个rip off动词短语原意是指偷或骗，这里加上“-”之后变为名词，表示“简直就是在偷，或者骗”，指的是这个东西的价格太贵太离谱，就跟骗钱似的。而第二个表达法的steal原意也是偷，但这里指的是这个东西价格低得太离谱，就跟不要钱、偷来的似的。厉害吧？

What? You spent $ 400 on that damn shirt? You crazy? That’s a complete rip-off!

What? You spent $ 4 on that beautiful shirt? You crazy? That’s a steal!

bird of a different feather

咱身边总有些特立独行、思想独到的人，这样的人可以叫bird of a different feather. 羽毛不同色的鸟儿。这句表达法来自于英文谚语Birds of a feather flock together. 羽毛颜色相同的鸟儿总是成群飞舞，比喻人以群分，小团体抱团，不接受异己。

Jin Xing, a bird of a different feather, realized she was different at the age of four.

【纠音跟读】常错元音3

/ɔ/
/аʊ/
/o/ ([əʊ])
saw law tall hall all
sow low toll hope oh
sour loud doubt how down town house
I saw a tall building with a hall full of loud people.
Lack of all hopes and doubts makes life sour.
Living in downtown with so many toll-houses.

音频第一遍为美式，第二遍为英式。注意录音中的重音、连读、失去爆破、变音、弱读、语调及节奏感。

What makes a home? Love and sympathy and confidence. It is a place where kindly affections exist among all the members of the family.The parents take good care of their children, and the children are interested in the activities of their parents. Thus all of them are bound together by affection, and they find their home to be the cheeriest place in the world.

item.title: Day 2 Speaking
item.description:
sleep

本题为日常类话题，答案贴合日常、讲出真实情况即可，不要一味求新求变。时态以现在时为主。
item.content:encoded:
sleep

本题为日常类话题，答案贴合日常、讲出真实情况即可，不要一味求新求变。时态以现在时为主。

How many hours do you sleep every day? Why?

I need to sleep at least 8 hours every day including a cozy catnap after lunch, or otherwise I would feel drowsy(昏昏欲睡的) all day long. insomnia失眠症

Is it necessary to have enough sleep?

If you wanna keep fit, this is my primal suggestion. Enough sleep means a complete rest for all body functions so that you can easily enjoy a faster metabolism（代谢） and own a better physique.

Is taking a nap important?

At least for me, yes. A short but sound nap after lunch every day esp after taking in a bit much starch which increases your blood sugar is essential. I can be reassured（安心） that I will have a productive afternoon after taking one.

Do old people sleep a lot?

As far as I know, no. Old people sleep much less than their younger generations or themselves in younger years. like my nanny who sleeps only 3 hours every day with the help of some hypnotics（安眠药）.

Do you think staying up late is a good thing?

It never is. Staying up destroys your biological clock and your body’s circadian rhythm 昼夜节律 would be in a mess. Having said that, I know how it feels to live in this modern society with excessive work to do, even at night.

good parent

Describe someonewho you think is a good parent.
You should say:
Who the person is
What kind of person he/she is
How you know this person
Why you think he/she is good
And explain how youfeel about him/her (why you think they are a good parent).

  1. 本题的A是someone，描述人；B是whoyou think is a good parent，限制此人的身份和属性。可以理解为人物+事件结合类题目，也可理解为单纯人物类题目。
  2. 提纲建议：
    a) 描述这位parent的基本信息，外貌、性格，引出好家长的最大特征
    b) 详述好家长原因或小故事
    c) 对ta的评价，适当升华
  3. 时态建议：描述人物特征时用一般现在时，讲故事用过去时，最后的评价如果涉及到类别问题，可用一般现在时；述及对现在的影响可用现在完成时。
    其他：好家长可以是自己家长，也可以是地球上任何一个孩子爸妈，所以不必拘泥于人物选择。比如串题时用一个名人（受欢迎的人）的材料，ta同时也上过“爸爸去哪儿”之类的节目显示出好家长的一面，也OK。

I can’t think of a better epitome缩影 of parenting than my mom. She is THE perfect parent. My mom is 53 years old, but she looks so young that when we stroll two abreast, we are often misrecognized as siblings. Without doubt, I consider her the best parent in the world because she’s always respected me and loved me for who I am in my childhood and during my formative years.

You know how kids are often picky eater right? Broccoli, green peppers and tomatoes used to be utterly repulsive厌恶 for me, and unlike other moms who authoritatively forced their kids to eat them, my mom didn’t think of it as a serious matter and told me that I needed to eat other vegetables sufficiently instead, because her theory is fibers and vitamins are something we’re looking for in vegetables, and there are tons of vegetables full of these nutrients as long as parents don’t make the mistake of substituting unhealthy foods. She thinks that it isn’t a big deal that children develop food preferences and go through them in stages. So in my home, we never turn mealtimes into unpleasant occasions. And since I could never find any junk food at home, fried chicken, chips and candy bars were never in my diet. Interestingly, the vegetables I hated back then are my favorites now.

Aside from that, my mom always fostered my independence. She thinks that children push for independence because it is part of human nature to want to feel in control rather than to feel controlled by someone else. And that’s why she never entered my room without knocking first, never read my diaries, supported my part-time jobs and my choice of girlfriends.

I’ve become this confident, altruistic independent man because of her. I am so grateful that I could be able have her in my life. I love my mom.

I’ve had better

表达“不喜欢、不乐意、不稀罕甚至糟糕透顶了”的委婉讲法，“嗯，我见过听过试过用过吃过更好的呢”，一点点傲娇，a little bit sassy，这就是这个表达法所显露出的心里状态。而这里的动词had也可以换成其他动词的过去分词，如I’ve seen better. I’ve heard better, etc. 另外，还可以在better后面继续加名词，如I’ve had better days.
To be honest, I’ve heard better ones. The three characters in my name are all mediocre.

  • How’s the new job treating you?
    Well, I’ve had better.
    The whole point of Silicon Valley is that people jump between jobs to kind of mix up the pot.

from scratch

从零开始，白手起家。这种从无到有的过程，就叫from scratch。它的愿意是指做菜的时候所有材料都自备，而不是使用店里卖的半成品。后来则所有从零开始做的事情都可以用这个表达形容。

Jack Ma, my favorite celebrity in China, a former English tutor, built his company from scratch.

After knowing that we needed to work on this project, we did it from scratch, searching for materials, preparing for the presentation, etc.

Voc

  • 第一组
    • Introvert/Introverted/Extrovert/Extroverted/Ambivert(n. 既外向又内向的人；中间性格者)
  • 第二组
    • Egoist(n.利己主以者)/Egoistic(adj. 自我中心的,自私自利的)/Egoism(n. 利己主义，自我主义)
    • Egotist(n. 自高自大者；言必称“我”者)/Egotistic(adj. 自我本位的;自负的)/Egotism(n. 自负；自我中心)/Egocentric(adj. 自我中心的,自私自利的)
    • Egocentrism(n.自我中心)/Egomaniac(n. 极端利己主义者,极端自我主义者,自大狂)/Egomaniacal(adj. 极端利己的)/Egomania(n. 自大狂；极端利己；利己癖；[医]自尊癖)
    • Altruist(n. 爱他主义者；利他主义者)/Altruistic(adj. 利他的,无私心的)/Altruism(n. 利他；利他主义)

Eg. Do you have many friends?

Well, as an introvert myself, I don’t think that’s how it works. I mean, I’m not an egoist or something. I just feel uncomfortably intimidated hanging out with a large group of people.

  1. Are you close to all of your family members? 【P1】

    . 使用以上语汇描述P2中自选的一个人物，不少于4句话。

item.title: Day 1[P1/P2]study and efficency/visitor
item.description:
本题为日常类话题，答案贴合日常、讲出真实情况即可，不要一味求新求变。时态以现在时为主。祝你在准备这道题的同时，也能提高学习效率哦！

独家范例：（满分素材）
item.content:encoded:
本题为日常类话题，答案贴合日常、讲出真实情况即可，不要一味求新求变。时态以现在时为主。祝你在准备这道题的同时，也能提高学习效率哦！

独家范例：（满分素材）

P1 学习与效率

cdn

Do you prefer to study in the morning or in the evening?

I prefer to study in the morning when most people at my age are still in their sweet dreams, right? I get to breathe in fresh and brisk air and enjoy mild temperature. besides, there are not so many distractions in the morning so that I can concentrate harder and be more productive.

Why do some people find it difficult to focus in the morning?

Modern people are so used to burning the midnight oil, working on their emails or assignments. I mean, young people hardly hit the sack before midnight, right? studying in the morning means less sleep, and trust me, that doesn’t feel good.

What do you do to improve your learning efficiency?

I mean, I could really use a cup of coffee sometimes, knowing that it isn’t the healthiest choice. But it works. A cup of latte keeps my torpor away.

P2 访客

cdn

Describe a visitor in your home (a time when someone visited your home)**

  • Who this visitor was
  • When he/she visited your home
  • What you did
  • Why he/she came to visit
  • And explain whether you like him/her or not
    提示：
  1. 本题的A是a visitor，描述人；B是in your home，限制地点。实质上是人物+事件结合类题目。
  2. 提纲建议：
    a) 描述这位visitor的基本信息，外貌、性格特征，以及事件时间地点
    b) 详述拜访过程、原因，把故事讲清
    c) 对ta的评价，适当升华
  3. 时态建议：描述人物特征时用一般现在时，讲故事用过去时，最后的评价如果涉及到类别问题，可用一般现在时；述及对现在的影响可用现在完成时。
  4. 其他：若抽到括号内题目，则可略过人物特征，直接讲故事。

You know how hard it is to deal with a bragger right? I’m gonna describe an unexpected visitor to my home last February who turned out an annoying one. It was Mrs. Huang, a family friend. She is a tall and gaunt woman in her fifties now and she always wears this tacky yellowish gem in front of her chest.

So, the awful story began on the last day of spring festival, or the Lantern’s Day, when traditionally customary visits are supposed to end. Our heroine dropped by out of the bluewhen my whole family were cleaning the living room. She barged through the door the second it was opened, rushed in and began bragging about her new property insurance. She tried to talk my mom into buying one. I was like, come on, are you a newly employed insurance saleswoman or what. Not a professional one, apparently. I couldn’t help grinning about this idea which she noticed and then turned to me. Uh-oh, here we go. It was no surprise that she began boasting about how brilliant her son was. So her son was going to get his green card and get married both in the summer, blah blah blah. What did all that have anything to do with me? I thought, I had actually been very close to Jimmy, her son, and I knew that there’s another side of the story. Anyhow, I kept smiling with curt nods, thinking, what I was gonna eat for my lunch.

You see, braggers are a lot to handle, esp. an overproud mom who goes around crowing about her life. But when it comes to visiting other people, manners really matter.

Skills

  • give or take
    说数字的时候是不是老觉得想表达个“左右”这个意思？这种情况下，这个习语是最好用的了，它表示“或多或少，大概”这一意思，直接接在在一个不太确切的数字后面再加一个上下浮动的空间，或放在数字后面都可以。
    My hometown has a population of 3 million people, give or take a few thousand.
    Ning Zetao has been followed by 20 million fans on Weibo, give or take.

  • to add insult to injury 火上浇油 负面的

  • to add fuel to the fire/flames 火上浇油 负面的

  • to ice the cake 给蛋糕上糖霜，听起来就很可口，是正面的，“锦上添花”这个意思

  • icing on the cake 最后一个则是名词性的，指“锦上添的那个花”。注意它们的定冠词
    The meal was perfect, the wonderful view from the restaurant the icing on the cake.
    The meal was perfect. To ice the cake, we got to enjoy the wonderful view from the restaurant.
    I can’t live with unpunctual people. Like my friend Mike. So last Sunday we had a date. First of all he arrived an hour late and then, to add insult to injury, he proceeded to complain about my choice of restaurant.
    The traffic in Beijing is already miserable. To add fuel to the fire, it rains particularly heavily lately.

link

  • Do you like to send expensive gifts?
    Certainly not. I mean, sending expensive gifts means the recipient would better send back something even more expensive. And that would definitely add up. By the way, in China, receiving costly gifts without returning one is considered a major social faux pas.
    这个答案你第一时间应该想不到，但这个你一定能想到：
    I don’t like to send expensive gifts. I’m a student now. I don’t have enough money. Someone sends me an expensive gift. More expensive than I can take. I feel terrified. I don’t know how to think of a way to respond.
    那么我们下面就来做加法，把简单答案连起来吧。
    I don’t like to send expensive gifts because I’m a student now and I don’t have enough money. So if someone sent me an expensive gift which is more expensive than I can take, I would feel so terrified that I wouldn’t know how to think of a way to respond.

今日任务：

  • 结合本季P1~P3题目，用Skil造句，并拓展成段落。
  • 回答以下问题，答案不要超过2个句号（那就表示，要尽量连接起来！）
    What was your favorite sport when you were young?
    Do you think it’s important to be punctual?

item.title: Interview topics for SDE
item.description: JAVA
item.content:encoded:
JAVA

基础题

  1. String s = new String(“abc”) 创建了几个对象

  2. 死锁

  3. HashMap什么情况下发生死链

    HashTable/hashMap-线程不安全 区别

  4. Java什么时候会发生内存泄漏-heap leak

    • Unclosed Streams or Unclosed Connections 资源未关闭（数据库连接／文件）

      • auto-close feature introduced in Java 8

    • Adding Objects with no hashCode() and equals() into a HashSet

      • hashCode() and equals() implementations

    • Static Field Holding On to the Object Reference 单例对象

    • Calling String.intern() on Long String

      • -XX:MaxPermSize=

      • interned String objects are stored in PermGen space

      • use Java 8 – where the PermGen space is replaced by the Metaspace – which won’t lead to any OutOfMemoryError when using internon Strings

    • 容器中的对象

    • 堆外内存

    Do Profiling: Visual VM

  5. JAVA：OutOfMemoryError（OOM）

    • 堆内存溢出
    • 虚拟机栈和本地方法栈溢出
      • -Xss设置栈大小 -Xoss设定本地方法栈大小
      • 不能减少线程或者换64虚拟机，减少最大堆和减少栈容量
    • 方法区和运行时常量池溢出（permGen）
      • -XX：Permsize/ -XX:MaxPermSize

  6. 分布式高并发系统如何保证对外接口的幂等性

    • 在请求参数中附带唯一标识作为唯一键，唯一标识可以由业务字段加时间戳低位拼接而成，或者使用snowflake、UUID等ID生成算法
    • 找出业务本身的唯一约束。比如一个客户对同一只新股只能申购一次，那么客户号加申购代码可以组成唯一键，去重表

多线程

  • Running：执行中。
  • Ready：除了CPU，其他资源都ok，等待调度。
  • Blocked：等待某个事件，如键盘输入。

  • 状态切换（4种情况）：

    • Ready到Running：CPU调度到了，开始执行
    • Running到Ready：时间片到了
    • Running到Blocked：执行到某个时候，需要等待某个事件
    • Blocked到Ready：等待的事件发生了，进入Ready等待CPU调度

  • 在性能上来说，如果竞争资源不激烈，两者的性能是差不多的，而当竞争资源非常激烈时（即有大量线程同时竞争），此时ReentrantLock的性能要远远优于synchronized。所以说，在具体使用时要根据适当情况选择。在JDK1.5中，synchronized是性能低效的。因为这是一个重量级操作，它对性能最大的影响是阻塞的是实现，挂起线程和恢复线程的操作都需要转入内核态中完成，这些操作给系统的并发性带来了很大的压力。相比之下使用Java提供的ReentrankLock对象，性能更高一些。到了JDK1.6，发生了变化，对synchronize加入了很多优化措施，有自适应自旋，锁消除，锁粗化，轻量级锁，偏向锁等等。导致在JDK1.6上synchronize的性能并不比Lock差。官方也表示，他们也更支持synchronize，在未来的版本中还有优化余地，所以还是提倡在synchronized能实现需求的情况下，优先考虑使用synchronized来进行同步。

JVM

内存模型

  • 堆内存（Heap Memory）： 存放Java对象

    -堆内存模型

    堆内存由垃圾回收器的自动内存管理系统回收。
    堆内存分为两大部分：新生代和老年代。比例为1：2。
    老年代主要存放应用程序中生命周期长的存活对象。
    新生代又分为三个部分：一个Eden区和两个Survivor区，比例为8：1：1。
    Eden区存放新生的对象。
    Survivor存放每次垃圾回收后存活的对象。

    Java虚拟机（这里默认指Hotspot）采用的是分代回收算法

  • 非堆内存（Non-Heap Memory）： 存放类加载信息和其它meta-data

  • 其它（Other）： 存放JVM 自身代码等

分代回收算法（复制算法和标记整理算法）

  • 复制算法：适用于存活对象很少。回收对象多-新生代
  • 标记整理算法: 适用用于存活对象多，回收对象少-老年代

堆区划分为老年代（Old Generation）和新生代（Young Generation），老年代的特点是每次垃圾收集时只有少量对象需要被回收，而新生代的特点是每次垃圾回收时都有大量的对象需要被回收

参考：http://jayfeng.com/2016/03/11/理解Java垃圾回收机制/

为什么不是一块Survivor空间而是两块？

这里涉及到一个新生代和老年代的存活周期的问题，比如一个对象在新生代经历15次（仅供参考）GC，就可以移到老年代了。问题来了，当我们第一次GC的时候，我们可以把Eden区的存活对象放到Survivor A空间，但是第二次GC的时候，Survivor A空间的存活对象也需要再次用Copying算法，放到Survivor B空间上，而把刚刚的Survivor A空间和Eden空间清除。第三次GC时，又把Survivor B空间的存活对象复制到Survivor A空间，如此反复。
所以，这里就需要两块Survivor空间来回倒腾。

为什么Eden空间这么大而Survivor空间要分的少一点？

新创建的对象都是放在Eden空间，这是很频繁的，尤其是大量的局部变量产生的临时对象，这些对象绝大部分都应该马上被回收，能存活下来被转移到survivor空间的往往不多。所以，设置较大的Eden空间和较小的Survivor空间是合理的，大大提高了内存的使用率，缓解了Copying算法的缺点。
我看8：1：1就挺好的，当然这个比例是可以调整的，包括上面的新生代和老年代的1：2的比例也是可以调整的。
新的问题又来了，从Eden空间往Survivor空间转移的时候Survivor空间不够了怎么办？直接放到老年代去。

Eden空间和两块Survivor空间的工作流程

现在假定有新生代Eden，Survivor A， Survivor B三块空间和老生代Old一块空间。

触发GC的类型

了解这些是为了解决实际问题，Java虚拟机会把每次触发GC的信息打印出来来帮助我们分析问题，所以掌握触发GC的类型是分析日志的基础。

GC_FOR_MALLOC: 表示是在堆上分配对象时内存不足触发的GC。
GC_CONCURRENT: 当我们应用程序的堆内存达到一定量，或者可以理解为快要满的时候，系统会自动触发GC操作来释放内存。
GC_EXPLICIT: 表示是应用程序调用System.gc、VMRuntime.gc接口或者收到SIGUSR1信号时触发的GC。
GC_BEFORE_OOM: 表示是在准备抛OOM异常之前进行的最后努力而触发的GC。

Algorithm

  1. 求第K大的数
    • 快速排序

Https

  1. 交互流程

  • 为什么需要交换密钥
    1. 防止中间人攻击
    2. RSA 加密效率较低（密钥交换时的非对称解密计算量占整个握手过程的 90% 以上）
    3. RSA 加密的明文长度不超过密钥长度。明文过长需自己处理分段加密（比如现在常用的公钥长度是 2048 位，意味着待加密内容不能超过 256 个字节。）
  • 建议优先支持 RSA 和 ECDHE_RSA 密钥交换算法

  1. 防止盗链

    • use https
    • 接口参数设置

    data=xxx&tm=xxx&sign=xxxx

    data: 按照字母顺序／tm:时间戳／sign:data + tm + salt（固定字符串）

    采用MD5 校验，或者其他AES／DES／非对称算法加密

    • 本地加密混淆
      • Android 加入so，so对签名进行认证，监听调试端口，禁止模拟器运行（模拟器的一些参数),so需要混淆，函数结构混淆
    • 服务端简单限制
      • user-agent 和 refer 限制，甚至用socket
      • 验证登录，cookie／session／token
    • 接口请求频次
      • 不建议封ip，共用ip出口比较多
      • deviceId最好服务器生成，本地容易伪造
    • 返回结果加密
    • 定期检查访问日志

Android 中的设计模式

  • Context/Java Stream的装饰者/包装者模式（Decorator/Wrapper pattern）
  • 适配器模式（ListView等的Adapter）

前端

跨域问题

1、同源策略

同源策略限制从一个源加载的文档或脚本如何与来自另一个源的资源进行交互。

一个源的定义：如果协议，端口（如果指定了一个）和主机对于两个页面是相同的，则两个页面具有相同的源。

2、主域相同的跨域

document.domain的场景只适用于不同子域的框架间的交互，及主域必须相同的不同源。

3、完全不同源的跨域（两个页面之间的通信）
3.1、通过location.hash跨域

假设域名a.com下的文件cs1.html要和jianshu.com域名下的cs2.html传递信息。
1、cs1.html首先创建自动创建一个隐藏的iframe，iframe的src指向jianshu.com域名下的cs2.html页面。
2、cs2.html响应请求后再将通过修改cs1.html的hash值来传递数据。
3、同时在cs1.html上加一个定时器，隔一段时间来判断location.hash的值有没有变化，一旦有变化则获取获取hash值。

注：由于两个页面不在同一个域下IE、Chrome不允许修改parent.location.hash的值，所以要借助于a.com域名下的一个代理iframe。

优点：1.可以解决域名完全不同的跨域。2.可以实现双向通讯。
缺点：location.hash会直接暴露在URL里，并且在一些浏览器里会产生历史记录，数据安全性不高也影响用户体验。另外由于URL大小的限制，支持传递的数据量也不大。有些浏览器不支持onhashchange事件，需要轮询来获知URL的变化。

3.2、通过window.name跨域

window对象有个name属性，该属性有个特征：即在一个窗口(window)的生命周期内,窗口载入的所有的页面都是共享一个window.name的，每个页面对window.name都有读写的权限，window.name是持久存在一个窗口载入过的所有页面中的。window.name属性的神奇之处在于name 值在不同的页面（甚至不同域名）加载后依旧存在（如果没修改则值不会变化），并且可以支持非常长的 name 值（2MB）。

如果是与iframe通信的场景就需要把iframe的src设置成当前域的一个页面地址。

3.3、通过window.postMessage跨域

HTML5为了解决这个问题，引入了一个全新的API：跨文档通信 API（Cross-document messaging）。这个API为window对象新增了一个window.postMessage方法，允许跨窗口通信，不论这两个窗口是否同源。

4、AJAX请求不同源的跨域
4.1、通过JSONP跨域

基本原理：网页通过添加一个script元素，向服务器请求JSON数据，这种做法不受同源政策限制；服务器收到请求后，将数据放在一个指定名字的回调函数里传回来。例子如下：

优点：简单适用，老式浏览器全部支持，服务器改造小。不需要XMLHttpRequest或ActiveX的支持。
缺点：只支持GET请求。

4.2、通过WebSocket跨域

WebSocket是一种通信协议，使用ws://（非加密）和wss://（加密）作为协议前缀。该协议不实行同源政策，只要服务器支持，就可以通过它进行跨源通信。

4.3、通过CORS跨域

跨源资源共享标准( cross-origin sharing standard) 使得以下场景可以使用跨站 HTTP 请求：

  • 1、使用 XMLHttpRequest 或 Fetch发起跨站 HTTP 请求。
  • 2、Web 字体 (CSS 中通过 @font-face 使用跨站字体资源)，因此，网站就可以发布 TrueType 字体资源，并只允许已授权网站进行跨站调用。
  • 3、WebGL 贴图
  • 4、使用drawImage绘制
  • 5、Images/video 画面到canvas.
  • 6、样式表（使用 CSSOM）
  • 7、Scripts (for unmuted exceptions)

常见的浏览器内核有哪些？

webSocket如何兼容低浏览器？(阿里)

居中对齐

为什么要初始化CSS样式。

Ajax 解决浏览器缓存问题？

  1. 在ajax发送请求前加上 anyAjaxObj.setRequestHeader(“If-Modified-Since”,”0”)。
  2. 在ajax发送请求前加上 anyAjaxObj.setRequestHeader(“Cache-Control”,”no-cache”)。
  3. 在URL后面加上一个随机数： “fresh=” + Math.random();。
  4. 在URL后面加上时间戳：”nowtime=” + new Date().getTime();。
  5. 如果是使用jQuery，直接这样就可以了 $.ajaxSetup({cache:false})。这样页面的所有ajax都会执行这条语句就是不需要保存缓存记录。

你有用过哪些前端性能优化的方法？

http状态码有那些？分别代表是什么意思？

item.title: IELTS writing part 1
item.description:
[TOC]

KEY

  • The task is to describe what you see. Write a
item.content:encoded:
[TOC]

KEY

  • The task is to describe what you see. Write a report. Don’t give opinions.
  • No conclusion. Instead write a summary(the overview).

Line graph

Bar chart

Pie chart

Table

numbers - describe - compare - changes/trends

Diagram-comparing

Diagram-process

4 paragraphs

  1. Introduction: One sentence, paraphrase the question

  2. Overview(or at end):2 sentences, the main, general things

  3. Details - comparison

  4. Details - trends

Line Chart

  • Compare the lines, but not to describe them separately
  • First, make a very general comparion(Summary)
  • Second, compare the lines at specific points
    • Write at least one thing about each line

Intro-paraphrase the question

  • the graph below shows/ the line graph compares electricity production/ the amount of electricity produced in France using four different sources of power between 1980 and 2012/ over a period of 32 years

Overview(highest/lowest)

It is clear that nuclear power was by far the most important means of electricity generation over the period shown. Renewables provided the lowest amount of electricity in each year

Detail - compare the lines

  • In 1980, thermal power stations were the main source of electricity in France, generating around 120 terawatt hourse of power.
  • Nuclear and xxx power station produced just under 75 xxx hours of electricity each, and renewables provided a negligible amount.
  • Just one year later, nuclear power overtook xxx power as the primary source of electricity
  1. Nuclear power was used to produce 75 terawatt hours of electricity
  2. Nuclear power station produced 75 xxx

Detail -2

  • Between 1980 and 2005, electricity production from nuclear rose dramatically to a peak of 430 terawatt hours.
  • By contrast, the figure for thermal power fell to only 50 terawatt hours in 1985, and remain at this level for the rest of the period.
  • xxx power generation remained relatively stable, at between 50 and 80 terawatt hours, for the whole 32-year period, but renewable electricity production saw only a small rise to approximately 25 terawatt hours by 2012

Bar chart

Introduction

  • global sales of/the number of mobile phones sold worldwide
  • the top five mobile phone brands/the five most popular manufacturers
  • between 2009 and 2013/ in the years 2009, 2011 and 2013

Overview

  • xxx saw the biggest rises in sales over the 5-year period

Detail -1

  • almost double the number of handsets sold by the second most successful manufacturer.
  • By 2013, Samsung had become the market leader/dominate the market/ be predominant with sales reaching 450 million units.

Detail -2

  • The other three top selling mobile phone/handsets brands
  • sold around xx, xxx, xx respectively
  • overtook the other two vendors while LG saw declining sales and the figures for ZTE rose only slightly.

Vocabulary

  • brands, manufacturers, companies, vendors
  • close to, almost, approximately, around

Pie chart

  • pie chart don’t always show changes over time
  • compare percentages(and numbers)
  • household spending pattern/five categories of household expenditure
  • Don’t describe each country or each year separately. Compare year and coutries together
  • Mention 5 categories
  • Divide the categories into 2 groups for the two ‘details’ paragraphs
  • The category called ‘other’ is not important
Overview
  • It is noticeable that the proportion of spending on food fell in both countries
  • over the 28-year period/spent a significantly larger percentage of their household budgets
Detail
  • talk about numbers
voc
  • spending, expenditure, spent, costs
  • proportion of spending
  • the equivalent figure for a UK home
  • fell, rose, saw an increase in
  • spent half as much in relative terms on recreation/leisure time
  • leisure accounted for the highest proportion

Table

  • the same information as a line graph, bar chart or pie chart
  • changes over time(increase decrease)/comparisons
  • select key numbers

Voc

  • the top five countries = the five highest ranking countries in terms of
  • in 2012 and 2013 = over a period of two years
  • It is clear that xxx in the years 2012 and 2013. However, xxxx over the same period
  • earned by far the most
  • revenue from tourism
  • 2013 saw a rise of between 1 and 4 million tourist visits to each country, with the exception of China, which received 2 million fewer visitors than in the previous year
  • saw a rise, increase, rising from, rose by

Keys

  • can’t mention every number. chose around 10 out of the total 20 numbers

2 different type chart

  • look for one main feature in each chart(summary)
  • describe specific numbers for each chart separately

voc

  • men and women/females and males
  • attend various evening courses/took four different evening classes(specific numbers)
  • the ages of these course participants/the age profile of these attendees
  • Younger adults were in the minority, with only 11% of students aged 20 to 29, and only 5% aged under 20.
  • the majority of/were in the minority
  • to be precise
  • significantly more, higher, highest
  • over 40 years of age/50 or more/between 40 and 49/aged 20 to 29/age under 20

Process diagram

difference

  • no numbers
  • can’t compare anything
  • can’t describe trend(increase, decrease)

talking about

  • how many steps
  • where the process begins and ends
  • each step in the process

‘step’ language

  • At the first stage in the process
  • The process begins with
  • Secondly, Finally
  • The second step involves
  • Next, then, after that
  • At the following stage

Some words

  • steps/stages in the process of
  • consists of four distinct stages
  • it requires five steps
  • raw materials, end products
  • production, manufacturing
  • turn used glass into new products
  • at the first stage, next, then, begin with
  • are mixed, is heated, can be shaped
  • is sorted according to
  • can be moulded to create
  • are mixed -> this mxiture
  • collection -> the collected glass
  • is melted -> the resulting molten glass

item.title: iOS逆向-微信自动添加好友(转载)
item.description:
工具

以下工具的详细使用方法可以查看iOS应用逆向工程 第二部分
item.content:encoded:
工具

以下工具的详细使用方法可以查看iOS应用逆向工程 第二部分 工具篇。

macbook 软件

  • theos

    制作 Tweak 的工具

  • hopper disassembler

    用于静态分析

  • usbmuxd

    端口转发，可以让我们通过 usb 连接手机进行 ssh、lldb 调试等。主要使用python-client目录下的文件

  • class-dump

    dump 目标对象的 class 信息的工具.

  • lldb

    调试神器，用过的都说好。默认自带，在/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/usr/bin/lldb 中。

越狱 iPhone 软件

以下软件在 Cydia 中即可下载(debugserver 除外)

  • OpenSSH

    实现在越狱手机上远程进行 ssh 服务，通过 ssh，即可以通过终端连接 iPhone 进行控制。

    iOS 工具大部分都需要在 ssh 环境中使用

  • Cycript

    脚本语言，用于 hook 正在运行的进程，并实时注入代码。

  • ondeviceconsole

    用于在 Terminal 中查看手机的 log

  • debugserver

    用于连接手机进行 lldb 调试的工具。用 Xcode 在手机上进行 app 调试即可在iPhone目录的 /Developer/usr/bin/ 中生成。

使用 debugserver 需要先进行处理。因为缺少task_for_pid权限，所以调试不了其他的 app。
先通过 ssh 拷贝 debugserver

减肥

下载 ldid 与 ent，进行

在使用 ssh 拷贝至手机,完成。

分析

思路：想要实现自动验证好友请求，则要拿到获取好友请求的方法，以及通过好友请求的方法。hook 获取好友请求的方法，在接收到好友请求的时候，执行添加好友的方法。
而这些主要逻辑在“新的朋友”界面。

定位好友请求的方法

UI 分析

我们知道，根据 MVC 模式，一般的方法实现都是在 ViewController 中的，所以想要拿到好友请求的方法，要先拿到当前界面的控制器。而这时候可以通过 UI 分析获得。

先打开新的朋友界面。

使用 usbmuxd 进行端口的转发(若手机不卡，可以跳过这步直接使用 ssh 进行 wifi 远程连接)

再使用 ssh 连接至手机

查看微信的进程信息

Cycript 注入

启动Cycript后，使用以下命令查看当前 UI 布局

因为知道当前的视图有 tableView，所以找到 tableView 的对象。从上图可以看到该对象的地址为0x18c4be00。
在使用 nextResponder()根据响应者往上找当前的 ViewController。


找到当前的控制器，为SayHelloViewController

Log 分析

使用class-dump dump 出微信的 class 信息。

再使用 theos 的 logify 工具，该工具用来注入NSLog来打印方法的入参和出参。(就是在 hook 某个类的所有的方法，并在里面加 log，并导出xm文件)

注意：一般该Tweak.xm仍然无法执行，需要进行修改：

  • 去掉 .cxx_destruct 方法
  • 将 HBLogDebug 改为 NSLog
  • 去掉所有的 delegate
  • 将所有的参数对象类型改成 id
  • 去掉所有的 weak

再使用 theos 配置相关文件(具体查看iOS逆向-微信helloWorld), 然后进行make package install 安装至手机。

重新启动微信进入新的朋友界面。

在ssh中使用ondeviceconsole打印手机的 log。

这时用另一个微信号添加自己好友。触发好友请求的方法。可以看到以下的 log

说明有好友添加请求的时候，会调用
-[SayHelloViewController OnSayHelloDataChange]

动态分析

既然已经知道了有好友请求的时候会调用OnSayHelloDataChange，那么我们可以在当前方法中进行处理，然而有个弊端，就是当有好友请求时，微信不在新的朋友界面时，是不会调用该方法的。所以我们应该在更底层的类中(假设为消息管理类)中进行处理，而怎么找到消息管理类呢？按照一般的逻辑，消息管理类中一定有方法触发了OnSayHelloDataChange,这时候就要用到 lldb + hopper 神器来找到相应的消息管理类与其处理方法了。

lldb 进行手机端调试，hopper 进行静态分析，分析OnSayHelloDataChange方法的信息，找出突破口。

先用 hopper 打开微信的二进制文件。搜索SayHelloViewController OnSayHelloDataChange方法。
可以看到当前方法在微信中的偏移地址0x14a4824。

启动debugserver 配合lldb调试

先打开微信，并使用 usbmuxd 转换端口

再 ssh 到手机上，开启 debugserver 。

使用新的 Terminal 窗口，打开 lldb，连接1234端口，并查看当前微信的进程信息(一般会在所有进程的首行)。
此时会卡住一段时间。

找到微信在当前手机上的进程内存基地址为0x000b2000(这个值不是定值)

通过以上可以找到 [SayHelloViewController OnSayHelloDataChange]方法在手机上的内存地址。即

使用br打断点查看

接着输入c继续运行，重新使用另一微信账号添加好友，会触发该断点。

使用bt查看调用栈信息，即哪些方法调用了当前的方法，找到方法的上游。(异步调用的话没办法查看)

第一个表示当前的方法，可以看到在调用此方法前，该进程总共调用了3个方法。
分别计算出这三个方法在微信中的偏移量。

将这三个地址在 hopper 中查看(按快捷键g,输入地址)，找到了对应的方法为

从以上方法名可以猜测

是用来接收添加好友消息的函数处理，其中MsgList:后面的参数可能为消息的数组，为了证明我们可以在该方法中打个断点查看下。
使用命令register read读取寄存器地址，并使用po打印该对象。

看出r3寄存器确实是个数组，同时也得到了消息的对象为CMessageWrap 证明我们是对的。

ps: 解释下为什么要看r3，因为在 armv7 中，一个方法的调用，一般寄存器都是这么存储的：前四个参数放在r0~r3，剩下的存放在堆栈中。查看堆栈的话使用x/10 $sp 查看前10个堆栈里的对象地址。

然而FriendAsistSessionMgr这个类可能在新的好友界面进行一些初始化，且放在SayHelloViewController中，而我们想要的是不管在哪个控制器里都可以 hook 住上面的消息数组对象。因此我们往上找，[CMessageMgr MainThreadNotifyToExt:]，然而里面并没有我们需要的信息。而根据类名我们推测CMessageMgr是用来管理消息的。有可能是在异步执行了消息数组的获取。

因此，重复以上步骤，使用 logify 对CMessageMgr进行 Log 分析。最终锁定了
CMessageMgr MessageReturn:MessageInfo:Event:

定位通过好友请求的方法

动态分析

既然找到了接收好友请求的方法，那么是时候找通过好友请求的方法了。
我们知道，通过好友请求的方法，是在新的朋友界面，点击接受的时候触发的。(可以通过 Log 分析，然而这里还有另一个比较快速的方法)

Cycript 定位

先通过 Cycript 打印出所有的 UI 层级。
找到接受按钮的对象，(有个技巧，我们知道当前按钮是在某个 cell 下面的，所以定位这个)。


再通过cycript将该对象的 hidden 动态修改为 1，看是否隐藏。

发现按钮不见了，证明我们是对的。这时候需要找到点击按钮的事件。

而我们知道 UIButton 是继承 UIControl 的，在 Cycript 中， 可以通过allTargets 与 allControlEvents查看当前UIControl所有的targets与events，再使用actionsForTarget:forControlEvent:从而找到触发的方法。

看出所触发的方法为[ContactsItemView onRightBtnAction]

静态分析

既然拿到了方法名，那我们怎么看他具体的实现呢？
接下来就是大名鼎鼎的 hopper 登场了。
用 hopper 打开微信的二进制文件,并进行汇编与伪代码的转换。
由于汇编读起来比较晦涩，所以还是进行伪代码的转换，这样效率比较快。点击该按钮进行转换

可以得到伪代码

上图我们看到了

可以得出，r11 = [r5 onContactsItemViewRightButtonClick:btn],而 r5 我们判断为 self 的代理，这个我们也可以通过在之前用 class-dump 的头文件里面搜索onContactsItemViewRightButtonClick,会发现在ContactsItemViewDelegate中。
也就是[ContactsItemView onRightBtnAction]内部调用了[self.delegate onContactsItemViewRightButtonClick:].
而 ContactsItemView 的delegate为 SayHelloViewController。

再用 hopper 定位onContactsItemViewRightButtonClick。


看到这里估计会很懵逼不知道从何下手。这时候只要加以推测就可以了。
上图中进行了两个if判断，第一个为

可以得出其实是执行了 if([r4 isKindOfClass：[CPushContact class]]);
而r4是什么呢？可以肯定是CPushContact对象，不然下面的代码都不执行了。我们可以根据动态分析，通过 lldb 打断点，并查看r3寄存器的对象类型，可以看到该对象为CPushContact对象。因此r4就是CPushContact对象，根据字面意思可以得到就是联系人对象。

继续看下面的代码，可以看到也进行了一次判断if (((loc_1c099bc(r6, @selector(m_bSuspiciousUser)) & 0xff) != 0x0) && ((loc_1c099bc(r6, @selector(isMMContact)) & 0xff) == 0x0))，看到了MMUIAlertView。推测是弹窗的 view ，推测如果是可疑的用户或者当前申请的好友已经是自己的好友，那就进行弹窗。而另一部分为verifyContactWithOpCode:opcode:，推测该部分为添加好友的方法。
可以通过 Log 分析或者通过 lldb 打断点，会看到都会进入该方法。且参数分别为CPushContact对象与 3。
接着继续分析verifyContactWithOpCode:opcode:方法。主要的部分如下所示。

通过分析，我们可以得到，确认好友申请，显示构造了CContactVerifyLogic对象。再构造了一个CVerifyContactWrap对象，并设置了相关属性，比如m_nsUsrName m_uiScene m_nsTicket.然后通过添加到数组中，通过CContactVerifyLogic对象的startWithVerifyContactWrap:opCode:parentView:fromChatRoom:方法发送。
代码如下：

这样我们就得到了 获取好友请求的方法与添加好友的方法。
而这里还有一个问题，就是添加好友的对象是CPushContact，而获得好友请求的对象的CMessageWrap。这里需要进行转换，而转换的方法也在SayHelloViewController中，可以重复上面的分析方法获得。

编写Tweak

通过以上的分析，将代码合并起来

##

item.title: Way to say attractive in native speaking
item.description:
「某人被某物吸引」這樣在中文裡被動的句子，除了要強調被吸引的是誰／什麼，多數時候英文會換為主動形式：「某物吸引了某人」讓句子變得簡潔。

●昨天的新聞頭條太聳動，全店的人都停下來抬頭看電視

Th
item.content:encoded:
「某人被某物吸引」這樣在中文裡被動的句子，除了要強調被吸引的是誰／什麼，多數時候英文會換為主動形式：「某物吸引了某人」讓句子變得簡潔。

●昨天的新聞頭條太聳動，全店的人都停下來抬頭看電視

The TV news headline was so horrifying that it caught every one’s attention.

● 上週末全台北的人大概都被華山新的展覽吸引去看了吧！

The new exhibition at Huashan Creative Park attracted an estimated 3 milllion people last weekend.

● 新來的經濟學老師又帥又會講課，很能吸引學生的注意力

Charismatic and experienced, the new Economic teacher can always grab students’ attention.

● 我在書店閒逛的時候被這本書的封面吸引，好像很有趣我就買了

I was appealed to the book cover when I browsed at a bookstore, so I just bought the book.

除了最直翻的 attract，appeal 也很常用，draw/catch/grab sb.’s attention可以用在各種場合。

除了動詞，你也可以用形容詞的句式，就像我們常說的令人印象深刻，除了 impressive 還可以怎麼說呢？

● 她的聲音很有特色，讓人一聽就能記住她

She has a distinguished voice.

● 他剛剛的演講實在太令人印象深刻了，演講完畢之後觀眾起立鼓掌三十秒之久

The speech he just delivered is impressive, which earned him a 30-second standing ovation.

● 天啊，牛排看起來太吸引人了，我要點這個！

Oh my God! The steak looks so appealing. I am going for it.

這邊幫你準備了懶人包，外帶到你的生活裡去吧！

●你的外帶餐點在這●

○簡潔俐落的形容詞

\1. impressive 令人印象深刻的

\2. appealing 吸引人的

\3. distinguished 很有特色的

\4. eye-catching 引人注目的

○用動詞來描述更多細節

\1. attract 吸引

\2. appeal to 吸引（to 接被吸引的人事物）

\3. draw/catch/grab sb.’s attention 吸引某（些）人的注意力

item.title: Why you can have millions of Goroutines but only thousands of Java Threads
item.description:
Problems

Many seasoned
item.content:encoded:
Problems

Many seasoned engineers working in JVM based languages have seen errors like this:

OutOfMemory…err…out of threads. On my laptop running Linux, this happens after a paltry 11500 threads.

If you try the same thing in Go by starting Goroutines that sleep indefinitely, you get a very different result. On my laptop, I got up to 70 million goroutines before I got bored. So why can you have so many more Goroutines than threads? The answer is a fun journey down the operating system and back up again. And this isn’t just an academic issue – it has real world implications for how you design software. I’ve run into JVM thread limits in production literally dozens of times, either because some bad code was leaking threads, or because an engineer simply wasn’t aware of the JVM’s thread limitations.

What’s a thread anyway?

The term “thread” can mean a lot of different things. In this post, I’m going to use it to refer to a logical thread. That is: a series of operations with are run in a linear order; a logical path of execution. CPUs can only execute about one logical thread per core truly concurrently.1An inherent side effect: If you have more threads than cores, threads must be paused to allow other threads to do work, then later resumed when it’s their turn again. To support being paused and resumed, a thread minimally needs two things:

  1. An instruction pointer of some kind. AKA: What line of code was I executing when I was paused?
  2. A stack. AKA: What is my current state? The stack contains local variables as well as pointers to heap allocated variables. All threads within the same process share the same heap.2

Given these two things, the system scheduling threads onto the CPU has enough information to pause a thread, allow other threads to run, then later resume the original thread where it left off. This operation is usually completely transparent to the threads. From the perspective of a thread, it is running continuously. The only way a thread could observe being descheduled is by measuring the time between subsequent operations.3

Getting back to our original question: why can you have so many more Goroutines?

The JVM uses operating system threads

Although, it’s not required by the spec, all modern, general purpose JVMs that I’m aware of delegate threading to operating system threads on all platforms where this is possible. Going forward, I’ll use the phrase “user space threads” to refer to threads that are scheduled by the language instead of by the kernel/OS. Threads implemented by the operating system have two properties that drastically limit how many of them can exist; no solution that maps language threads 1:1 with operating system threads can support massive concurrency.

In the JVM: Fixed Stack Size

Using operating system threads incurs a constant, large, memory cost per thread

The second major problem with operating system threads comes because each OS thread has its own fixed-size stack. Though the size is configurable, in a 64-bit environment, the JVM defaults to a 1MB stack per thread. You can make the default stack size smaller, but you tradeoff memory usage with increased risk of stack overflow. The more recursion in your code, the more likely you are to hit stack overflow. If you keep the default value, 1k threads will use almost 1GB of RAM! RAM is cheap these days, but almost no one has the terabyte of ram you’d need to run a million threads with this machinery.

How Go does it differently: Dynamically Sized Stacks

Golang prevents large (mostly unused) stacks running the system out of memory with a clever trick: Go’s stacks are dynamically sized, growing and shrinking with the amount of data stored. This isn’t a trivial thing to do, and the design has gone through a couple ofiterations.4 While I’m not going to get into the internal details here (they’re more than enough for their own posts and others have written about it at length), the upshot is that a new goroutine will have a stack of only about 4KB. With 4KB per stack, you can put 2.5 million goroutines in a gigabyte of RAM – a huge improvement over Java’s 1MB per thread.

In the JVM: Context Switching Delay

Using operating system threads caps you in the double digit thousands, simply from context switching delay.

Because the JVM uses operating system threads, it relies on the operating system kernel to schedule them. The operating system has a list of all the running processes and threads, and attempts to give them each a “fair” share of time running on the CPU.5 When the kernel switches from one thread to another, it has a significant amount of work do. The new thread or process running must be started with a view of world that abstracts away the fact that other threads are running on the same CPU. I won’t the get into the nitty gritty here, but you can read more if you’re curious. The critical takeaway is that switching contexts will take on the order of 1-100µ seconds. This may not seem like much, but at a fairly realistic 10µ seconds per switch, if you want to schedule each thread at least once per second, you’ll only be able to run about 100k threads on 1 core. And this doesn’t actually give the threads time to do any useful work.

How Go does it differently: Run multiple Goroutines on a single OS thread

Golang implements its own scheduler that allows many Goroutines to run on the same OS thread. Even if Go ran the same context switching code as the kernel, it would save a significant amount of time by avoiding the need to switch into ring-0 to run the kernel and back again. But that’s just table stakes. To actually support 1 million goroutines, Go needs to do something much more sophisticated.

Even if JVM brought threads to user space, it still wouldn’t be able to support millions of threads. Suppose for a minute that in your new system, switching between new threads takes only 100 nanoseconds. Even if all you did was context switch, you could only run about a million threads if you wanted to schedule each thread ten times per second. More importantly, you’d be maxing out your CPU to do so. Supporting truly massive concurrency requires another optimization: Only schedule a thread when you know it can do useful work! If you’re running that many threads, only a handful can be be doing useful work anyway. Go facilitates this by integrating channels and the scheduler. If a goroutine is waiting on a empty channel, the scheduler can see that and it won’t run the Goroutine. Go goes one step further and actually sticks the mostly-idle goroutines on their own operating system thread. This way the (hopefully much smaller) number of active goroutines can be scheduled by one thread while the millions of mostly-sleeping goroutines can be tended to separately. This helps keep latency down.

Unless Java added language features that the scheduler could observe, supporting intelligent scheduling would be impossible. However, you can build runtime schedulers in “user space” that are aware of when a thread can do work. This forms the basis for frameworks like Akka that can support millions of actors6

Closing Thoughts

Transitioning from a model using operating system threads to a model using lightweight, user space threads has happened over and over again and will probably continue to happen.7 For use cases where a high degree of concurrency is required, it’s simply the only option. However, it doesn’t come without considerable complexity. If Go opted for OS threads instead of their own scheduler and growable-stack scheme, they would shave thousands of lines off the runtime. For many use cases, it’s simply a better model. The complexity can be abstracted away by language and library writers, and software engineers can write massively concurrent programs.

This post’s link is here. See Chinese version here.
  1. Hyperthreading doubles the effective cores. Instruction pipelining will also increase the effective parallelism the CPU allows. At the end of the day, however, it will be O(numCores). [return]
  2. There’s probably some esoteric case where this isn’t true. I’m sure someone will tell me about it. [return]
  3. This is actually an attack vector. Javascript can detect minute differences in timing caused by keyboard interrupts. This can be used by a malicious website to listen, not to your keystrokes, but to their timing. https://mlq.me/download/keystroke_js.pdf [return]
  4. Golang first started with a segmented-stacks model where the stack would actually expand into a separate area of memory, using some clever bookkeeping to keep track. A later implementation improved performance in specific cases by instead using a contiguous stack where instead of splitting the stack, much like resizing a hashtable, a new large stack is allocated and through some very tricky pointer manipulation, all the contents are carefully copied into the new, larger, stack. [return]
  5. Threads can mark their priority by calling nice (see man nice) for more info to control how frequently they’re scheduled. [return]
  6. Actors serve the same purpose as Goroutines for Scala/Java by enabling massive concurrency. Just like Goroutines, the actor scheduler can see which actors have messages in their mailbox and only run actors that are ready to do useful work. You can actually have even more actors than you can have goroutines because actors don’t need a stack. However, this means that if an actor does not quickly process a message, the scheduler will be blocked (since it can’t pause in the middle of the message since the Actor doesn’t have it’s own stack). A blocked scheduler means no messages are processed and things will quickly grind to a halt. Trade offs! [return]
  7. In Apache, each request is handled by 1 OS thread, effectively capping Apache to thousands of concurrent connections. Nginx opts for a model where 1 OS thread tends to hundreds or even thousands of concurrent connections, allowing a much higher degree of concurrency. Erlang uses a similar model that allows millions of actors to execute concurrently. Gevent brings greenlets (user space threads) to Python, enabling a much higher degree of concurrency than would be supported otherwise (Python threads are OS threads). [return]

item.title: Notes about IELTS writing task 2
item.description:
Minimum 250 words for 40 minutes

criteria：1 Task response 2 Coherence and cohesion 3 vocabulary 4 grammer（less
item.content:encoded:
Minimum 250 words for 40 minutes

criteria：1 Task response 2 Coherence and cohesion 3 vocabulary 4 grammer（less important）

Timing（40 minutes）

Planning - 10 minutes

Introduction - 2sentences - 5 minutes - to be short

Four types of questions:

1.Discussion (Discuss both views and give your own opinion)

Topic - study in groups or alone

Answer - sometimes better alone, usually better in a group

People have different views about the effectiveness of group study as opposed to working alone. While there are some benefits to studying independently, I believe that group work is usually more productive.

2.Opinion (To what extent do you agree or disagree)

Topic - community services for all teenagers

Answer - 3 choices: agree, disagree, or balanced opinion(when possible)

It is sometimes argued that high school students should be made to do some work in their local communities. (agree) I completely argee that this kinds of scheme would be a good idea. (disagree) In my opinion, it would be wrong to force teenagers to do any kind of unsalaried work. (balanced) While I disagree with the idea of making such programmes compulsory , I do believe that voluntary community service could benefit young people.

3.Problem and solution (what measures can be taken to tackle this problem)

Topic - criminals reoffend
